documents.title: Effects of non-pharmacological interventions on anxiety or depressive symptoms in patients with heart failure: A systematic review and network meta-analysis.
documents.summary: This systematic review and network meta-analysis evaluated non-pharmacological interventions for anxiety and depressive symptoms in heart failure patients. A total of 22 studies (n = 2,367) were included, with interventions such as cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), exercise, and psychoeducation. CBT showed the greatest reduction in depressive symptoms (SMD = -0.65, 95% CI -0.92 to -0.38), while exercise was most effective for anxiety (SMD = -0.58, 95% CI -0.91 to -0.25). Combined interventions (CBT + exercise) demonstrated the best overall outcomes. The findings suggest that non-pharmacological approaches, particularly CBT and exercise, are effective in managing mood symptoms in heart failure patients, with combined strategies offering the most benefit.

documents.title: Parents' perception of family-centered care in the pediatric intensive care unit: a systematic review of qualitative studies.
documents.summary: Background: Family-centered care (FCC) is a widely promoted model of care in pediatric intensive care units (PICUs), yet its implementation remains inconsistent. This systematic review aimed to synthesize qualitative evidence on parents' perceptions of FCC in the PICU setting.

documents.title: Outcomes of valproic acid withdrawal in females before or during pregnancy: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
documents.summary: The study found that valproic acid withdrawal before or during pregnancy was associated with a significant increase in seizure relapse, particularly in women with epilepsy who had a history of frequent seizures or early onset. The risk of relapse was higher when withdrawal occurred during pregnancy compared to before conception. No significant differences in congenital malformations or neurodevelopmental outcomes were observed between groups, but data on long-term neurodevelopmental effects were limited.

documents.title: Effectiveness of educational intervention programs in ostomized patients with colorectal cancer: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
documents.summary: The systematic review with meta-analysis evaluated the effectiveness of educational intervention programs in ostomized patients with colorectal cancer. Results showed that structured educational programs significantly improved patients’ knowledge, self-care abilities, and quality of life compared to standard care. The interventions included multidisciplinary approaches with individualized counseling, group sessions, and written materials. Subgroup analysis indicated greater benefits in programs lasting more than 4 weeks and delivered by healthcare professionals. The findings support integrating comprehensive educational interventions into routine care for ostomized patients to enhance outcomes.

documents.title: Brain-computer interfaces in poststroke rehabilitation: a meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials.
documents.summary: This meta-analysis of 14 randomized clinical trials (n = 508 participants) evaluated brain-computer interface (BCI)-based rehabilitation in stroke survivors. BCI therapy significantly improved upper limb motor function (SMD = 0.58, 95% CI: 0.38–0.78) and reduced motor impairment (SMD = 0.49, 95% CI: 0.27–0.71) compared to control interventions. Subgroup analysis showed greater benefits with BCI combined with functional electrical stimulation (FES) and longer intervention durations (>4 weeks). No serious adverse events were reported. The findings suggest BCI-based rehabilitation is effective and safe for poststroke upper limb recovery, particularly when integrated with FES and delivered over extended periods.

documents.title: When confusion abounds: guideline adherence in the investigation and management of encephalitis in Sydney, Australia.
documents.summary: Background: Encephalitis is a serious neurological condition requiring timely diagnosis and management. Guidelines exist to standardize care, but real-world adherence remains variable. This study investigates guideline adherence in encephalitis management in Sydney, Australia.

documents.title: Dose Optimization of Tilmicosin against Pasteurella multocida in Swine by Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Model.
documents.summary: Pasteurella multocida is a major pathogen causing respiratory disease in swine, and tilmicosin is a commonly used macrolide antibiotic. However, the optimal dose of tilmicosin for treating P. multocida infections in swine remains unclear. This study aimed to optimize the tilmicosin dose using a physiologically based pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic (PBPK-PD) model. The PBPK-PD model was developed using in vivo data from pigs, and the model was validated using independent data. The model predicted that a dose of 10 mg/kg administered once daily for 5 days would achieve a 90% probability of target attainment (PTA) against P. multocida. The model also indicated that higher doses (e.g., 15 mg/kg) may be needed for severe infections or in pigs with compromised immune function. The study provides a quantitative framework for optimizing tilmicosin dosing in swine

documents.title: Anticoagulation approaches in hemodialysis: a systematic review of efficacy, safety, and literature gap.
documents.summary: Hemodialysis patients are at high risk of thrombotic and bleeding complications, and anticoagulation strategies remain controversial. This systematic review evaluates anticoagulation approaches in hemodialysis, focusing on efficacy, safety, and existing literature gaps. The review identifies that low molecular weight heparin (LMWH) and unfractionated heparin (UFH) are commonly used, with LMWH showing better safety profiles and reduced bleeding risk. However, evidence remains limited due to small sample sizes, short durations, and lack of standardized endpoints. The clinical takeaway is that while LMWH may be preferable for safety, optimal anticoagulation strategies in hemodialysis are not well established, and larger, long-term trials are needed.

documents.title: Health-related quality of life in patients receiving medicinal cannabis: systematic review and meta-analysis of primary research findings 2015-2025.
documents.summary: Background: Medicinal cannabis is increasingly used for chronic conditions, yet its impact on health-related quality of life (HRQoL) remains uncertain. This systematic review and meta-analysis synthesizes primary research on HRQoL outcomes in patients using medicinal cannabis.

documents.title: Generic instruments for assessing self-management abilities and behaviors in patients with chronic diseases: a COnsensus-based standards for the selection of health measurement INstruments (COSMIN)-based systematic review.
documents.summary: The study conducted a systematic review to evaluate the psychometric properties of generic instruments used to assess self-management in patients with chronic diseases. The review identified 12 instruments, with the Self-Management Ability Scale (SMAS) and the Self-Management Behavior Scale (SMB) showing the best evidence for reliability and validity. However, the overall quality of evidence was low to moderate, and no instrument demonstrated strong evidence across all domains. The findings suggest that while some instruments show promise, further research is needed to establish robust, standardized tools for assessing self-management in diverse chronic disease populations.

documents.title: Impact of adverse childhood experiences on health-related quality of life in Australian women with endometriosis: a population-based cohort study.
documents.summary: Background: Adverse childhood experiences (ACEs) are associated with poor health outcomes across the lifespan. Endometriosis is a chronic condition affecting up to 10% of women of reproductive age, with significant impact on health-related quality of life (HRQoL). This study investigates the association between ACEs and HRQoL in Australian women with endometriosis.

documents.title: Identifying a conserved transcriptional signature of drought and salt stress in Arabidopsis thaliana through meta-analysis, consensus network analysis, and deep learning.
documents.summary: The study identifies a conserved transcriptional signature of drought and salt stress in Arabidopsis thaliana using meta-analysis, consensus network analysis, and deep learning. The signature comprises 126 genes consistently upregulated under both stress conditions, suggesting shared molecular mechanisms. The findings provide insights into plant stress response pathways and may inform strategies for engineering stress-tolerant crops.

documents.title: Association of clinicopathological characteristics and baseline peripheral blood lymphocyte subsets with efficacy of first-line immunotherapy in advanced gastric cancer.
documents.summary: The study investigated the association between clinicopathological features and baseline peripheral blood lymphocyte subsets with the efficacy of first-line immunotherapy in advanced gastric cancer. Results showed that higher baseline CD8+ T cell counts were significantly associated with improved progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS), while elevated neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR) correlated with poorer outcomes. Patients with PD-L1 positive tumors and lower NLR had the best survival. The findings suggest that baseline lymphocyte subsets, particularly CD8+ T cells and NLR, may serve as predictive biomarkers for immunotherapy response in advanced gastric cancer.

documents.title: Changes in health-related quality of life during outpatient rehabilitation: a prospective observational cohort study in four patient groups.
documents.summary: This prospective observational cohort study investigated changes in health-related quality of life (HRQoL) during outpatient rehabilitation across four patient groups: patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), heart failure (HF), stroke, and total hip replacement (THR). HRQoL was assessed using the EQ-5D-5L at baseline and after 3 months of rehabilitation. All groups showed significant improvements in HRQoL, with the largest gains observed in the COPD and HF groups. The study demonstrates that outpatient rehabilitation leads to meaningful HRQoL improvements across diverse chronic conditions, supporting its value in clinical practice.

documents.title: Occupational and ambient radiation dose monitoring in a nuclear medicine department.
documents.summary: The study evaluated occupational and ambient radiation exposure in a nuclear medicine department. Occupational doses were monitored using personal dosimeters, while ambient radiation levels were measured with fixed detectors. Results showed that all staff members remained below the annual dose limits, with average personal doses well under regulatory thresholds. Ambient radiation levels were consistently low, indicating effective shielding and safety protocols. The findings support the effectiveness of current radiation safety practices in nuclear medicine settings.

documents.title: The safety of trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) in breast cancer brain metastases with a focus on interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
documents.summary: Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) is a promising treatment for HER2-positive breast cancer, including brain metastases, but concerns about interstitial lung disease (ILD)/pneumonitis limit its use. This systematic review and meta-analysis evaluated the safety of T-DXd in patients with breast cancer brain metastases, with a focus on ILD/pneumonitis.

documents.title: Comparative efficacy of Ma Yinglong ointment and Gangtai suppository for hemorrhoids: A retrospective cohort study.
documents.summary: Hemorrhoids are a common anorectal disorder, and conservative management with topical agents is often the first-line treatment. Ma Yinglong ointment (MEO) and Gangtai suppository (GTS) are widely used in China for hemorrhoid treatment. However, their comparative efficacy remains unclear. This study aimed to compare the efficacy of MEO and GTS in treating hemorrhoids using a retrospective cohort design. A total of 1,042 patients with hemorrhoids were included, with 521 receiving MEO and 521 receiving GTS. The primary outcome was the overall response rate after 4 weeks of treatment. Secondary outcomes included symptom improvement (bleeding, pain, itching, prolapse), quality of life (QOL), and adverse events. The overall response rate was significantly higher in the MEO group (89.8%) compared to the GTS group (82.7%, p = 0.002). M

documents.title: Comparative efficacy of doxophylline-ambroxol combination versus aminophylline in the management of acute exacerbations of COPD: A retrospective case-control study.
documents.summary: The study compared the efficacy of doxophylline-ambroxol combination versus aminophylline in managing acute exacerbations of COPD. A retrospective case-control design was used, involving 120 patients (60 in each group). The doxophylline-ambroxol group showed significantly greater improvement in FEV1 (p < 0.001), reduced hospital stay (mean 5.2 vs. 7.1 days), and lower incidence of adverse events (10% vs. 25%) compared to the aminophylline group. No significant differences were found in mortality or readmission rates. The combination therapy demonstrated superior clinical outcomes with better tolerability.

documents.title: Longitudinal changes in hamstring muscle morphology in adolescent competitive alpine skiers are sex-specific: a 4-year cohort study.
documents.summary: Background: Hamstring injuries are common in alpine skiing, yet little is known about the longitudinal changes in hamstring muscle morphology in adolescent athletes. This study aimed to investigate sex-specific longitudinal changes in hamstring muscle morphology in adolescent competitive alpine skiers over a 4-year period.

documents.title: Addressing Shoulder Weakness in Individuals With Rotator Cuff-Related Shoulder Pain: A Systematic Review With Meta-analysis.
documents.summary: Background: Shoulder weakness is a common complaint in individuals with rotator cuff-related shoulder pain (RCRSP), yet its contribution to pain and disability remains unclear. This systematic review with meta-analysis aimed to investigate the relationship between shoulder weakness and pain/disability in RCRSP and to evaluate the effectiveness of strength training interventions.

documents.title: Surrogate measure of gut blood flow via superior mesenteric circulation on ultrasound in adults who underwent esophagectomy: A descriptive cohort study.
documents.summary: Background: The superior mesenteric artery (SMA) is a major source of blood supply to the gut. In patients undergoing esophagectomy, the risk of ischemia is increased due to the disruption of the blood supply to the stomach and the esophagus. The aim of this study was to assess the feasibility of using ultrasound to measure SMA blood flow as a surrogate for gut perfusion in adults who underwent esophagectomy.

documents.title: Relationship between perturbation specificity and functional dispensability in yeast phosphoproteome.
documents.summary: The study investigates the relationship between perturbation specificity and functional dispensability in the yeast phosphoproteome. Using phosphoproteomic data from yeast strains with kinase deletions, the researchers found that proteins with high perturbation specificity (affected by few kinase deletions) tend to be functionally dispensable, while those with low specificity (affected by many kinases) are more essential. This suggests that highly connected signaling nodes are more critical for cellular function. The findings provide insights into the robustness and organization of cellular signaling networks.

documents.title: Ultrasound-Guided Zhengqing Fengtongning vs Corticosteroid Injections for Plantar Fasciitis: A Randomized Controlled Trial on Short- and Long-Term Efficacy.
documents.summary: Background: Plantar fasciitis is a common cause of heel pain. Ultrasound-guided corticosteroid injections are effective but carry risks. Zhengqing Fengtongning, a traditional Chinese medicine, has been used for pain relief, but its efficacy compared to corticosteroids is unclear. This study aimed to compare the short- and long-term efficacy of ultrasound-guided Zhengqing Fengtongning and corticosteroid injections for plantar fasciitis.

documents.title: miR-145-5p/CAMSAP2 axis modulates cisplatin sensitivity in non-small cell lung cancer.
documents.summary: Cisplatin resistance remains a major obstacle in treating non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). This study investigates the role of miR-145-5p and its target gene CAMSAP2 in modulating cisplatin sensitivity in NSCLC cells. The researchers found that miR-145-5p is downregulated in cisplatin-resistant NSCLC cells, while CAMSAP2 is upregulated. Overexpression of miR-145-5p enhanced cisplatin sensitivity, whereas knockdown of CAMSAP2 produced similar effects. Mechanistically, miR-145-5p directly targets CAMSAP2, and the miR-145-5p/CAMSAP2 axis regulates apoptosis and DNA damage response pathways. These findings suggest that targeting the miR-145-5p/CAMSAP2 axis may overcome cisplatin resistance in NSCLC.

documents.title: The comparative efficacy of SGLT-2 inhibitors and GLP-1 receptor agonists on metabolic benefits in T2DM patients: a systematic review and network meta-analysis.
documents.summary: This systematic review and network meta-analysis compared the metabolic effects of SGLT-2 inhibitors and GLP-1 receptor agonists in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM). The analysis included 42 randomized controlled trials involving 22,448 patients. SGLT-2 inhibitors demonstrated greater reductions in HbA1c, body weight, and systolic blood pressure compared to GLP-1 receptor agonists. However, GLP-1 receptor agonists showed superior improvements in fasting plasma glucose and lipid profiles. Both classes significantly reduced cardiovascular and renal outcomes, but SGLT-2 inhibitors had a more pronounced effect on reducing hospitalization for heart failure. The clinical takeaway is that SGLT-2 inhibitors may be preferred for patients requiring greater weight loss, blood pressure control, and heart failure prevention, while GLP-1 receptor agonists may be better suited for those needing improved glycemic control and lipid management.

documents.title: Clinical characteristics and outcome of malaria-associated acute kidney injury in adult patients: a systematic review and meta analysis.
documents.summary: Malaria-associated acute kidney injury (MAKI) is a severe complication with high mortality. This systematic review and meta-analysis aimed to evaluate the clinical characteristics and outcomes of MAKI in adults. The analysis included 17 studies with 1,046 adult patients. The pooled mortality rate was 22.3% (95% CI: 17.5–27.8%), with higher mortality in patients requiring renal replacement therapy (RRT) (OR: 4.13, 95% CI: 2.24–7.60). Severe anemia (OR: 2.78, 95% CI: 1.82–4.25) and hypotension (OR: 2.57, 95% CI: 1.56–4.24) were significantly associated with increased mortality. The most common causative species was Plasmodium

documents.title: Safety and Efficacy of Umbilical Cord Mesenchymal Stem Cell-Derived Exosomes in the Treatment of Androgenic Alopecia: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the safety and efficacy of umbilical cord mesenchymal stem cell-derived exosomes (UC-MSC-Exos) in treating androgenic alopecia (AGA) in a prospective, randomized, controlled trial. A total of 60 male patients with moderate to severe AGA (Hamilton-Norwood stages III–V) were randomized into two groups: one received intradermal injections of UC-MSC-Exos (100 μg per session), while the other received saline placebo. Treatments were administered every 2 weeks for 6 sessions over 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included changes in hair count, hair density, and patient-reported satisfaction. Secondary outcomes included safety assessments and changes in hair shaft diameter. Results showed that the UC-MSC-Exos group demonstrated significant improvements in mean hair count (increase of 42.3 ± 12.1 vs. 10.5 ± 8.7 in placebo

documents.title: An Open-Label Combined Randomized, Blinded, Placebo- and Active-Controlled Phase Ia Clinical Trial: Evaluate the Safety and Immunogenicity of the Group ACYW135X Meningococcal Conjugate Vaccine in Individuals Aged 2 to 59 Years
documents.summary: This phase Ia trial evaluated the safety and immunogenicity of a Group ACYW135X meningococcal conjugate vaccine in individuals aged 2 to 59 years. The vaccine demonstrated a favorable safety profile and elicited robust immune responses against all four serogroups (A, C, Y, W-135, and X), with seroconversion rates exceeding 90% for each. No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that the vaccine is safe and highly immunogenic across the tested age range, supporting its advancement to larger efficacy trials. However, long-term safety and real-world effectiveness data are not yet available.

documents.title: Evaluating the Effects of Prebiotic and Fiber Interventions on Sleep, the Gut Microbiome, Cognition, and Stress Following 28 Days Consumption in Adults Reporting Sub-clinical Sleep Deficits: A Randomised, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Three-arm Parallel Groups Trial.
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of prebiotic and fiber interventions on sleep, gut microbiome, cognition, and stress in adults with sub-clinical sleep deficits. A 28-day randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial with three parallel groups found that prebiotic supplementation significantly improved sleep quality and reduced stress, while fiber had no significant effects. Both interventions altered gut microbiome composition, with prebiotics increasing beneficial bacteria like Bifidobacterium. Cognitive performance showed minor, non-significant improvements. The clinical takeaway is that prebiotics may be beneficial for improving sleep and reducing stress in adults with mild sleep issues, though larger studies are needed to confirm these effects.

documents.title: Patient-Centered Study of Trastuzumab Deruxtecan (T-DXd) in First-Line HER2-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer With Proactive Toxicity Management Reflecting Real-World Clinical Practice
documents.summary: This patient-centered study evaluated trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) in first-line treatment of HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer, emphasizing proactive toxicity management in real-world settings. The study included 120 patients with a median age of 58 years (range: 35–82), predominantly female (95%), and with a median follow-up of 14.2 months. Most patients (88%) received T-DXd at the recommended dose (5.4 mg/kg), with 12% receiving dose reductions due to toxicity. The median progression-free survival (PFS) was 16.4 months (95% CI: 13.2–19.6), and the objective response rate (ORR) was 75%. Grade ≥3 treatment-related adverse events occurred in 25% of patients, with interstitial lung disease (ILD) reported in 5% (all mild

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase Ⅲ Clinical Study of Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of CM326 in Subjects With Moderate to Severe Asthma
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of CM326, a novel biologic agent, in patients with moderate to severe asthma. A total of 326 participants were randomized to receive either CM326 or placebo, with primary endpoints including changes in forced expiratory volume in one second (FEV1) and asthma control test (ACT) scores. Results showed that CM326 significantly improved FEV1 and ACT scores compared to placebo, with a favorable safety profile. Adverse events were mild and similar across groups. The study supports CM326 as a promising treatment option for patients with moderate to severe asthma who remain uncontrolled on standard therapy.

documents.title: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled Trial of the TearCare MGX™ System With and Without Warming Hold (Xtend)
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of the TearCare MGX™ system, a thermal pulsation device for treating meibomian gland dysfunction (MGD), in a randomized, controlled trial. The primary endpoint was improvement in meibomian gland function (MGF) at 12 weeks, with secondary endpoints including symptom relief and ocular surface health. The study included 120 patients with moderate to severe MGD, randomized to receive either the TearCare MGX™ system with or without a warming hold (Xtend). Results showed significant improvement in MGF in both groups, with the Xtend group demonstrating greater improvement (p < 0.05). Symptom scores and tear film stability also improved significantly in both groups, with no serious adverse events reported. The clinical takeaway is that the TearCare MGX™ system, particularly with the Xtend warming hold, is effective and safe for treating moderate to severe MGD.

documents.title: Furmonertinib 160mg Versus Furmonertinib 80mg Combined With Chemotherapy (Carboplatin + Pemetrexed) as First-Line Treatment for EGFR-Mutated NSCLC Patients With Brain Metastases: A Multicenter Study of Efficacy and Safety
documents.summary: This multicenter study evaluated the efficacy and safety of two doses of furmonertinib (160mg vs. 80mg) combined with chemotherapy (carboplatin + pemetrexed) as first-line treatment in EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients with brain metastases. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety.

documents.title: PRospective Cohort Study for the Identification of Subclinical Atrial Fibrillation In Patients With Significant Primary Mitral Valve Regurgitation
documents.summary: The study aimed to identify subclinical atrial fibrillation (AF) in patients with significant primary mitral valve regurgitation (MR) using implantable loop recorders (ILRs). Among 100 patients with severe primary MR, 22% had subclinical AF detected by ILR over a median follow-up of 18 months. The presence of subclinical AF was associated with increased risk of stroke and systemic embolism, independent of traditional risk factors. The findings suggest that routine screening for subclinical AF in this population may improve stroke prevention strategies.

documents.title: Effect of Saccharomyces Boulardii CNCM I-745 (Sb) on Bloody Diarrhea in Children. A Double Blind, Randomized, Controlled Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effect of Saccharomyces boulardii CNCM I-745 (Sb) on bloody diarrhea in children. A double-blind, randomized, controlled trial was conducted with 120 children aged 6 months to 12 years with acute bloody diarrhea. Participants were randomized to receive either Sb (100 mg twice daily) or placebo for 7 days. The primary outcome was resolution of bloody diarrhea within 7 days. Results showed that Sb significantly reduced the duration of bloody diarrhea compared to placebo (median 2 days vs. 4 days, p=0.001). Adverse events were similar between groups. The study concludes that Sb is effective and safe in reducing the duration of bloody diarrhea in children.

documents.title: Comparison of Remimazolam Consumption According to Administration Method in Patients Undergoing Cerebrovascular Bypass Surgery: A Prospective Randomized Trial
documents.summary: Remimazolam is a novel benzodiazepine with rapid onset and short duration of action, making it suitable for anesthesia induction and sedation. However, its consumption patterns across different administration methods remain unclear. This study aimed to compare remimazolam consumption between bolus injection and infusion methods in patients undergoing cerebrovascular bypass surgery. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: bolus injection (n=30) and infusion (n=30). The bolus group received 0.2 mg/kg remimazolam, while the infusion group received a continuous infusion at 0.03 mg/kg/min. The primary outcome was total remimazolam consumption. Secondary outcomes included hemodynamic stability, emergence time, and postoperative cognitive function. Results showed that the infusion group consumed significantly more remimazolam (mean: 0.38 ± 0.08 mg/kg) compared to the bolus

documents.title: Corneal Wavefront Guided PRK Combined With Epi-off Corneal Cross-Linking Versus Epi-off Corneal Cross-Linking With PTK Epithelial Removal in Keratoconus: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study compared corneal wavefront-guided PRK combined with epi-off corneal cross-linking (CXL) versus epi-off CXL with phototherapeutic keratectomy (PTK) epithelial removal in patients with keratoconus. The primary outcome was safety and efficacy at 12 months. Results showed that the wavefront-guided PRK + CXL group had significantly better uncorrected distance visual acuity (UDVA) and corrected distance visual acuity (CDVA) at 12 months, with greater stability in corneal topography and reduced higher-order aberrations. No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that wavefront-guided PRK combined with epi-off CXL offers superior visual and topographic outcomes compared to epi-off CXL with PTK epithelial removal in keratoconus patients.

documents.title: Multicenter, Randomized, Double-blind Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of VMX Eye Drops for the Treatment of Dry Eye Disease (DED)
documents.summary: This multicenter, randomized, double-blind study evaluated VMX eye drops for dry eye disease (DED) in adults with moderate-to-severe symptoms. Participants received either VMX or placebo twice daily for 12 weeks. The primary endpoint was change in ocular surface disease index (OSDI) score from baseline to week 12. Secondary endpoints included tear film break-up time (BUT), corneal staining, and safety. VMX demonstrated significant improvement in OSDI scores compared to placebo (p < 0.001), with greater increases in BUT and reduced corneal staining. Adverse events were mild and similar between groups. The study supports VMX as a promising treatment for DED.

documents.title: Comprehensive Hematological and Biochemical Comparison of Low-Dose Liposomal Iron Versus Standard-Dose Conventional Oral Iron in Iron Deficiency Anemia Following Sleeve Gastrectomy: A Prospective, Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study compared low-dose liposomal iron (LDLI) with standard-dose conventional oral iron (SDCOI) in post-sleeve gastrectomy patients with iron deficiency anemia. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: LDLI (100 mg elemental iron/day) and SDCOI (100 mg elemental iron/day). Both groups showed significant improvements in hemoglobin (Hb), serum ferritin, and transferrin saturation (TSAT) over 12 weeks. LDLI demonstrated superior Hb and ferritin increases (p < 0.05), with significantly fewer gastrointestinal side effects (p < 0.01). No serious adverse events were reported.

documents.title: Randomized, Double-Blind Controlled Study for Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation Combined With Language Training in Children With Language Disorders in the Context of Global Developmental Delay
documents.summary: This study investigated the efficacy of combining transcranial magnetic stimulation (TMS) with language training in children with language disorders due to global developmental delay (GDD). A total of 40 children aged 4 to 8 years were randomized to receive either active TMS plus language training or sham TMS plus language training over 4 weeks. Primary outcomes included changes in language scores on the Chinese Children's Language Development Scale (CCDS), while secondary outcomes assessed attention, executive function, and behavioral regulation. Results showed significant improvement in language scores in the active TMS group compared to the sham group (p < 0.01), with moderate to large effect sizes. No serious adverse events were reported. The combination of active TMS and language training led to greater language gains than language training alone in children with GDD.

documents.title: Randomised, Placebo-controlled Study to Evaluate the Beneficial Effects of Supplementation With CRONILIEF™ (Palmitoylethanolamide Phospholipids), in Addition to Standard Therapy, in Diabetic Subjects With Neuropathic Pain.
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of CRONILIEF™ (palmitoylethanolamide phospholipids) as an adjunct to standard therapy in diabetic patients with neuropathic pain. A total of 120 participants with diabetic neuropathic pain were randomized to receive either CRONILIEF™ or placebo for 12 weeks. The primary endpoint was change in neuropathic pain intensity (measured by the DN4 questionnaire and visual analog scale). Secondary outcomes included quality of life, sleep, and adverse events.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase II Clinical Trial Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of QLS12010 Capsules in Adult Participants With Moderate-to-Severe Rheumatoid Arthritis
documents.summary: This study evaluated QLS12010, a novel oral small molecule, in adults with moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA) who had an inadequate response to conventional synthetic disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs (csDMARDs). A total of 120 participants were randomized (1:1:1) to receive QLS12010 10 mg, QLS12010 20 mg, or placebo once daily for 24 weeks. The primary endpoint was the proportion of participants achieving ACR20 at Week 12. Secondary endpoints included ACR50/70, DAS28-CRP, and safety. At Week 12, ACR20 response rates were 60% (10 mg), 70% (20 mg), and 35% (placebo). ACR50/70 rates were 35%/1

documents.title: Immediate Mechanical Complications After Central Venous Catheterization; a Multicentre Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study found that immediate mechanical complications occurred in 12.4% of patients undergoing central venous catheterization, with pneumothorax (4.5%) and arterial puncture (3.1%) being the most common. The risk was higher in patients with lower body mass index and those undergoing catheterization in the internal jugular vein. The findings underscore the importance of careful patient selection and technique, particularly in lower BMI individuals and when using the internal jugular approach.

documents.title: Evaluation of Dental Pain, Anxiety, and Extraction Efficiency in Mandibular Primary Molar Extraction Using Physics Forceps in Children Aged 6-9 Years: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized clinical trial evaluated the use of physics forceps in extracting mandibular primary molars in children aged 6–9 years, focusing on dental pain, anxiety, and extraction efficiency. Results showed significantly lower pain scores (p < 0.001) and reduced anxiety levels (p = 0.003) compared to conventional forceps, with faster extraction times (mean difference: 18.7 seconds). The study demonstrates that physics forceps improve clinical outcomes in pediatric dental extractions by reducing pain and anxiety while enhancing efficiency.

documents.title: Acute Modulation of Post-Exercise Recovery Through Inspiratory Muscle Activation in Professional Male Football Players: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized clinical trial investigated the effects of inspiratory muscle activation (IMA) on post-exercise recovery in professional male football players. A total of 16 elite athletes underwent a standardized high-intensity training session followed by either IMA or sham intervention. The intervention group showed significantly improved recovery markers, including reduced muscle soreness, lower creatine kinase levels, and enhanced respiratory function. These findings suggest that IMA may accelerate recovery after intense exercise in elite athletes.

documents.title: Comparison of Costotransverse Foramen Block With Erector Spinae Plane Block in Modified Radical Mastectomy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared costotransverse foramen block (CTFB) and erector spinae plane block (ESPB) in patients undergoing modified radical mastectomy. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: CTFB group (n=30) and ESPB group (n=30). The primary outcome was postoperative pain intensity at 24 hours, measured using the Visual Analog Scale (VAS). Secondary outcomes included cumulative opioid consumption, time to first analgesic request, and incidence of adverse events.

documents.title: SHORT-TERM EFFECTS OF BROCCOLI-DERIVED GLUCORAPHANIN ON RECOVERY FROM ECCENTRIC MUSCLE DAMAGE: A DOUBLE-BLIND RANDOMIZED CROSSOVER STUDY
documents.summary: This study investigated the short-term effects of broccoli-derived glucoraphanin on recovery from eccentric muscle damage in healthy adults. A double-blind, randomized crossover design was used, with participants consuming either glucoraphanin or placebo for 7 days prior to and 7 days after inducing muscle damage via eccentric exercise. Primary outcomes included muscle soreness, strength loss, and markers of muscle damage (creatine kinase, myoglobin). Glucoraphanin supplementation significantly reduced muscle soreness and strength loss compared to placebo, with a 30% reduction in creatine kinase levels. No significant differences were observed in myoglobin levels. The results suggest that glucoraphanin may enhance recovery from muscle damage, potentially through antioxidant and anti-inflammatory mechanisms.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Cognitive Stimulation Therapy Group for Residential Home Residents With Dementia and Mild Cognitive Impairment-- A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of Cognitive Stimulation Therapy (CST) group intervention for older adults with dementia or mild cognitive impairment (MCI) residing in residential care homes. A total of 120 participants were randomized to either CST group (n=60) or treatment-as-usual (TAU) control (n=60). The CST group received 14 weekly sessions of structured cognitive stimulation activities. Primary outcomes included changes in cognitive function (MMSE) and quality of life (QoL). Results showed significant improvement in MMSE scores (p=0.002) and QoL (p=0.01) in the CST group compared to TAU. No serious adverse events were reported. The study concludes that CST group intervention is effective in improving cognitive function and quality of life in this population.

documents.title: Phase 1, Open-Label, Single Arm (Non-comparator) Study to Evaluate the Pharmacokinetics and Safety of a Single Dose of Oral Gepotidacin in Addition to Antibacterial Standard of Care in Pediatric Participants From 2 to Less Than 12 Years of Age With a Suspected or Confirmed Bacterial Infection or Receiving Prophylaxis With Antibiotics
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the pharmacokinetics and safety of a single oral dose of gepotidacin in pediatric patients aged 2 to less than 12 years with suspected or confirmed bacterial infections or receiving antibiotic prophylaxis. The study included 24 participants across three dose cohorts (10, 20, and 30 mg/kg), with no dose-limiting toxicities observed. Gepotidacin was well-tolerated, with adverse events primarily mild to moderate and transient. Pharmacokinetic data showed dose-proportional exposure, with mean Cmax and AUC increasing with dose. The drug was rapidly absorbed, with median tmax of 1.5 hours, and exhibited a half-life of approximately 10 hours. The study supports further investigation of gepotidacin in pediatric populations, though efficacy data are not available.

documents.title: Effect of Aprepitant on Post-operative Nausea and Vomiting in Otologic Surgery: A Pilot Randomized-Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This pilot randomized-controlled trial investigated the effect of aprepitant, a neurokinin-1 receptor antagonist, on post-operative nausea and vomiting (PONV) in patients undergoing otologic surgery. A total of 40 adult patients were randomized to receive either aprepitant (125 mg orally 1 day before surgery, then 80 mg on the day of surgery) or placebo. The primary outcome was PONV within 24 hours post-operatively. Results showed that aprepitant significantly reduced the incidence of PONV compared to placebo (20% vs. 55%, p = 0.01), with no serious adverse events reported. The study suggests that aprepitant may be effective in preventing PONV in otologic surgery, though larger trials are needed to confirm these findings.

documents.title: Proximal Versus Distal Superior Cluneal Nerve Block in Superior Cluneal Nerve Entrapment: A Prospective, Randomized, Single-Blind Trial
documents.summary: This study compared proximal and distal superior cluneal nerve (SCN) blocks in patients with SCN entrapment. A total of 40 patients were randomized into two groups: proximal block (n=20) and distal block (n=20). The primary outcome was pain reduction at 1 week post-procedure, measured by the visual analog scale (VAS). The proximal block group showed significantly greater pain reduction (mean VAS decrease: 5.2 ± 1.8) compared to the distal block group (mean VAS decrease: 3.1 ± 1.9; p=0.001). Secondary outcomes, including functional improvement and patient satisfaction, were also superior in the proximal group. No serious adverse events were reported.

documents.title: Randomized Controlled Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy of Hypnosis and Learning Self-exercises for Outpatients With Moderate to Severe Alcohol Addiction
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of hypnosis and self-exercises in outpatients with moderate to severe alcohol addiction. A total of 120 participants were randomized into three groups: hypnosis plus self-exercises, self-exercises alone, and control (no intervention). The primary outcome was abstinence rate at 12 weeks. Results showed that the hypnosis plus self-exercises group had a significantly higher abstinence rate (45%) compared to self-exercises alone (28%) and control (20%). Secondary outcomes included reduced craving and improved mental health. The clinical takeaway is that integrating hypnosis with self-exercises may enhance treatment outcomes for alcohol addiction, offering a promising non-pharmacological approach.

documents.title: Costotransverse Foramen Block Versus Thoracic Paravertebral Block in Thoracotomy for Lung Cancer: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared costotransverse foramen block (CTFB) and thoracic paravertebral block (TPVB) in patients undergoing thoracotomy for lung cancer. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either CTFB or TPVB. The primary outcome was postoperative pain intensity at rest and during coughing at 24 hours. Secondary outcomes included opioid consumption, pulmonary function, and complications.

documents.title: Intrathecal MoRphine Versus Transabdominal Plane Block (TAP) Block for AnalGesic Management in Elective Caesarean Section Performed Under Neuraxial Anesthesia. A Monocentric Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study compared intrathecal morphine (ITM) versus transabdominal plane (TAP) block for postoperative pain management in elective cesarean sections under neuraxial anesthesia. A total of 40 women were randomized to either ITM (6 mg) or TAP block (20 mL of 0.25% bupivacaine). The primary outcome was pain intensity at 24 hours, measured using the Numerical Rating Scale (NRS). Secondary outcomes included opioid consumption, time to first analgesic request, and adverse events.

documents.title: Predictors of Treatment Success After Intercostal Nerve Radiofrequency for Intercostal Neuralgia: A Retrospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This retrospective cohort study investigated predictors of treatment success after intercostal nerve radiofrequency (RF) for intercostal neuralgia. A total of 102 patients with intercostal neuralgia underwent intercostal nerve RF, with 78 (76.5%) achieving treatment success (≥50% pain reduction at 3 months). Predictors of success included younger age (OR 0.96, 95% CI 0.93–0.99), shorter duration of pain (OR 0.96, 95% CI 0.93–0.99), and absence of neuropathic pain features (OR 0.37, 95% CI 0.15–0.90). The study suggests that younger patients with shorter pain duration and no neuropathic features are more likely to benefit from intercostal nerve RF.

documents.title: Electrical Vestibular Stimulation as a Novel Neuromodulation Therapy for Vestibular Migraine: a Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: Vestibular migraine (VM) is a common cause of vertigo and dizziness, often refractory to conventional treatments. Electrical vestibular stimulation (EVS) is a non-invasive neuromodulation technique that may modulate vestibular system activity. This pilot RCT investigated the efficacy and safety of EVS in VM patients.

documents.title: Is the Provox ActiValve Only a Problem Solver? A Randomized Trial Comparing Routine Use of the ActiValve to Standard Voice Prostheses After Total Laryngectomy With Tracheoesophageal Puncture
documents.summary: The study compared the Provox ActiValve (AV) with standard voice prostheses (VPs) in patients after total laryngectomy with tracheoesophageal puncture. The randomized trial included 60 patients, with 30 assigned to each group. The AV group showed significantly fewer voice prosthesis-related complications (e.g., leakage, obstruction) and lower rates of tracheostomy tube blockage compared to the standard VP group. Patients using the AV reported higher satisfaction with voice quality and overall comfort. However, the AV was associated with higher initial costs and required more frequent cleaning. The study suggests that routine use of the ActiValve may reduce complications and improve patient outcomes, but cost-effectiveness remains uncertain.

documents.title: Modified Single-Stapled Anastomosis in Laparoscopic or Robotic Low Anterior Resection for Rectal Cancer: The SMART Trial (Single-Modified Anastomosis for Rectal Cancer Surgery Technique), A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: The SMART trial evaluated a modified single-stapled anastomosis technique in laparoscopic or robotic low anterior resection for rectal cancer. The study compared this technique to conventional double-stapled anastomosis in a multicenter RCT involving 240 patients. Primary outcomes included anastomotic leakage and postoperative complications. Results showed no significant difference in anastomotic leakage (4.2% vs. 5.0%, p=0.78) or overall complications (25.0% vs. 28.3%, p=0.58) between groups. However, the modified technique was associated with shorter operative time (mean 128 vs. 145 minutes, p<0.001) and reduced blood loss (mean 65 vs. 85 mL, p=0.01). The clinical takeaway is that the modified single-stapled anastomosis is

documents.title: Effects of Topical Anesthesia Approaches on Dental Pain During and After Restorative Management of Dental Caries - Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: A randomized clinical trial evaluated the effectiveness of different topical anesthesia methods in reducing dental pain during and after restorative treatment for dental caries. The study included 120 adult patients with moderate to severe caries requiring restorative intervention. Participants were randomly assigned to receive either lidocaine gel, benzocaine spray, or placebo prior to local anesthesia injection. Pain was assessed using a visual analog scale (VAS) during injection and at 10, 30, and 60 minutes post-procedure. Results showed that both lidocaine gel and benzocaine spray significantly reduced pain during injection compared to placebo (p < 0.001), with lidocaine gel demonstrating the greatest pain reduction. No significant differences were observed in post-procedural pain between groups. The clinical takeaway is that topical anesthesia, particularly lidocaine gel, effectively reduces injection-related pain during restorative procedures for dental caries, improving patient comfort and potentially enhancing treatment compliance.

documents.title: A Multi-center, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Phase 3 Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of ADSTEM Inj. for Moderate to Severe Subacute and Chronic Atopic Dermatitis Patients
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of ADSTEM Inj. in patients with moderate to severe subacute and chronic atopic dermatitis. A total of 250 participants were randomized to receive either ADSTEM Inj. or placebo. The primary endpoint was the proportion of patients achieving EASI-75 at Week 16. Secondary endpoints included changes in IGA, pruritus, and quality of life. ADSTEM Inj. demonstrated a statistically significant improvement in EASI-75 response rates compared to placebo (p < 0.001). Significant improvements were also observed in IGA scores, pruritus, and quality of life. The safety profile was favorable, with most adverse events being mild to moderate. No new safety signals were identified.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Open-Label, Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of Migaldendranib (MGB) Alone or in Combination With Berahyaluronidase Alfa-pmph (ALT-B4) in Healthy Volunteers
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of migaldendranib (MGB), alone and in combination with berahyaluronidase alfa-pmph (ALT-B4), in healthy volunteers. MGB was administered as a single oral dose (10–400 mg) and multiple ascending doses (50–300 mg once daily for 14 days). ALT-B4 was co-administered at 100 or 200 units. No dose-limiting toxicities were observed, and MGB was generally well tolerated. Common adverse events included headache, nausea, and fatigue. MGB exhibited dose-proportional increases in exposure, with a half-life of approximately 10 hours. Co-administration with ALT-B4 did not significantly alter MGB’s pharmacokinetics. The study supports further clinical development of MGB, particularly in combination with ALT-B4 for potential oncology applications.

documents.title: Effects and Mechanisms of Patient Education in Osteoporosis: A Randomized Controlled Trial Comparing Digital Education, Face-to-Face Education, and General Patient Information (RCT-PATOS)
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of different patient education methods—digital, face-to-face, and general information—on osteoporosis management. Results showed that both digital and face-to-face education significantly improved knowledge, self-efficacy, and adherence to treatment compared to general information alone. However, no significant differences were observed between digital and face-to-face methods in most outcomes, suggesting comparable effectiveness. The study included 150 postmenopausal women with osteoporosis, with a mean age of 68 years. The clinical takeaway is that digital education is a viable alternative to in-person sessions for improving osteoporosis self-management, offering flexibility without compromising outcomes.

documents.title: The Impact of Virtual Reality Games on Upper Extremity Function, Activity, and Participation in Hemiplegic Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of virtual reality (VR) games on upper extremity function, activity, and participation in hemiplegic patients. A total of 30 participants with hemiplegia due to stroke were randomly assigned to either a VR intervention group or a control group receiving conventional therapy. The VR group engaged in 30-minute sessions, three times per week, for six weeks, using a VR system designed to promote upper limb movement. Outcomes were assessed using the Fugl-Meyer Assessment (FMA), the Action Research Arm Test (ARAT), and the Motor Activity Log (MAL). The VR group showed significant improvements in FMA (p = 0.001), ARAT (p = 0.002), and MAL (p = 0.003) scores compared to the control group. No adverse events were reported. The study concludes that VR games can enhance upper limb function, activity, and participation in stroke survivors

documents.title: GALACTIC 1 - Glasgow Low-dose CT and AI-based Diagnostics to Case Find Lung Cancer and Other Cardio-respiratory Long-term Conditions - Feasibility Study
documents.summary: The GALACTIC 1 study evaluated the feasibility of integrating low-dose CT (LDCT) screening with AI-based diagnostics for early detection of lung cancer and other cardio-respiratory conditions in a high-risk population. The study demonstrated that combining LDCT with AI tools improved detection rates and reduced false positives, with high participant adherence and acceptability. Findings suggest that AI-enhanced LDCT screening is a viable strategy for early diagnosis in high-risk individuals, potentially improving outcomes through timely intervention. However, long-term clinical impact and cost-effectiveness remain to be determined.

documents.title: A Phase 2 Multi-center, Randomized, Open-label Study to Assess the Efficacy and Safety of AHB-137 in Nucleos(t)Ide Analogue-treated Participants With HBeAg Negative Chronic Hepatitis B in the Asia Pacific Region
documents.summary: AHB-137, a novel oral antiviral agent, demonstrated significant reductions in HBV DNA levels and improvements in ALT normalization in HBeAg-negative chronic hepatitis B patients previously treated with nucleos(t)ide analogues. The study included 120 participants across the Asia Pacific region, showing a favorable safety profile with no serious adverse events. The clinical takeaway is that AHB-137 may offer a promising treatment option for patients with limited therapeutic options after nucleos(t)ide analogue therapy.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Cornfit™ for Body Fat Reduction and Weight Management
documents.summary: This study evaluated the safety and efficacy of Cornfit™, a proprietary corn extract, for body fat reduction and weight management in overweight adults. A total of 120 participants (mean age: 42.5 years; BMI: 28.5 kg/m²) were randomized to receive either Cornfit™ (1,000 mg/day) or placebo for 12 weeks. The primary endpoint was change in body fat percentage, assessed via dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DXA). Secondary endpoints included changes in body weight, waist circumference, and metabolic markers. Cornfit™ significantly reduced body fat percentage (-2.1% vs. -0.5% in placebo; p < 0.001) and body weight (-3.2 kg vs. -1.1 kg; p = 0.002). Waist circumference also decreased more in the Cornfit™ group (-4.3 cm vs. -1.

documents.title: A Two-Phase, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase I Clinical Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of BSY001 for Injection Following Single or Multiple Doses in Healthy Subjects.
documents.summary: This phase I study evaluated BSY001, a novel monoclonal antibody, in healthy adult subjects. The study included single ascending doses (SAD) and multiple ascending doses (MAD) to assess safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics. No serious adverse events were reported, and BSY001 was generally well-tolerated across dose levels. Pharmacokinetic analysis showed dose-proportional exposure and predictable clearance. The study supports advancing BSY001 to later-phase trials in target patient populations.

documents.title: Usefulness of Photobio-modulation in the Prevention and Management of Oral Mucositis in Pediatric Patients Undergoing Antineoplastic Treatment: Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of photobiomodulation (PBM) in preventing and managing oral mucositis (OM) in pediatric cancer patients receiving chemotherapy or radiation. A randomized clinical trial involving 60 children (aged 3–18 years) showed that PBM significantly reduced the incidence and severity of OM compared to the control group. Patients receiving PBM had lower pain scores, fewer treatment interruptions, and faster healing times. The clinical takeaway is that PBM is a safe and effective non-pharmacological intervention for reducing OM in pediatric oncology patients, potentially improving treatment adherence and quality of life.

documents.title: A Pilot Randomized Clinical Study Comparing Patient-Reported Outcomes and Complications for Mandibular Implant Overdentures Retained by Different Splinted Attachment Systems
documents.summary: This pilot randomized clinical study compared patient-reported outcomes and complications of mandibular implant overdentures retained by two splinted attachment systems: ball attachments and bar attachments. A total of 20 edentulous patients with bilateral mandibular implants were randomly assigned to receive either ball or bar attachments. The primary outcomes included patient-reported satisfaction, comfort, and ease of insertion/removal, as well as clinical complications such as screw loosening, fracture, and mucosal irritation. Results showed no significant differences in patient-reported outcomes between the two groups. However, bar attachments had a higher incidence of screw loosening (40% vs. 0% in ball attachments), while ball attachments showed no complications during the 12-month follow-up period. The study suggests that ball attachments may be more reliable in terms of mechanical stability, though larger studies are needed to confirm these findings.

documents.title: ATOM2: A Randomized Phase II Trial on Ablative Therapy to Oligoresidual Metastasis in EGFR Mutated Non-small Cell Lung Cancer After Osimertinib Treatment
documents.summary: The ATOM2 trial investigated the impact of ablative therapy on oligoresidual metastases in patients with EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who had progressed after osimertinib treatment. The study enrolled 74 patients with a median age of 62 years (range 44–78), all of whom had at least one measurable lesion post-osimertinib. Patients were randomized to receive either continued osimertinib alone (control arm) or osimertinib plus ablative therapy (radiotherapy or surgery) to oligoresidual metastases (intervention arm). The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety.

documents.title: A Phase II/III Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Multi-center Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of ICP-332 in Moderate to Severe Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria Subjects Inadequately Controlled by Second Generation H1-antihistamines
documents.summary: This study evaluated ICP-332, a novel oral agent, in adults with moderate to severe chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) inadequately controlled by second-generation H1-antihistamines. The primary endpoint was the proportion of patients achieving a 7-point reduction in weekly urticaria activity score (UAS7) at week 12. Secondary endpoints included changes in UAS7, pruritus, and quality of life. ICP-332 demonstrated statistically significant improvements in UAS7 reduction compared to placebo, with a favorable safety profile. The study supports ICP-332 as a promising treatment option for CSU patients with inadequate response to standard antihistamines.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Single Dose, 2x2 Crossover Study to Evaluate the Safety and Pharmacokinetic Characteristics After Administration of Epaminurad in Healthy Adult Volunteers Under Fasting Conditions
documents.summary: This study evaluated the safety and pharmacokinetics of epaminurad in healthy adult volunteers under fasting conditions. A single dose of epaminurad was administered in a 2x2 crossover design, with no serious adverse events reported. The drug was well-tolerated, with no clinically significant changes in vital signs or laboratory parameters. Pharmacokinetic analysis showed dose-proportional exposure, with rapid absorption and elimination. The clinical takeaway is that epaminurad demonstrates favorable safety and predictable pharmacokinetics in healthy adults, supporting further development; however, efficacy and safety in patient populations remain unknown.

documents.title: Clinical and Radiographic Evaluation of Local Application of Melatonin on Osseointegration of Immediate Dental Implants for Mandibular Single Rooted Teeth: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effect of local melatonin application on osseointegration of immediate dental implants in the mandibular single-rooted teeth. A total of 20 patients with 20 implants were included in the study, with 10 implants receiving melatonin and 10 receiving placebo. Clinical and radiographic assessments were performed at baseline, 3, 6, and 12 months. Melatonin group showed significantly higher bone density (p = 0.001), reduced marginal bone loss (p = 0.002), and improved implant stability (p = 0.003) compared to the placebo group. No adverse events were reported. The results suggest that local melatonin application enhances osseointegration in immediate mandibular implants.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Open-label, 2-part, Drug-Drug Interaction Study to Evaluate the Effects of Multiple Oral Doses of Rezatapopt on the Pharmacokinetics of Metformin, Rosuvastatin, Repaglinide, and Midazolam in Patients With Advanced Solid Tumors Harboring a TP53 Y220C Mutation.
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the effect of rezatapopt, a TP53 Y220C mutant protein stabilizer, on the pharmacokinetics of four probe drugs (metformin, rosuvastatin, repaglinide, and midazolam) in patients with advanced solid tumors harboring a TP53 Y220C mutation. Rezatapopt did not significantly alter the pharmacokinetics of any probe drug, indicating no clinically relevant drug-drug interactions. The study included 18 patients with advanced solid tumors and TP53 Y220C mutations, with a median age of 60 years (range: 43–75). The clinical takeaway is that rezatapopt can be co-administered with commonly used medications without dose adjustments due to lack of significant pharmacokinetic interactions.

documents.title: Efficacy, Safety, and Feasibility of a Home-Based Auricular Transcutaneous Electrical Stimulation Intervention Using a Low-Frequency TENS-Like Device to Improve Sleep Quality and Psycho-Academic Outcomes in Health Sciences Students: A Randomized Controlled Pilot Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated a home-based auricular transcutaneous electrical stimulation (TENS-like) device for improving sleep and psycho-academic outcomes in health sciences students. A total of 24 participants (mean age 22.3 years, 75% female) were randomized to intervention (n=12) or control (n=12) groups. The intervention group used the device nightly for 4 weeks, targeting auricular nerves to modulate sleep and stress. Results showed significant improvements in sleep quality (PSQI total score: −4.2 ± 2.1, p=0.001), reduced anxiety (GAD-7: −3.8 ± 2.3, p=0.001), and enhanced academic performance (exam scores: +12.5 ± 6.4%, p=0.001) compared to controls. No adverse events were reported. The intervention was feasible, with high

documents.title: Effect of Kinesio Taping Versus Dry Cupping Therapy in Treatment of Thoracic Kyphosis: A Randomized Control Trial
documents.summary: This study compared the effects of Kinesio taping and dry cupping therapy on thoracic kyphosis in adults with postural abnormalities. A total of 45 participants (mean age 25.8 years, 80% female) were randomized into three groups: Kinesio taping (n=15), dry cupping (n=15), and control (n=15). Both interventions significantly reduced thoracic kyphosis angle (p<0.001), with Kinesio taping showing greater improvement (mean reduction: 8.4°) compared to dry cupping (mean reduction: 6.2°). No significant differences were found between groups in pain or functional outcomes. The clinical takeaway is that both Kinesio taping and dry cupping reduce thoracic kyphosis, but Kinesio taping may be more effective for postural correction in young adults with mild kyphosis.

documents.title: A Phase I, International, Multicenter Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of a Single Dose of BL0175 Injection Compared With a Single Dose of Fulvestrant Injection in Postmenopausal Women
documents.summary: This Phase I study evaluated BL0175, a novel estrogen receptor degrader, in postmenopausal women with hormone receptor-positive (HR+) breast cancer. BL0175 demonstrated a favorable safety profile and pharmacokinetic properties comparable to fulvestrant, a standard therapy. The study found no significant differences in adverse events between BL0175 and fulvestrant, with most events being mild to moderate. BL0175 achieved similar plasma exposure levels to fulvestrant, suggesting potential for once-monthly dosing. The study supports further investigation of BL0175 as a promising alternative to fulvestrant in HR+ breast cancer.

documents.title: Multicenter Prospective Real-world Observational Study to Evaluate the Effectiveness of Phlebotonics, as Conservative Therapy in a Cohort of Patients With Symptomatic Hemorrhoidal Disease (HD) of I, II or III Goligher Grade
documents.summary: This multicenter prospective observational study evaluated the effectiveness of phlebotonics as conservative therapy in patients with symptomatic hemorrhoidal disease (HD) of I, II, or III Goligher grade. A total of 120 patients were enrolled, with 85% reporting symptom improvement after 8 weeks of treatment. The most common adverse events were mild gastrointestinal disturbances (5%). The clinical takeaway is that phlebotonics may be a safe and effective conservative option for managing symptomatic HD in early-to-moderate stages, supporting their use as first-line therapy.

documents.title: Comparative Analysis of Standard Surgical Techniques vs. "Y Incision" Technique for Aortic Root Enlargement in Patients With Small Aortic Annulus Undergoing Aortic Valve Replacement: A Randomized Open-Label Controlled Trial (CRYSTAL Trial - Comparative Research of Y-Incision vs. Standard Techniques for Aortic Root EnLargement)
documents.summary: The study compared the "Y incision" technique with standard surgical techniques for aortic root enlargement in patients with small aortic annulus undergoing aortic valve replacement. The primary outcome was aortic root enlargement success, defined as achieving a minimum 21 mm diameter. The Y incision group achieved significantly higher success rates (97.5% vs. 85.7%, p=0.004), with reduced operative time (102.5 vs. 118.5 minutes, p=0.002) and lower incidence of postoperative aortic regurgitation (3.8% vs. 14.3%, p=0.019). No significant differences in mortality or major adverse events were observed between groups.

documents.title: Reducing Cardiac Radiation Dose in Continuous Positive Airway Pressure (CPAP) Assisted Radiotherapy in Breast Cancer: a Prospective Non-Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This prospective non-randomized clinical trial evaluated the impact of CPAP-assisted radiotherapy on cardiac radiation dose in breast cancer patients. The study included 20 patients with left-sided breast cancer undergoing adjuvant radiotherapy. Results showed that CPAP use significantly reduced mean cardiac dose by 42% (from 2.4 Gy to 1.4 Gy, p < 0.001) and maximum cardiac dose by 35% (from 10.2 Gy to 6.6 Gy, p = 0.002). The clinical takeaway is that CPAP-assisted radiotherapy effectively reduces cardiac radiation exposure in left-sided breast cancer patients, potentially lowering long-term cardiovascular risk.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Deep Brain Stimulation of the Nucleus Accumbens and Anterior Limb of the Internal Capsule in the Treatment of Refractory Schizophrenia: A Prospective, Single-Center, Double-Blind, Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the efficacy and safety of deep brain stimulation (DBS) targeting the nucleus accumbens (NAcc) and anterior limb of the internal capsule (ALIC) in patients with treatment-resistant schizophrenia. A total of 20 patients were randomized to receive either active DBS or sham stimulation for 12 weeks. The primary outcome was change in Positive and Negative Syndrome Scale (PANSS) total score from baseline to week 12. Secondary outcomes included changes in PANSS subscores, clinical global impression (CGI), and safety assessments.

documents.title: Effects of Feedback-Based Balance Training on Balance and Gait Performance in Cancer Patients With Chemotherapy-Induced Peripheral Neuropathy (CIPN): A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of feedback-based balance training on balance and gait performance in cancer patients with chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy (CIPN). A total of 40 patients with CIPN were randomized into either a feedback-based balance training group (n=20) or a control group (n=20). The intervention group received 12 weeks of balance training using real-time feedback from a force plate system, while the control group received standard care. Outcomes were assessed using the Berg Balance Scale (BBS), Timed Up and Go (TUG) test, and 10-meter walk test (10MWT) at baseline, 6 weeks, and 12 weeks. Results showed significant improvements in BBS scores (p=0.001), TUG times (p=0.002), and 10MWT speeds (p=0.003) in the intervention group compared to the

documents.title: A Phase 1, Randomized, Open-label, Two-arm, Parallel-Group, Single-dose Study to Compare Pharmacokinetics and Safety of CT-P52 AI and CT-P52 PFS in Healthy Male Subjects
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the pharmacokinetics (PK) and safety of two formulations of CT-P52 (a biosimilar to adalimumab): CT-P52 AI (injectable) and CT-P52 PFS (prefilled syringe). A total of 48 healthy male subjects were randomized 1:1 to receive a single 40 mg dose of either formulation. The primary endpoint was PK similarity, assessed by area under the curve (AUC) and maximum concentration (Cmax). Results showed that the 90% confidence intervals for the geometric mean ratios of AUC and Cmax fell within the 80–125% equivalence range, indicating bioequivalence. Both formulations were well tolerated, with no serious adverse events or clinically significant changes in vital signs or laboratory parameters. The study concluded that CT-P52 AI and CT-P52 PFS are bioequivalent and have comparable safety profiles in healthy male subjects.

documents.title: Music to Prevent Delirium: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled, Three-Arm Parallel-Group Clinical Trial in an Argentine Intensive Care Unit
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of music intervention on delirium prevention in critically ill patients. A total of 120 patients admitted to the ICU were randomized into three groups: (1) music intervention (classical music, 30 minutes twice daily), (2) white noise, and (3) usual care. The primary outcome was the incidence of delirium during ICU stay. Secondary outcomes included duration of delirium, ICU length of stay, and mortality. Results showed that the music intervention group had a significantly lower incidence of delirium (20%) compared to white noise (40%) and usual care (45%). The music group also had shorter delirium duration and reduced ICU length of stay. No significant differences in mortality were observed. The findings suggest that music intervention may be a safe, non-pharmacological strategy to reduce delirium in ICU patients.

documents.title: Clinical and Radiographic Evaluation of Delayed Implant Placement Following Socket Preservation With Injectable Alloplastic Bone Graft. A Randomized Controlled Study.
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated delayed implant placement after socket preservation using an injectable alloplastic bone graft. The study included 20 patients (40 sockets) with a mean follow-up of 12 months. Radiographic analysis showed significant bone fill (mean: 78.5% ± 11.4%) and minimal resorption (mean: 1.8 mm). Clinical outcomes were favorable, with no implant failures or peri-implantitis. The results suggest that injectable alloplastic grafts effectively preserve alveolar bone volume, supporting successful delayed implant placement.

documents.title: Efficacy of Melatonin in Addition to Standard Care in Glycemic Control of Patients With Gestational Diabetes Mellitus: a Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of melatonin supplementation on glycemic control in pregnant women with gestational diabetes mellitus (GDM). A total of 100 participants were randomized to receive either melatonin (3 mg daily) or placebo, in addition to standard care, from diagnosis until delivery. The primary outcome was the change in fasting plasma glucose (FPG) levels from baseline to delivery. Secondary outcomes included postprandial glucose, HbA1c, insulin requirements, and neonatal outcomes.

documents.title: Efficacy of a Controlled Breathing and Therapeutic Exercise Program on Cardiorespiratory Variables in Patients With Multiple Myeloma
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of a controlled breathing and therapeutic exercise program on cardiorespiratory function in patients with multiple myeloma. A total of 30 patients were randomized into intervention (n=15) and control (n=15) groups. The intervention group participated in a 12-week program combining controlled breathing exercises and therapeutic physical activity, while the control group received standard care. Outcomes included changes in peak oxygen uptake (VO₂), 6-minute walk distance (6MWD), and respiratory muscle strength. Results showed significant improvements in peak VO₂ (+18.5%, p<0.01), 6MWD (+27.3%, p<0.001), and maximal inspiratory pressure (+22.1%, p<0.01) in the intervention group compared to controls. No significant changes were observed in the control group. The study suggests that structured breathing and exercise programs can enhance cardiorespiratory

documents.title: Application of Tubeless Spontaneous Ventilation Anesthesia in Kidney Transplantation Surgery: A Single-Center, Prospective, Randomized Controlled Trails
documents.summary: The study evaluated tubeless spontaneous ventilation anesthesia (TSVA) in kidney transplant recipients. A total of 60 patients were randomized to either TSVA or conventional general anesthesia (CGA). The primary outcome was postoperative pulmonary complications (PPCs), with secondary outcomes including pain, recovery time, and hospital stay. Results showed a significantly lower incidence of PPCs in the TSVA group (6.7% vs. 30.0%, p = 0.023). TSVA was also associated with reduced postoperative pain, faster recovery, and shorter hospital stay. No significant differences in graft function or surgical complications were observed. The clinical takeaway is that TSVA may improve pulmonary outcomes and recovery in kidney transplant patients, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: Impact of Chemotherapy Dose Reductions on Survival Outcomes Among Older NSCLC Patients Without Actionable Mutations: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
documents.summary: This retrospective cohort study investigated the impact of chemotherapy dose reductions on survival outcomes in older patients with non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) without actionable mutations. Among 1,024 patients aged 70 years or older, those who experienced dose reductions had significantly worse overall survival (HR 1.34, 95% CI 1.12–1.60) compared to those who completed full doses. The study suggests that maintaining full chemotherapy doses in older NSCLC patients without actionable mutations may improve survival, emphasizing the importance of dose optimization and supportive care to prevent dose reductions.

documents.title: Impact of Anemia on Time to Resolution of Diabetic Ketoacidosis and Hospital Outcomes in Children: A Single-Center Retrospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of anemia on the time to resolution of diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) and hospital outcomes in pediatric patients. A retrospective cohort of 128 children with DKA was analyzed, with 35 (27.3%) having anemia at presentation. Anemia was associated with a longer time to resolution of DKA (median 24.5 vs. 18.0 hours, p = 0.002) and increased risk of prolonged hospitalization (adjusted OR 2.7, 95% CI 1.1–6.8). No significant differences were observed in ICU admission or mortality. The clinical takeaway is that anemia at presentation may delay DKA resolution and prolong hospital stay in children, warranting closer monitoring and potential targeted interventions.

documents.title: Bladder Overactivity and Post-Botulinum Toxin Telemonitoring: a Randomized, Controlled, Superiority Trial
documents.summary: Bladder overactivity (BO) is a common condition affecting quality of life, particularly in patients with neurogenic bladder dysfunction. Botulinum toxin (BoNT) injections are used to treat refractory cases, but post-injection monitoring remains challenging. This study evaluated the efficacy of telemonitoring in managing BO after BoNT injection. A total of 80 patients with neurogenic bladder and refractory BO were randomized to either telemonitoring (n=40) or standard care (n=40). The telemonitoring group received weekly video consultations and symptom tracking via a mobile app. Primary outcomes included changes in overactive bladder symptom score (OABSS) and urinary incontinence episodes at 12 weeks. Results showed a significant improvement in OABSS (mean reduction: 4.2 vs. 2.1, p<0.001) and a greater reduction in incontinence episodes (−6.3 vs. −3.

documents.title: Sintilimab Plus Chemotherapy as Neoadjuvant and Adjuvant Treatment for Locally Advanced Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma: A Multicenter, Open-label, Randomized, Phase III Clinical Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated neoadjuvant and adjuvant sintilimab (anti-PD-1 antibody) combined with chemotherapy in patients with locally advanced oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC). A total of 108 patients were randomized to either sintilimab plus chemotherapy or chemotherapy alone. The primary endpoint was pathological complete response (pCR) rate. The sintilimab group achieved a significantly higher pCR rate (47.2% vs. 20.0%, p=0.001), with improved event-free survival (EFS) and overall survival (OS) trends. Adverse events were manageable, with no new safety signals.

documents.title: A Phase II Study of Nivolumab Plus Relatlimab 360 mg/360 mg and Gemcitabine/Cisplatin as First-Line Treatment in Patients With Advanced Biliary Tract Cancer.(NOBLE）
documents.summary: The study evaluated the combination of nivolumab plus relatlimab (360 mg/360 mg) with gemcitabine/cisplatin as first-line therapy in patients with advanced biliary tract cancer (BTC). The primary endpoint was objective response rate (ORR), with secondary endpoints including progression-free survival (PFS), overall survival (OS), and safety. The ORR was 42.9% (95% CI: 28.1–58.8), with a median PFS of 7.4 months (95% CI: 5.5–9.3) and median OS of 15.5 months (95% CI: 11.9–19.1). Grade 3–4 treatment-related adverse events occurred in 42.9% of patients, with immune-related adverse events (irAEs) reported in 18.6%. The most
