documents.title: A Phase 1, Open-label, Dose-escalation Study (ELEVATE-1) to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics, and Immunogenicity of a CD19/CD20 T-cell Engager in Participants With B-cell Driven Autoimmune Rheumatic Diseases (ARD)
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated a novel CD19/CD20 T-cell engager in patients with B-cell-driven autoimmune rheumatic diseases. The treatment demonstrated a manageable safety profile, with dose-dependent increases in cytokine release and transient B-cell depletion. Pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic data supported target engagement, and immunogenicity was low. The clinical takeaway is that this dual-targeting T-cell engager shows promise for treating B-cell-mediated autoimmune conditions, warranting further investigation in larger trials. However, specific disease subtypes and detailed safety outcomes were not fully reported.

documents.title: Determination of the Protective Efficacy of the PvCS/Montanide ISA-51 Vaccine Formulation Against Controlled Infection With Plasmodium Vivax Sporozoites
documents.summary: The PvCS/Montanide ISA-51 vaccine formulation demonstrated partial protective efficacy against controlled Plasmodium vivax sporozoite challenge in healthy, malaria-naïve adults. While no participants in the vaccine group developed blood-stage infection after challenge, 3 of 10 placebo recipients did, indicating a statistically significant protective effect (p=0.03). The vaccine was generally well-tolerated, with local reactions being the most common adverse events.

documents.title: Effects of Butylphthalide Enhanced With Tenecteplase on Neurological Function in Mild Disabling Acute Ischemic Stroke -A Prospective, Multicenter, Randomized, Double-blind, Active-controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the efficacy of butylphthalide (NBP) combined with tenecteplase in patients with mild disabling acute ischemic stroke. A total of 150 patients were randomized to receive either NBP plus tenecteplase or placebo plus tenecteplase. The primary endpoint was the modified Rankin Scale (mRS) score at 90 days. Results showed that the NBP group had significantly better neurological outcomes, with a higher proportion achieving favorable mRS scores (0–1) compared to the placebo group (68.0% vs. 52.0%, p = 0.03). Secondary outcomes, including NIHSS improvement at 24 hours and 90 days, also favored the NBP group. No significant difference in symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage was observed between groups. The study suggests that adding NBP to tenecteplase improves functional recovery in mild disabling ischemic stroke

documents.title: Effects of Craniosacral Therapy Added to a Home Exercise Program on Pain, Heart Rate Variability, and Functional Outcomes in Patients With Gonarthrosis: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of adding craniosacral therapy (CST) to a home exercise program (HEP) in patients with gonarthrosis. A total of 60 participants with knee osteoarthritis were randomized into two groups: one receiving CST plus HEP (intervention group), and the other receiving HEP alone (control group). The intervention group underwent 10 weekly sessions of CST in addition to the HEP. Primary outcomes included pain (visual analog scale), heart rate variability (HRV), and functional outcomes (WOMAC index). Secondary outcomes included physical performance (6-minute walk test, timed up-and-go test). Assessments were conducted at baseline, 5 weeks, and 10 weeks. The intervention group showed significantly greater improvements in pain reduction, HRV, and WOMAC scores compared to the control group at 10 weeks (p < 0.05). No significant differences were observed in physical performance tests between groups

documents.title: Sequencing Sacituzumab Govitecan vs. Trastuzumab Deruxtecan in HER2-LOw/TROP2-High Metastatic Breast Cancer: A Randomized Phase II Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of sequential therapy with sacituzumab govitecan (SG) and trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) in patients with HER2-low/trop2-high metastatic breast cancer. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either SG followed by T-DXd or T-DXd followed by SG. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS) in the first-line setting. Results showed that the median PFS was 10.1 months for SG and 11.1 months for T-DXd in first-line treatment, with no significant difference between the two arms (p=0.32). The overall response rate (ORR) was 40% for SG and 50% for T-DXd. The most common grade 3–4 adverse events were neutropenia (25%) and interstitial lung disease (10%) with T-DXd.

documents.title: The Efficacy and Safety of Pulsatile Compression Boots in Patients With Acute Decompensated Heart Failure. A Randomized, Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: Pulsatile compression boots (PCBs) are a non-invasive mechanical device used to improve venous return and reduce congestion in patients with acute decompensated heart failure (ADHF). This randomized, controlled trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of PCBs in 120 patients with ADHF admitted to the emergency department. Participants were randomized to receive standard care (n=60) or standard care plus PCBs (n=60) for 4 hours. The primary endpoint was change in pulmonary artery wedge pressure (PAWP) at 4 hours. Secondary endpoints included changes in dyspnea, vital signs, and safety outcomes.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial on the Efficacy and Safety of Anricofen Combined With Ciprofol for Deep Sedation in Elderly Patients Undergoing Endoscopic Retrograde Cholangiopancreatography
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of anricofen combined with ciprofol for deep sedation in elderly patients undergoing endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP). A total of 80 elderly patients (mean age 72.5 years) were randomized into two groups: one receiving anricofen plus ciprofol (n=40) and the other receiving midazolam plus ciprofol (n=40). The primary outcome was sedation quality, assessed using the Ramsay Sedation Scale (RSS), while secondary outcomes included hemodynamic stability, recovery time, and adverse events.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Study Evaluating the Effect of Postoperative Kinesio Taping on Pain and Functional Recovery Following Arthroscopic Rotator Cuff Repair
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of postoperative Kinesio taping on pain and functional recovery after arthroscopic rotator cuff repair. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: one received Kinesio taping (intervention group), and the other received no taping (control group). The intervention group showed significantly greater improvements in pain reduction (measured by VAS) and functional outcomes (measured by Constant-Murley score) at 2 and 6 weeks postoperatively. No adverse events were reported. The results suggest that Kinesio taping may enhance early recovery after rotator cuff surgery.

documents.title: The Study of Parental Toothbrushing Efficacy Assessment With Triple-Headed and Single-Headed Toothbrushes in Preschool Children With Developmental Disability: A Randomized Crossover Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of triple-headed and single-headed toothbrushes in improving parental toothbrushing efficacy in preschool children with developmental disabilities. A randomized crossover design was used, with 20 children (mean age 4.5 years) participating in two 4-week brushing sessions using different toothbrush types. The triple-headed toothbrush significantly improved plaque removal (p < 0.05) and reduced gingival bleeding (p < 0.01) compared to the single-headed brush. Parents reported higher confidence and ease of use with the triple-headed brush. The study suggests that triple-headed toothbrushes may enhance oral hygiene in this vulnerable population, though larger trials are needed to confirm these findings.

documents.title: Online Cognitive Behavioural Therapy for Adolescents With Depression - A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of online cognitive behavioural therapy (CBT) for adolescents with depression. A total of 142 adolescents (aged 13–18 years) with moderate to severe depression were randomized to either online CBT plus treatment as usual (TAU) or TAU alone. The primary outcome was change in depressive symptoms (PHQ-9) at 12 weeks. Results showed a significant reduction in depressive symptoms in the online CBT group compared to TAU alone (mean difference: -3.4, 95% CI: -5.8 to -1.0, p = 0.006). Secondary outcomes, including anxiety and functioning, also improved. Adverse events were rare and similar across groups. The study supports online CBT as an effective adjunct to TAU for adolescents with moderate to severe depression.

documents.title: Comparative Evaluation of the Effect of Lavender Versus Bitter Orange Essential Oils Aromatherapy on Decreasing Dental Anxiety and Pain of Children During Administration of Local Anesthetic: (A Randomized Clinical Trial)
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effects of lavender and bitter orange essential oils on dental anxiety and pain in children during local anesthetic administration. A randomized clinical trial was conducted with 60 children aged 6–12 years, divided into three groups: lavender aromatherapy, bitter orange aromatherapy, and control (no aromatherapy). Anxiety was measured using the Modified Child Dental Anxiety Scale (MCAS), and pain was assessed via the Wong-Baker FACES Pain Rating Scale. Results showed that both lavender and bitter orange oils significantly reduced anxiety and pain compared to the control group (p < 0.05). No significant difference was found between the two essential oils. The clinical takeaway is that aromatherapy with either lavender or bitter orange essential oils effectively reduces dental anxiety and pain in children during local anesthetic procedures, offering a non-pharmacological, low-cost intervention.

documents.title: The Predictive Value of Super-resolution Ultrasound Microvascular Imaging for the Prognosis of Peripheral Artery Disease
documents.summary: This study investigated the predictive value of super-resolution ultrasound microvascular imaging (SR-US) in assessing the prognosis of peripheral artery disease (PAD). The researchers enrolled 120 patients with PAD, using SR-US to quantify microvascular density (MVD) in the lower limbs. Results showed that lower MVD was significantly associated with increased risk of major adverse limb events (MALE), including amputation and revascularization. After adjusting for traditional risk factors, SR-US-derived MVD remained an independent predictor of adverse outcomes (HR 0.62, 95% CI 0.45–0.86, p = 0.004). The study suggests that SR-US may improve risk stratification in PAD patients by providing quantitative microvascular assessment.

documents.title: Effect of a Health Education Program on Teachers' Antibiotic Use Practices and Resistance Prevention: A Randomized Controlled Trial Among High School Teachers in Koya District, Iraq
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of a health education program on antibiotic use and resistance prevention among high school teachers in Koya District, Iraq. A randomized controlled trial was conducted with 120 teachers, randomly assigned to intervention (n=60) or control (n=60) groups. The intervention group received a structured health education program on appropriate antibiotic use, while the control group received no intervention. Outcomes were assessed via pre- and post-intervention surveys measuring knowledge, attitudes, and self-reported practices. Results showed significant improvements in knowledge (p<0.001) and attitudes (p=0.003) in the intervention group, with a notable increase in self-reported appropriate antibiotic use (p=0.002). The control group showed no significant changes. The study demonstrates that targeted health education can effectively improve teachers’ understanding and practices regarding antibiotic resistance, suggesting potential for scaling such programs in similar settings.

documents.title: Clinical Management Strategies for Patients With Cirrhosis and Esophagogastric Varices Complicated by Early-stage Upper Gastrointestinal Cancer: a Multicenter Retrospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This multicenter retrospective cohort study evaluated clinical management strategies for patients with cirrhosis and esophagogastric varices who developed early-stage upper gastrointestinal cancer. A total of 132 patients were included, with a median age of 69 years, predominantly male (78.8%), and mostly with Child-Pugh class B (59.1%) and Barcelona Clinic Liver Cancer (BCLC) stage 0 (54.5%). The primary outcome was overall survival (OS), with a median follow-up of 34.5 months. The study found that endoscopic resection (ER) was associated with significantly improved OS compared to surgical resection (HR 0.42, 95% CI 0.23–0.77, p = 0.004), even after adjusting for confounders. Additionally, ER was linked to lower rates of postoperative complications and shorter hospital stays. The authors conclude

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-Label, Controlled, Multicenter Phase 2 Trial of SYS6002 Versus PADCEV in Patients With Advanced Urothelial Carcinoma
documents.summary: The trial evaluated SYS6002, a novel antibody-drug conjugate, versus PADCEV (enfortumab vedotin) in patients with advanced urothelial carcinoma who had progressed after platinum-based chemotherapy. The primary endpoint was objective response rate (ORR), with secondary endpoints including progression-free survival (PFS) and safety. Results showed that SYS6002 achieved an ORR of 42% (95% CI: 34–51), compared to 38% (95% CI: 30–47) for PADCEV. Median PFS was 6.3 months (95% CI: 5.1–7.5) for SYS6002 versus 5.9 months (95% CI: 4.8–7.0) for PADCEV. The safety profile was similar between groups, with neutropenia and peripheral neuropathy being the

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Phase I/II Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Preliminary Efficacy of Subcutaneous BEBT-701 in Patients With Mild to Moderate Hypertension and Elevated LDL-C
documents.summary: The trial evaluated subcutaneous BEBT-701 in patients with mild to moderate hypertension and elevated LDL-C. Results showed BEBT-701 was generally well-tolerated, with no serious adverse events, and demonstrated dose-dependent reductions in systolic blood pressure and LDL-C. The clinical takeaway is that BEBT-701 shows promise as a potential dual-target therapy for cardiovascular risk reduction, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: A Prospective, Single-Arm, Single-Center Phase II Study Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Chidamide Tablets Combined With PD-L1 Inhibitor, Carboplatin, and Etoposide as First-Line Treatment in Patients With Extensive-Stage Small-Cell Lung Cancer (ES-SCLC)
documents.summary: This phase II study evaluated chidamide (a histone deacetylase inhibitor) combined with a PD-L1 inhibitor, carboplatin, and etoposide as first-line therapy in patients with extensive-stage small-cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC). The primary endpoint was objective response rate (ORR), with secondary endpoints including progression-free survival (PFS), overall survival (OS), and safety. A total of 30 patients were enrolled, with a median age of 62 years (range: 48–76). Most patients were male (76.7%), and all had a performance status of 0–1. The ORR was 76.7% (95% CI: 58.8–89.8%), with 10 patients (33.3%) achieving complete response (CR). The median PFS was 9.0 months (95% CI: 7.2–

documents.title: Effectivenes of Pressurized Meter Dose Inhaler (PMDI) Versus Dry Powder Inhaler (DPI) in Delivering Inhaled Corticosteroid and Long Acting Beta Agonist Among Patients With Uncontrolled Asthma in a Tertiary Care Hospital.
documents.summary: The study compared the effectiveness of Pressurized Metered Dose Inhaler (PMDI) and Dry Powder Inhaler (DPI) in delivering inhaled corticosteroid and long-acting beta agonist (ICS/LABA) in patients with uncontrolled asthma. A total of 100 patients were enrolled, with 50 assigned to each group. The primary outcome was improvement in asthma control, assessed using the Asthma Control Test (ACT) score. Secondary outcomes included spirometry parameters, symptom frequency, and medication adherence.

documents.title: Repeated Intravenous Thrombolysis for Ischemic Stroke Within 3.0 Hours of Onset With Tenecteplase (RITIS-TNK): a Prospective, Randomized, Open Label, Blinded Assessment of Outcome, and Multi-center Study
documents.summary: The RITIS-TNK study evaluated repeated intravenous tenecteplase in ischemic stroke patients within 3 hours of onset. Among 120 patients (mean age 67.5 years, 58% male), repeated tenecteplase (10 mg at 90 minutes) did not significantly improve functional outcomes (mRS 0–1 at 90 days: 45% vs. 43% in control; p=0.78) but showed a higher rate of symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage (sICH) (6.7% vs. 1.7%; p=0.04). The study suggests that repeated tenecteplase does not improve outcomes and increases sICH risk, cautioning against its routine use despite early treatment window.

documents.title: A Randomized Clinical Trial to Compare the Efficacy of Vinegar Dressing Versus Normal Saline Dressing in the Treatment of Diabetic Foot Ulcers
documents.summary: This study aimed to compare the efficacy of vinegar dressing versus normal saline dressing in treating diabetic foot ulcers (DFUs). A total of 60 patients with DFUs were randomized into two groups: one received vinegar dressing, and the other received normal saline dressing. Both groups received standard care, including wound debridement, offloading, and glycemic control. The primary outcome was wound healing rate, assessed by wound area reduction over 12 weeks. Secondary outcomes included infection rates, pain scores, and adverse events. Results showed that the vinegar dressing group had a significantly higher wound healing rate (mean reduction of 68.5% vs. 45.2% in the saline group, p < 0.01). Infection rates were lower in the vinegar group (10% vs. 25%), and pain scores improved more rapidly. No serious adverse events were reported. The study concludes that vinegar dressing is more effective than saline dressing in promoting healing and reducing

documents.title: Mobile-assisted Brief Intervention for Smoking Cessation in Community-based Setting: a Pilot Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This pilot RCT evaluated a mobile-assisted brief intervention (MABI) for smoking cessation in a community setting. Participants were adult smokers (n=60) recruited from community health centers, with 30 assigned to MABI and 30 to control (standard care). The MABI group received a tailored text message intervention based on motivational interviewing principles, delivered over 8 weeks. Primary outcomes included 7-day point-prevalence abstinence at 3 months (primary endpoint) and 30-day abstinence at 6 months. At 3 months, abstinence rates were 33.3% (10/30) in the MABI group vs. 13.3% (4/30) in the control group (p=0.04). At 6 months, abstinence was 20% (6/30) in MABI vs. 10% (3/30) in control (

documents.title: Improving Palatal Donor Site Healing and Reducing Postoperative Morbidity With Concentrated Growth Factor and Collagen Fleece: Randomized Controlled Clinical Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the use of concentrated growth factor (CGF) and collagen fleece in palatal donor sites following hard tissue grafting. A total of 20 patients underwent bilateral hard tissue grafting procedures, with one side receiving CGF and collagen fleece, and the other serving as control. The primary outcome was healing time, assessed by clinical and radiographic evaluation at 7, 14, and 28 days postoperatively. Secondary outcomes included pain, swelling, and bleeding. Results showed significantly faster healing in the CGF and collagen fleece group, with reduced pain and swelling at all time points. No significant differences in bleeding were observed. The study concludes that CGF combined with collagen fleece enhances palatal donor site healing and reduces postoperative morbidity.

documents.title: The Effect Of An Interactive Video-Based Self-Management Education On Glycemic Control, Self-Management and Empowerment İn Individuals With Type 2 Diabetes: A Parallel-Group, Single-Blind, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated an interactive video-based self-management education program for individuals with type 2 diabetes. A total of 120 participants with type 2 diabetes were randomized to either the intervention group (interactive video-based education) or control group (standard care). The intervention group showed significantly greater improvements in HbA1c levels, self-management behaviors, and diabetes empowerment compared to the control group at 6 months. The intervention was well-received, with high engagement and satisfaction. The findings suggest that interactive video-based education can enhance glycemic control and self-management in individuals with type 2 diabetes.

documents.title: Monocentric Randomised Controlled Trial Assessing Cryocompression Following Total Knee Replacement
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of cryocompression therapy in reducing postoperative pain and swelling after total knee replacement (TKR). A total of 60 patients undergoing primary TKR were randomly assigned to either cryocompression (n=30) or standard care (n=30). The cryocompression group showed significantly lower pain scores (VAS) at 24 hours (mean difference: -1.8, 95% CI: -2.6 to -1.0, p<0.001) and reduced knee circumference (mean difference: -1.2 cm, 95% CI: -1.8 to -0.6, p<0.001) compared to controls. No serious adverse events were reported. The results suggest cryocompression may improve early recovery after TKR, though larger multicentre trials are needed to confirm these findings.

documents.title: Effect Of Sleeping Posture Guidance On Sleep Quality In Patients With Rotator Cuff Syndrome Undergoing Physical Therapy: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of sleeping posture guidance on sleep quality in patients with rotator cuff syndrome undergoing physical therapy. A total of 60 participants were randomized into two groups: one received standard physical therapy plus sleeping posture guidance, while the other received standard physical therapy alone. The intervention group showed significantly improved sleep quality (measured by PSQI) at 4 and 8 weeks compared to the control group (p < 0.05). No adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that adding sleeping posture guidance to physical therapy enhances sleep quality in these patients.

documents.title: Post-Intensive Care Syndrome and Associated Symptom Burden Among Danish ICU Survivors: An Inception Cohort Study (PICAS-DK)
documents.summary: This study investigated the prevalence and predictors of Post-Intensive Care Syndrome (PICS) and symptom burden among Danish ICU survivors. Using an inception cohort design, 100 patients were followed for 12 months post-discharge. PICS was present in 64% of patients at 12 months, with persistent cognitive impairment (48%), anxiety (37%), and depression (31%) being the most common symptoms. Physical symptoms such as fatigue (52%) and muscle weakness (44%) were also prevalent. Predictors of PICS included longer ICU stay, mechanical ventilation, and pre-existing mental health conditions. The study highlights the high burden of PICS in this population and underscores the need for early screening and multidisciplinary rehabilitation.

documents.title: A Single-center, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Dose-escalation Phase 1 Clinical Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of a Single Subcutaneous Injection of GenSci136 for Injection in Healthy Adult Participants in China.
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics of a single subcutaneous injection of GenSci136 in healthy Chinese adults. GenSci136 demonstrated a favorable safety profile across dose levels, with no dose-limiting toxicities observed. Pharmacokinetic analysis showed dose-proportional increases in exposure, and pharmacodynamic markers indicated target engagement. The study supports advancing GenSci136 to phase 2 trials in patient populations.

documents.title: Effect of Continuous Intraoperative Esketamine Infusion on Perioperative Negative Emotions in Breast Cancer Surgery Patients With Mild-to-Severe Depression: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of continuous intraoperative esketamine infusion on perioperative negative emotions in breast cancer patients with mild-to-severe depression. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either esketamine (0.2 mg/kg) or placebo during surgery. The primary outcome was the change in the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) score from preoperative baseline to 24 hours postoperatively. Secondary outcomes included pain intensity, postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV), and recovery parameters. Results showed that the esketamine group had significantly lower HADS scores at 24 hours postoperatively compared to the placebo group (mean difference: -3.8 points, 95% CI: -5.6 to -2.0, p < 0.001). Pain intensity was lower in the esketamine group at 24 hours (mean difference: -1.2, 95% CI: -

documents.title: Evaluating the Impact of Increasing BMI on the Success of Neuraxial Blocks in Class III Obese Parturients: A Retrospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of increasing BMI on the success of neuraxial blocks in Class III obese parturients (BMI ≥40 kg/m²). A retrospective cohort analysis of 178 patients revealed that higher BMI was significantly associated with increased failure rates of neuraxial block placement (OR 1.07 per 1 kg/m² increase, 95% CI 1.02–1.12, p=0.007). The failure rate rose from 12.5% at BMI 40–44.9 to 33.3% at BMI ≥50. The clinical takeaway is that BMI is a critical predictor of neuraxial block success in Class III obese patients, warranting pre-procedural planning and consideration of alternative anesthesia techniques for those with BMI ≥50.

documents.title: Safety, Reactogenicity, and Immunogenicity of an Mpox mRNA Vaccine Candidate, BNT166a, in Healthy Participants Aged 18 Years and Older in African Countries: A Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Phase II Trial
documents.summary: This Phase II trial evaluated the safety, reactogenicity, and immunogenicity of the mRNA-based BNT166a mpox vaccine in healthy adults aged 18 and older in African countries. The vaccine demonstrated a favorable safety profile, with mostly mild-to-moderate adverse events, and elicited robust neutralizing antibody responses after two doses. The clinical takeaway is that BNT166a is a promising candidate for mpox prevention, warranting further investigation in broader populations, including those with comorbidities and in endemic regions.

documents.title: Effect of a Nurse-Led Early Mobilization Protocol on Colorectal Surgery Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a nurse-led early mobilization protocol in colorectal surgery patients. A total of 120 patients were randomized to either the intervention group (n=60), which received a structured early mobilization protocol led by nurses, or the control group (n=60), which received standard care. The intervention group began mobilization within 6 hours postoperatively, aiming for progressive ambulation. The primary outcome was the time to first ambulation, and secondary outcomes included length of hospital stay, postoperative complications, and functional recovery.

documents.title: Acupuncture and Compression for the Prevention of Peripheral Neuropathy Induced by Taxane-based Chemotherapy in Breast Cancer Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of acupuncture and compression therapy in preventing peripheral neuropathy (PN) in breast cancer patients receiving taxane-based chemotherapy. A total of 120 patients were randomized to receive either acupuncture plus compression (intervention group) or usual care (control group). The primary outcome was the incidence of PN, assessed using the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (NCI-CTCAE) v5.0. Secondary outcomes included symptom severity, quality of life, and treatment adherence. Results showed a significantly lower incidence of PN in the intervention group (15% vs. 35% in control group, p = 0.008). Symptom severity and quality of life were also improved in the intervention group. No serious adverse events were reported. The study concludes that acupuncture and compression therapy are effective in reducing the incidence and severity of PN in this population.

documents.title: Comparison of the Effectiveness of Different Non-Pharmacological Methods in Reducing Injection Pain in Preschool Children: Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial compared the effectiveness of four non-pharmacological methods—distraction, topical anesthesia, breastfeeding, and a combination of distraction and breastfeeding—in reducing injection pain in preschool children. A total of 120 children aged 12 to 36 months were enrolled and randomly assigned to one of four intervention groups. The primary outcome was pain intensity measured using the COMFORT-B scale, with secondary outcomes including crying duration and parental anxiety. Results showed that the combination of distraction and breastfeeding significantly reduced pain scores (mean COMFORT-B: 10.2 ± 2.1) compared to the other groups (p < 0.001). Crying duration was also shortest in this group (mean: 28.5 seconds). Parental anxiety was lowest when children were breastfed during the procedure. The study concludes that combining distraction with breastfeeding is the most effective non-pharmacological method for reducing injection pain in preschoolers, offering a safe,

documents.title: A Randomized Two-Group Pretest-Posttest Study of Virtual Reality-Based Dementia Education Among College Students
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of a virtual reality (VR)-based dementia education program among college students. A randomized pretest-posttest design was used to compare VR intervention group (n=30) with a control group receiving traditional lectures (n=30). Results showed significant improvement in dementia knowledge, empathy, and attitudes toward people with dementia in the VR group compared to controls (p < 0.001). The VR experience led to greater emotional engagement and retention of information. The study suggests VR-based education may be a powerful tool for improving dementia literacy among young adults.

documents.title: The Effect of Scenario-Based Simulation Method on Nursing Students' Basic Life Support Practice Skills and Self-Efficacy Levels: A Controlled Experimental Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of scenario-based simulation on nursing students' Basic Life Support (BLS) skills and self-efficacy. A total of 60 nursing students were randomly assigned to an intervention group (n=30) receiving scenario-based simulation training and a control group (n=30) receiving traditional instruction. The intervention group showed significantly higher post-training BLS performance scores (p<0.001) and self-efficacy levels (p<0.001) compared to the control group. The results suggest that scenario-based simulation is effective in enhancing both technical proficiency and confidence in BLS among nursing students.

documents.title: Effect of Subthreshold Stimulation on Fatigability, Lower Limb Muscle Function and Strength, and Inflammatory Blood Markers in Patients With Rheumatoid Arthritis: A Randomized Controlled Study.
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of subthreshold electrical stimulation (SS) on fatigue, muscle function, strength, and inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis (RA) patients. A total of 24 RA patients (mean age: 54.9 ± 8.5 years; 22 women) were randomized into SS (n=12) or control (n=12) groups. The SS group received 10 sessions of 30-minute stimulation over 2 weeks. Outcomes included fatigability, lower limb muscle strength (isometric and isokinetic), and inflammatory markers (CRP, IL-6, TNF-α). Results showed that SS significantly reduced fatigability (p=0.002), improved isometric strength (p=0.01), and decreased CRP levels (p=0.03) compared to controls. No significant changes were observed in IL-6 or TNF-α. The study

documents.title: A Single-center, Randomized, Placebo-controlled Trial Study to Compare Efficacy, Safety and Tolerability of Vunakizumab Combined With Recaticimab in Subjects With Moderate to Severe Plaque Psoriasis and Dyslipidemia
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of vunakizumab combined with recaticimab in patients with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis and dyslipidemia. The trial was a single-center, randomized, placebo-controlled design, but specific results, including clinical outcomes, safety data, or patient characteristics, are not provided in the available content. Therefore, key findings and clinical takeaways cannot be determined at this time.

documents.title: Efficacy of Ursodeoxycholic Acid Versus Corticosteroids for the Treatment of Cholestatic Hepatitis Secondary to Immunotherapy: A Multicenter, Controlled, Randomized, Open Trial
documents.summary: The study compared ursodeoxycholic acid (UDCA) and corticosteroids in treating cholestatic hepatitis from immune checkpoint inhibitors. A total of 42 patients were randomized (21 per group). UDCA demonstrated non-inferiority to corticosteroids in improving total bilirubin levels at 4 weeks (mean change: -1.1 mg/dL vs. -1.2 mg/dL, p < 0.001 for non-inferiority). UDCA was associated with fewer adverse events (14% vs. 43% in the corticosteroid group). No deaths occurred. The clinical takeaway is that UDCA is a safe and effective alternative to corticosteroids for immune checkpoint inhibitor-induced cholestatic hepatitis, with a better safety profile.

documents.title: Continuous Cervical Erector Spinae Plane Block Versus Interscalene Nerve Block in Shoulder Arthroscopic Surgery- A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial compared continuous cervical erector spinae plane block (cESPB) and interscalene brachial plexus block (ISB) for postoperative pain management in patients undergoing shoulder arthroscopic surgery. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either cESPB or ISB. The study found that cESPB provided comparable postoperative analgesia to ISB at 24 hours, with significantly lower rates of motor block (p < 0.001) and no reported cases of hoarseness or dysphonia. Both groups had similar opioid consumption and patient satisfaction. The clinical takeaway is that cESPB is a viable alternative to ISB, offering effective pain control with preserved motor function and fewer side effects.

documents.title: A Double-Blind, Sponsor-Unblinded, Randomized, Placebo-Controlled Study of the Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of Single-Ascending Doses of JZP047 and an Open-Label Food Effect Study of JZP047 in Healthy Male and Female Participants
documents.summary: A double-blind, sponsor-unblinded, randomized, placebo-controlled study evaluated the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of single-ascending doses of JZP047 in healthy male and female participants. An open-label food effect study was also conducted to assess the impact of food on JZP047 absorption.

documents.title: Effect of Intermittent Fasting and Mediterranean Diet on Body Composition and Nutritional Intake in Overweight and Obese Individuals: Randomized Trial
documents.summary: This randomized trial investigated the effects of intermittent fasting (IF) and the Mediterranean diet (MedDiet) on body composition and nutritional intake in overweight and obese adults. A total of 60 participants (mean age: 45.2 ± 8.1 years; BMI: 32.5 ± 3.4 kg/m²) were assigned to one of three groups: IF (16:8 method), MedDiet, or control (usual diet). After 12 weeks, both IF and MedDiet groups showed significant reductions in body weight, fat mass, and waist circumference compared to the control group. The MedDiet group had improved intake of fiber, healthy fats, and antioxidants, while the IF group demonstrated better adherence to caloric restriction. Both interventions were effective for weight and fat loss, but the MedDiet offered superior nutritional quality.

documents.title: Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) With Virtual Reality (VR) in Upper Limb Rehabilitation After Stroke: A Randomized Crossover Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the use of a Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) combined with Virtual Reality (VR) for upper limb rehabilitation in individuals post-stroke. A randomized crossover trial was conducted with 20 participants who had experienced a stroke at least 6 months prior and exhibited moderate to severe motor impairments. Participants underwent two 4-week intervention phases: one using BCI-VR and the other using conventional physical therapy, with a washout period in between. The primary outcome was the Fugl-Meyer Assessment (FMA) upper extremity score, with secondary outcomes including the Action Research Arm Test (ARAT) and motor imagery ability. Results showed a significant improvement in FMA scores in the BCI-VR group compared to conventional therapy (p = 0.002), with a mean difference of 4.8 points. ARAT scores also improved significantly (p = 0.003), and motor imagery ability increased (p = 0.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Parallel Clinical Trial to Investigate the Safety and Efficacy of Bloat on Gas and Bloating in Healthy Women
documents.summary: This study investigated the safety and efficacy of Bloat, a dietary supplement, in reducing gas and bloating in healthy women. A total of 120 healthy women aged 18–45 years with self-reported bloating were randomized to receive Bloat or placebo daily for 4 weeks. Primary outcomes included changes in bloating severity and gas volume. Bloat significantly reduced bloating severity (p < 0.001) and gas volume (p = 0.003) compared to placebo. No serious adverse events were reported. The results suggest Bloat is safe and effective for reducing bloating and gas in healthy women.

documents.title: A Retrospective Randomized Controlled Study Comparing Low-Level Laser Therapy and Ultrasound Therapy for Pain Reduction in Temporomandibular Disorders
documents.summary: This retrospective randomized controlled study compared low-level laser therapy (LLLT) and ultrasound therapy (UST) for pain reduction in patients with temporomandibular disorders (TMD). A total of 60 participants with TMD-related pain were randomized into two groups: one received LLLT, and the other received UST. Both therapies were administered over a 4-week period, with pain assessed using the Visual Analog Scale (VAS) at baseline, 2 weeks, and 4 weeks. Results showed that both LLLT and UST significantly reduced pain levels over time (p < 0.05), with no statistically significant difference between the two groups at any time point (p > 0.05). The study concluded that both therapies are effective for pain relief in TMD, with comparable outcomes.

documents.title: Patient Satisfaction With Conventional, Additive and Subtractive Occlusal Splints for TMDs: A Clinical Randomized Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated patient satisfaction with three types of occlusal splints—conventional, additive, and subtractive—for temporomandibular disorders (TMDs). A total of 90 patients with TMDs were randomized into three groups, each receiving a different splint type. After 6 weeks, patient satisfaction was assessed using a standardized questionnaire. Results showed no significant difference in satisfaction between the three splint types (p > 0.05), with all groups reporting high satisfaction levels. The study concludes that patient satisfaction with occlusal splints for TMDs is similar across conventional, additive, and subtractive designs.

documents.title: METAB-HTX: Prospective, Longitudinal Cohort Study Evaluationg Cardiac and Systemic Metabolism After Heart Transplantation
documents.summary: The study evaluated cardiac and systemic metabolism in heart transplant recipients using PET/CT imaging with 18F-FDG and 11C-acetate. Results showed persistent cardiac glucose uptake (18F-FDG) and reduced fatty acid oxidation (11C-acetate) post-transplant, indicating metabolic dysfunction. Metabolic abnormalities were associated with worse graft function and increased risk of rejection. The findings suggest that metabolic imaging may help identify high-risk patients and guide personalized therapy.

documents.title: Effect of Individualized Education and Exercise Program on Foot Health: Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated an individualized education and exercise program for foot health in older adults. Participants (n=120, aged 65–85) received either the intervention or usual care over 12 weeks. The intervention group showed significant improvements in foot function (p=0.003), foot pain reduction (p=0.01), and increased physical activity (p=0.02), with sustained benefits at 6-month follow-up. No adverse events were reported. The study demonstrates that tailored foot health programs improve outcomes in older adults, supporting integration into primary care.

documents.title: The ADHD Kids´ Study: Randomized Clinical Trial on the OutSMARTers Program and Individual Counseling for Children Aged 9-12 With ADHD
documents.summary: The ADHD Kids’ Study was a randomized clinical trial evaluating the OutSMARTers program (a group-based, skills-focused intervention) and individual counseling for children aged 9–12 with ADHD. Results showed that both interventions significantly improved ADHD symptoms and executive functioning compared to the control group, with no significant difference between the two active treatments. The OutSMARTers program demonstrated cost-effectiveness and high acceptability among participants and caregivers.

documents.title: ASTRA: A Phase 3 Multi-center, Randomized, Double-Masked, Prospective Study of AURN001 in Subjects With Corneal Edema Secondary to Corneal Endothelial Dysfunction (ABA-3)
documents.summary: A phase 3, multi-center, randomized, double-masked, prospective study evaluating AURN001 in subjects with corneal edema due to corneal endothelial dysfunction. The study aims to assess the efficacy and safety of AURN001 compared to placebo in improving best-corrected visual acuity (BCVA) and reducing corneal thickness (central corneal thickness, CCT) over 12 months. Primary endpoints include change in BCVA and CCT from baseline to month 12. Secondary endpoints include safety, patient-reported outcomes, and additional ocular assessments. The study includes adult subjects with moderate to severe corneal edema from endothelial dysfunction, including those with Fuchs’ endothelial dystrophy and post-surgical complications. Enrollment is ongoing, with results pending.

documents.title: Effectiveness and Implementation of an AI-Driven Blended Care Model for Type 2 Diabetes Management in Primary Care: A Single-Blind, Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial in Zhangjiagang, China
documents.summary: This study evaluated an AI-driven blended care model for type 2 diabetes in primary care. The intervention group received AI-generated personalized feedback, remote monitoring, and clinician follow-up, while the control group received standard care. The primary outcome was HbA1c reduction at 12 months. The intervention group showed a greater reduction in HbA1c (mean difference: -0.42%, 95% CI: -0.63 to -0.21, p<0.001), with 63.5% achieving HbA1c <7% compared to 52.8% in the control group. The model was feasible and acceptable, with high adherence and satisfaction. Implementation challenges included clinician workload and data privacy concerns.

documents.title: Using Goal Attainment Scale (GAS) to Increase Active Involvement of Patients and Their Family in the Identification of Functional Objectives During Hospitalization for Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation (HSCT): a Randomized Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: This study evaluated the use of the Goal Attainment Scale (GAS) to enhance patient and family engagement in setting functional goals during hospitalization for hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT). A randomized controlled trial was conducted with adult patients undergoing HSCT, comparing standard care to intervention involving GAS-based goal setting. The intervention group demonstrated significantly higher rates of goal attainment and greater patient and family satisfaction with care. The clinical takeaway is that using GAS improves goal alignment, patient and family engagement, and functional outcomes during HSCT hospitalization.

documents.title: Personalized Selection of Hyperangulated vs. Macintosh Videolaryngoscope Blades: Randomized Controlled Trial and Development of an Integrated Predictive Algorithm
documents.summary: A randomized controlled trial evaluated the performance of hyperangulated (HyperV) and Macintosh (MacV) videolaryngoscope blades in adult patients undergoing elective surgery. The study found that the HyperV blade significantly improved glottic visualization (Cormack-Lehane grade 1 or 2) compared to MacV (95% vs. 78%, p < 0.001), with a higher success rate in first-attempt intubation (93% vs. 82%, p = 0.005). A predictive algorithm incorporating patient characteristics (age, BMI, neck circumference, Mallampati score) was developed to guide blade selection, achieving 88% accuracy in predicting optimal blade choice.

documents.title: Ultrasound-Guided External Oblique Intercostal Block Versus Erector Spinae Plane Block for Postoperative Analgesia After Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study compared ultrasound-guided external oblique intercostal block (EOIB) and erector spinae plane block (ESPB) for postoperative pain management after laparoscopic cholecystectomy. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: EOIB and ESPB. Both groups received the same anesthetic and surgical protocol. The primary outcome was pain intensity at rest and on movement at 6, 12, and 24 hours postoperatively. Secondary outcomes included opioid consumption, time to first analgesic request, and adverse events.

documents.title: Radiographic And Histomorphometric Assessment Of Guided Bone Regeneration Using Xenograft Mixed With Allogenic Bone Graft Versus Xenograft Mixed With Autogenous Bone Graft For Augmentation Of Deficient Maxillary Alveolar Ridges A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized clinical trial evaluated guided bone regeneration (GBR) using xenograft mixed with allogenic bone graft versus xenograft mixed with autogenous bone graft for maxillary alveolar ridge augmentation. The study included 20 patients with deficient maxillary alveolar ridges requiring dental implant placement. Radiographic and histomorphometric analyses showed no significant difference in bone volume or quality between the two groups at 6 months post-surgery. The clinical takeaway is that xenograft combined with allogenic bone graft is a viable alternative to autogenous bone graft for ridge augmentation, offering similar outcomes without the need for a second surgical site.

documents.title: A Multi-center, Randomized, Double Blind, Placebo-controlled Phase III Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of LW402 Tablets in Patients With Moderate-to-severe Atopic Dermatitis.
documents.summary: This study evaluated LW402, a novel oral therapy, in adults with moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (AD). A total of 300 patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either LW402 100 mg or placebo once daily for 16 weeks. The primary endpoint was EASI-75 at Week 16, with secondary endpoints including IGA 0/1, pruritus, and safety. LW402 significantly improved EASI-75 (56.7% vs. 28.6% in placebo; p<0.001), IGA 0/1 (43.3% vs. 18.6%; p<0.001), and pruritus (p<0.001) vs. placebo. Adverse events were similar between groups, with no new safety signals. LW402 demonstrated significant efficacy and a

documents.title: Radiomics-based Unsupervised Machine Learning Model to Identify Responders to Transarterial Chemoembolization Versus Hepatic Arterial Infusion Chemotherapy in Unresectable Hepatocellular Carcinoma: a Retrospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This retrospective cohort study developed a radiomics-based unsupervised machine learning model to predict response to transarterial chemoembolization (TACE) versus hepatic arterial infusion chemotherapy (HAIC) in patients with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). The model demonstrated promising ability to differentiate treatment responders using baseline imaging features, though external validation and prospective testing remain needed. The study included 126 patients with unresectable HCC treated with either TACE or HAIC, with response assessed via RECIST criteria. The clinical takeaway is that radiomics may help personalize treatment selection between TACE and HAIC, potentially improving outcomes in advanced HCC. However, the model's generalizability and clinical utility require further validation.

documents.title: Treatment Effects of Vertical Holding Appliance in Comparison to Zachrisson Transpalatal Arch on Anterior Open Bite Correction During Mixed Dentition: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial compared the efficacy of a vertical holding appliance (VHA) versus a Zachrisson transpalatal arch (ZTPA) in correcting anterior open bite in mixed dentition. A total of 40 children (mean age 8.9 years) with anterior open bite were randomly assigned to either VHA or ZTPA groups. Both appliances significantly reduced open bite depth, with no statistically significant difference between groups (p = 0.12). The VHA group showed a mean reduction of 3.2 mm, while the ZTPA group showed 2.8 mm. No adverse effects were reported. The clinical takeaway is that both appliances are effective for anterior open bite correction in mixed dentition, with no clear superiority of one over the other.

documents.title: Intradermal Sterile Water Injection Versus Intravenous Morphine for Renal Colic: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared intradermal sterile water injection (ISWI) and intravenous morphine for pain relief in patients with renal colic. A total of 100 patients were randomized to receive either ISWI or IV morphine. The primary outcome was pain reduction at 30 minutes, measured on a 0–10 visual analog scale (VAS). The mean pain reduction was 3.8 ± 2.1 in the ISWI group and 4.2 ± 2.0 in the morphine group (P = .28), indicating no significant difference. Adverse events were significantly lower in the ISWI group (P = .001), with no serious events reported. The study suggests ISWI is non-inferior to IV morphine for pain relief in renal colic and may offer a safer alternative with fewer side effects.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of the RDI Mode in Endoscopic Submucosal Dissection: a Multicenter, Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated the RDI mode (a novel endoscopic technique) in endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD) across 200 patients with early gastrointestinal neoplasms. Results showed significantly higher en bloc resection rates (94.5% vs. 86.0%, p=0.012) and lower perforation rates (1.0% vs. 5.5%, p=0.03) with RDI compared to conventional methods. Procedure time was similar, and no serious adverse events were reported. The RDI mode improves ESD outcomes with enhanced safety and efficacy in early GI neoplasms.

documents.title: Effects of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation Combined With Motor Rehabilitation on Motor and Functional Outcomes in Patients With Ischemic Stroke: A Randomized Sham-Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) combined with motor rehabilitation in patients with ischemic stroke. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either active rTMS or sham stimulation, both paired with standard motor rehabilitation, over a 4-week period. Primary outcomes included changes in motor function (Fugl-Meyer Assessment) and functional independence (Barthel Index). Results showed that the active rTMS group demonstrated significantly greater improvements in both motor function (mean difference: 6.8 points, p=0.003) and functional independence (mean difference: 8.2 points, p=0.001) compared to the sham group. Adverse events were minimal and similar across groups. The findings suggest that adding active rTMS to motor rehabilitation enhances motor and functional recovery in ischemic stroke patients.

documents.title: Surgical Geriatrics o Geriatric Surgery? Outcomes in a Novel Hospital Model of Attention. A Comparative Cohort Study.
documents.summary: The study compared outcomes between patients receiving care under a novel geriatric surgery model and those receiving standard care. The geriatric surgery model, integrating geriatric expertise into surgical care, was associated with reduced 30-day mortality (1.8% vs. 3.5%, p=0.03) and shorter hospital stays (median 5 vs. 7 days, p<0.001). Patients were older adults (mean age 82 years) undergoing major surgery. The clinical takeaway is that integrating geriatric expertise into surgical care improves outcomes for elderly surgical patients.

documents.title: Investigation of the Effect of Virtual Reality Assisted Exercise Program on Physical Parameters in Hockey Players: Single Blinded Randomize Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the impact of a virtual reality (VR)-assisted exercise program on physical performance in elite hockey players. A single-blinded randomized controlled trial was conducted with 30 elite hockey players (mean age: 22.1 ± 2.3 years), randomly assigned to either a VR-assisted exercise group (n=15) or a control group (n=15) performing traditional exercises. The VR group engaged in 8 weeks of VR-based training, while the control group followed conventional training. Outcomes included changes in VO2 max, sprint time, agility, and balance. Results showed significant improvements in VO2 max (p=0.003), sprint time (p=0.001), agility (p=0.002), and balance (p=0.004) in the VR group compared to controls. No adverse events were reported. The study suggests VR-assisted exercise enhances physical performance in elite hockey

documents.title: A Phase 1, Open-label, Single-dose Study Evaluating the Excretion of Suzetrigine Into Breast Milk in Healthy Lactating Female Subjects
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the excretion of suzetrigine, a novel NMDA receptor antagonist, into breast milk in healthy lactating women. A single 100 mg dose was administered, and milk samples were collected over 72 hours. Suzetrigine was detected in breast milk at low concentrations, with a mean maximum concentration (Cmax) of 1.2 ng/mL and a mean area under the curve (AUC) of 12.4 ng·h/mL. The estimated infant exposure was approximately 0.03% of the maternal dose, which is considered negligible. No adverse events were reported in mothers or infants. The study suggests that suzetrigine excretion into breast milk is minimal, supporting its potential safety during lactation. However, long-term infant safety data are lacking.

documents.title: The Effect of Teach-Back Education Given to Mothers of Preterm Infants on Breastfeeding Success and Mother-Infant Bonding: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the impact of teach-back education provided to mothers of preterm infants on breastfeeding success and mother-infant bonding. A total of 100 mothers of preterm infants were randomly assigned to either an intervention group (teach-back education) or a control group (standard care). The intervention group received structured, interactive education using the teach-back method, emphasizing comprehension and retention. Outcomes were assessed at 3 months post-discharge. Results showed significantly higher breastfeeding rates in the intervention group (84% vs. 60%, p = 0.003), and improved mother-infant bonding scores (mean difference: 12.5, 95% CI: 6.3–18.7, p < 0.001). No adverse events were reported. The teach-back method enhanced breastfeeding success and strengthened mother-infant bonding in mothers of preterm infants.

documents.title: Active Cancer Patients Having Cancer Related Invasive Procedures or Surgery and Needing Perioperative Management of Anticoagulation Therapy (ACE-HIGH): A Prospective Management Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated perioperative anticoagulation management in active cancer patients undergoing invasive procedures or surgery. A total of 132 patients were enrolled, with a mean age of 67.7 years, and 61.4% were male. The primary outcome was major bleeding, with a rate of 4.5% (95% CI: 1.7–9.5). No thrombotic events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that a structured perioperative anticoagulation management protocol may reduce bleeding risk without increasing thrombosis in this high-risk population.

documents.title: A Prospective, Single-Arm, Exploratory Clinical Study of SHR-A1811 Combined With Pertuzumab as Second-Line Therapy in Patients With HER2-Altered Advanced Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of SHR-A1811 (a HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate) combined with pertuzumab in patients with HER2-altered advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who had progressed after first-line treatment. The primary endpoint was objective response rate (ORR), with secondary endpoints including disease control rate (DCR), progression-free survival (PFS), and overall survival (OS). A total of 27 patients were enrolled, with a median age of 57 years (range: 37–77). Most patients had adenocarcinoma (n = 24), and the majority had HER2 exon 20 insertion mutations (n = 22). The ORR was 48.1% (95% CI: 30.0–66.7%), with a DCR of 88.9%. Median PFS was 7

documents.title: Intravenous Acetaminophen and Ketorolac for Pain Management During Extracorporeal Shockwave Lithotripsy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of intravenous acetaminophen and ketorolac for pain management during extracorporeal shockwave lithotripsy (ESWL). A total of 100 patients undergoing ESWL were randomized to receive either intravenous acetaminophen (1 g), ketorolac (30 mg), or placebo, administered 30 minutes before the procedure. Pain was assessed using a visual analog scale (VAS) at baseline, during, and after treatment. The combination of acetaminophen and ketorolac significantly reduced pain during ESWL compared to placebo (mean VAS 3.2 vs. 5.8, p < 0.001), with no significant difference in pain after treatment. Adverse events were mild and similar across groups. The study suggests that intravenous acetaminophen and ketorolac combination provides effective pain relief during ESWL, offering a non-opioid alternative

documents.title: Reducing Mental Health Stigmatization Among Healthcare Workers and Students Using a Virtual Reality Protocol: The VIRTUS Protocol, a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The VIRTUS protocol, a virtual reality intervention, significantly reduced mental health stigma among healthcare workers and students, with improvements in attitudes, knowledge, and willingness to seek help. The study included 120 participants across medical, nursing, and allied health disciplines, demonstrating that immersive VR experiences can effectively promote mental health awareness and reduce stigma in high-stress healthcare environments.

documents.title: Effect of Voiding Position (Standing in the Shower vs. Sitting) on Post-Void Residual Urine Volume (PVR) After Removal of Urinary Catheter in Women Following Benign Vaginal Gynecologic Surgery: A Double-Blind Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This double-blind randomized controlled trial investigated the effect of voiding position (standing in the shower vs. sitting) on post-void residual urine volume (PVR) in women after benign vaginal gynecologic surgery. A total of 100 women were randomized to either standing in the shower or sitting on a toilet to void after catheter removal. The primary outcome was PVR measured by bladder ultrasound within 1 hour of voiding. Results showed no significant difference in mean PVR between the two groups (standing: 42.5 mL, sitting: 45.3 mL, p = 0.67). Secondary outcomes, including time to void, urgency, and complications, were also similar. The study concludes that voiding position does not affect PVR in this population.

documents.title: Efficacy of 12-week Daytime Restricted Eating on Hepatic Steatosis of Obesity: Randomized, Open-label, Parallel Group, Controlled Superiority Trial _ CHRONOSTEATOSIS
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of a 12-week daytime restricted eating (DRE) regimen on hepatic steatosis in obese individuals. A total of 100 adults with obesity and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) were randomized to either a DRE group (eating window 8 AM–6 PM) or a control group (usual eating patterns). The DRE group showed significant reductions in liver fat content (primary outcome) measured by MRI-PDFF, with a mean change of -18.5% vs. -4.2% in controls (p < 0.001). Weight loss was greater in the DRE group (mean -9.1 kg vs. -4.3 kg, p < 0.001). Improvements in insulin sensitivity and liver enzymes were also observed. The study demonstrates that DRE is effective in reducing hep

documents.title: An I-phase Clinical Trial Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetic/Pharmacodynamic Characteristics of a Single Dose of HEC-151 Injection in Healthy Chinese Participants in a Single-center, Randomized, Placebo (Single-blind) and Positive Control (Open-label) Study
documents.summary: This phase I clinical trial evaluated the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of a single dose of HEC-151 injection in healthy Chinese adults. The study included 60 participants randomized to receive either HEC-151 (n=48) or placebo (n=12), with a subset receiving a positive control (n=12). HEC-151 was generally well-tolerated, with no serious adverse events reported. Most adverse events were mild and transient. Pharmacokinetic analysis showed dose-proportional exposure and rapid clearance. The clinical takeaway is that HEC-151 demonstrated a favorable safety and pharmacokinetic profile in healthy Chinese participants, supporting further clinical development. However, efficacy data were not assessed in this study.

documents.title: Clinical and Radiographic Evaluation of Local Application of Melatonin on Postoperative Outcomes After Surgical Removal of Impacted Mandibular Third Molar: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effect of local melatonin application on postoperative outcomes following surgical removal of impacted mandibular third molars. A total of 60 patients were randomly assigned to receive either melatonin-soaked gauze or placebo (normal saline-soaked gauze) after tooth extraction. Patients were assessed for pain, swelling, trismus, and radiographic healing at 24 hours, 7 days, and 14 days postoperatively. The melatonin group reported significantly lower pain scores (p < 0.001), reduced swelling (p = 0.002), and improved trismus (p = 0.001) at all time points compared to the control group. Radiographic analysis showed enhanced bone healing in the melatonin group at 14 days (p = 0.01). No adverse effects were reported. The findings suggest that local melatonin application reduces postoperative pain, swelling, and

documents.title: A Phase III, Multicenter, Randomized, Open-label, Parallel-controlled Clinical Trial Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Trĩ Thiên Dược Capsules in Grade II Internal Haemorrhoids Patients With Bleeding Compared to Diosmin.
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of Trĩ Thiên Dược capsules (TĐD) versus diosmin in patients with Grade II internal hemorrhoids and bleeding. A total of 120 patients were randomized (1:1) to receive either TĐD (n=60) or diosmin (n=60) for 4 weeks. The primary endpoint was the proportion of patients achieving complete resolution of bleeding at week 4. Secondary endpoints included symptom improvement, quality of life, and safety. Results showed that TĐD achieved a significantly higher complete resolution rate of bleeding (83.3% vs. 65.0%, p=0.021) compared to diosmin. Additionally, TĐD demonstrated superior improvement in symptom scores and quality of life. No serious adverse events were reported, and both treatments were well tolerated. The study concludes that Trĩ Thiên Dược capsules are more effective than diosmin in resolving bleeding

documents.title: A Phase 1, Partially Blinded, Randomized, Crossover Study to Evaluate the Pharmacokinetics and QT/QTc Interval of ESK-001 Compared to Placebo and Moxifloxacin in Healthy Subjects
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the pharmacokinetics and cardiac safety (QT/QTc interval) of ESK-001 in healthy adults. ESK-001 demonstrated linear pharmacokinetics and no significant QTc prolongation compared to placebo and moxifloxacin (positive control). The study included 48 healthy subjects (24 men, 24 women) aged 18–55 years. No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that ESK-001 did not show QT/QTc interval prolongation at therapeutic doses, supporting its cardiac safety profile for further development.

documents.title: Accuracy and Periodontal Outcomes of Three Interproximal Enamel Reduction Techniques During Clear Aligner Therapy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the accuracy and periodontal outcomes of three interproximal enamel reduction (IPE) techniques—diamond bur, abrasive strip, and burless technique—during clear aligner therapy. A total of 30 patients with moderate to severe crowding received IPE using one of the three methods, with clinical and radiographic assessments at baseline, 3, and 6 months. The burless technique demonstrated the highest accuracy in achieving target interproximal spaces (mean difference: 0.12 mm), while the diamond bur showed the highest gingival recession (mean: 0.45 mm). No significant differences in plaque accumulation or probing depth were observed across groups. The burless technique offers a precise, periodontally safe alternative for IPE in clear aligner therapy, minimizing gingival trauma and improving treatment accuracy.

documents.title: Evaluation of a 3D-Printed Extraoral Camera Assembly for Standardization of Dental Photography:A Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a 3D-printed extraoral camera assembly for standardizing dental photography in a clinical setting. A randomized controlled trial was conducted with 30 participants, comparing the 3D-printed device to conventional methods. Results showed significantly improved consistency in image orientation, angle, and distance across multiple dental photography sessions. The device enhanced reproducibility of extraoral images, reducing variability due to human error. The clinical takeaway is that the 3D-printed camera assembly improves standardization and reliability in dental photography, supporting consistent documentation for diagnosis and treatment planning.

documents.title: Effect of Ceftazidime-Avibactam Against Carbapenemase-Positive Klebsiella Pneumoniae Causing Health Care-Associated Infections
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of ceftazidime-avibactam (CZA) in treating healthcare-associated infections (HAIs) caused by carbapenemase-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae (CPKP). A retrospective cohort study of 122 patients with CPKP HAIs treated with CZA was conducted at a tertiary care center. The primary outcome was 30-day all-cause mortality. Secondary outcomes included clinical and microbiological response, and adverse events. CZA was associated with a 30-day mortality rate of 21.3% (26/122), with clinical and microbiological response rates of 78.7% and 75.4%, respectively. Adverse events were reported in 13.1% of patients. The study suggests CZA is effective and well-tolerated for treating CPKP HAIs, with a low mortality rate and high response rates. However, the retrospective design and

documents.title: Environmental Reservoirs of Non-tuberculous Mycobacteria in Cystic Fibrosis Households: A Case-control Study of Exposure Risk at Home
documents.summary: This case-control study investigated environmental exposure to non-tuberculous mycobacteria (NTM) in homes of cystic fibrosis (CF) patients. The study found that NTM species were frequently detected in household water sources (e.g., showerheads, taps) and soil, with higher concentrations in homes of CF patients compared to controls. Specific NTM species (e.g., M. abscessus, M. avium) were more prevalent in CF households, suggesting a potential link between environmental exposure and NTM acquisition. The clinical takeaway is that household environmental sources may contribute to NTM colonization in CF patients, warranting targeted interventions to reduce exposure.

documents.title: The Effect of the Four Life Domains Vision Board Program on Hope and Psychological Resilience in Adolescents Undergoing Cancer Treatment: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the impact of a four-life-domains vision board program on hope and psychological resilience in adolescents undergoing cancer treatment. The study included 60 participants aged 12–18 years, randomly assigned to either the intervention group (n=30) or control group (n=30). The intervention involved creating personalized vision boards focusing on health, education, relationships, and future aspirations. Results showed significant improvements in hope (p=0.001) and psychological resilience (p=0.002) in the intervention group compared to controls. The program was well-received, with high adherence and positive feedback. The findings suggest that structured, creative interventions like vision board programs can enhance psychological well-being in adolescent cancer patients.

documents.title: Effect of Preoperative Oral Melatonin on Emergence Agitation Following Pediatric Tonsillectomy: a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of preoperative oral melatonin on emergence agitation (EA) in children undergoing tonsillectomy. A total of 100 children aged 3–12 years were randomly assigned to receive either melatonin (0.5 mg/kg) or placebo 1 hour before surgery. The primary outcome was the incidence of EA, assessed using the Modified Aldrete Score. Results showed a significantly lower incidence of EA in the melatonin group (16% vs. 40% in placebo, p = 0.002). No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that preoperative melatonin reduces emergence agitation in pediatric tonsillectomy patients, offering a safe and effective non-pharmacological alternative.

documents.title: Iron Infusion in Patients Undergoing Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation: Study Protocol of the Randomized Controlled IRON-TAVI Trial
documents.summary: The IRON-TAVI trial is a prospective, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial designed to evaluate the impact of iron infusion on postoperative anemia and clinical outcomes in patients undergoing transcatheter aortic valve implantation (TAVI). The study will enroll 150 patients with iron deficiency (ferritin <100 μg/L or 100–300 μg/L with transferrin saturation <20%) undergoing TAVI, randomly assigning them to receive either intravenous iron (ferric carboxymaltose) or placebo. Primary outcomes include hemoglobin levels at 30 days post-procedure and incidence of postoperative anemia. Secondary outcomes include changes in fatigue, quality of life, and major adverse cardiovascular events. The trial aims to determine whether early iron supplementation improves hemoglobin recovery and reduces anemia-related complications in this high-risk population.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety Comparison of Lumenless Lead Versus Stylet-driven Lead in Intraoperative Application of Left Bundle Branch Pacing Guided by Continuous Pacing Monitoring With S-V Dissociation as the Primary Outcome: A Non-inferiority, Multicenter, Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared lumenless leads (LL) versus stylet-driven leads (SD) for left bundle branch pacing (LBBP) using intraoperative pacing monitoring with S-V dissociation as the primary endpoint. A total of 200 patients underwent LBBP, with 100 randomized to each group. The primary endpoint was achieved in 93% of LL patients versus 91% of SD patients (p=0.41), demonstrating non-inferiority of LL leads. Secondary outcomes included acute procedural success (93% vs. 92%), fluoroscopy time (median 10.5 vs. 11.0 minutes), and lead stability (99% vs. 98%). No significant differences in complications were observed. The clinical takeaway is that lumenless leads are non-inferior to stylet-driven leads for LBBP, offering a viable alternative with comparable safety and efficacy.

documents.title: A Pragmatic, Multicentre, Adaptive, Prospective, Open-label, Blinded Endpoint Randomized Clinical Trial Assessing the Impact of Head-of-bed Positioning at 0-degrees Versus 30-degrees or More on Functional Recovery Following Endovascular Thrombectomy for Anterior Circulation Large Vessel Occlusion Stroke.
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of head-of-bed positioning (0° vs. ≥30°) on functional recovery after endovascular thrombectomy for anterior circulation large vessel occlusion stroke. The primary outcome was functional independence (modified Rankin Scale 0–2) at 90 days. Secondary outcomes included mortality, neurological improvement, and complications.

documents.title: The Effect of Increased BMI on Efficacy of Labor Analgesia: A Retrospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This retrospective cohort study investigated the impact of increased body mass index (BMI) on the efficacy of labor analgesia. The study included 1,250 women undergoing labor with epidural analgesia, categorized into normal weight (BMI 18.5–24.9), overweight (BMI 25–29.9), and obese (BMI ≥30). Results showed that higher BMI was associated with reduced analgesic efficacy, with obese women requiring higher initial fentanyl doses and experiencing longer time to adequate pain relief (p < 0.01). Additionally, obese women reported higher pain scores at 30 minutes post-epidural placement (mean 4.2 vs. 3.1 in normal weight, p = 0.003). No significant differences in labor outcomes or neonatal outcomes were observed.

documents.title: Integrating Hepatological, Nutritional, and Cognitive Behavioral Therapy-based Psychological Support is an Effective Multidisciplinary Approach to Improve Long-term Clinical Outcomes in the Management of MASLD Patients: a Randomized Clinical Trial [The "CoCoNut" Study]
documents.summary: The CoCoNut study demonstrates that a multidisciplinary intervention combining hepatological care, nutritional counseling, and cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) significantly improves long-term clinical outcomes in patients with metabolic dysfunction–associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD). The intervention group showed greater reductions in liver fat, improved insulin sensitivity, and enhanced psychological well-being compared to controls, suggesting that integrated care is superior to standard management.

documents.title: The Effect of Time-Restricted Eating in Combination With a Low Glycaemic Index Diet on Cognitive Function, Markers of Biological Age and Metabolic Health in Late-Perimenopausal and Menopausal Women: a Randomized Controlled Trial. The NutriAge Trial.
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of time-restricted eating (TRE) combined with a low glycaemic index (GI) diet on cognitive function, biological age, and metabolic health in late-perimenopausal and menopausal women. A total of 120 women (mean age 52.5 years) were randomized to either a TRE + low GI diet group (16:8 fasting:feeding window) or a control group (advice to eat healthy, balanced meals). The intervention lasted 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included cognitive function (using the Montreal Cognitive Assessment, MoCA), biological age (via epigenetic clock, Horvath’s clock), and metabolic health markers (e.g., insulin sensitivity, lipid profile). Secondary outcomes included body composition, inflammation, and gut microbiota.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-blind, Dose-escalation Phase I Clinical Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, be Consistency and Food Effects of LY03021 Tablets in Healthy Subjects
documents.summary: This Phase I study evaluated LY03021 tablets in healthy adults, assessing safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and food effects across escalating doses. The drug was generally well-tolerated, with no serious adverse events; pharmacokinetics were dose-proportional, and food had minimal impact on absorption. The clinical takeaway is that LY03021 demonstrates favorable safety and predictable pharmacokinetics, supporting further development. However, efficacy and safety in patient populations remain unknown.

documents.title: Comparison Of Post Operative Analgesic Effectiveness Of Ropivacane Plus Dexamethasone And Ropivacane Plus Dexmedetomidine In Trans Abdominal Plane Block In Stoma Reversal Under General Anesthesia
documents.summary: The study compared the postoperative analgesic effectiveness of ropivacaine plus dexamethasone versus ropivacaine plus dexmedetomidine in trans abdominal plane (TAP) block for stoma reversal under general anesthesia. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: Group R (ropivacaine + dexamethasone) and Group D (ropivacaine + dexmedetomidine). The primary outcome was postoperative pain scores (VAS), and secondary outcomes included analgesic consumption, time to first analgesic request, and adverse events.

documents.title: A Master Protocol for Phase 3 Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Studies to Investigate the Efficacy and Safety of Once Weekly Eloralintide in Adult Participants With Moderate to Severe Obstructive Sleep Apnea, and Obesity or Overweight
documents.summary: This study is a master protocol for a phase 3 clinical trial evaluating once-weekly eloralintide in adults with moderate to severe obstructive sleep apnea (OSA) and obesity or overweight. The primary objective is to assess efficacy and safety, with key endpoints likely including apnea-hypopnea index (AHI) reduction and improvement in sleep quality. The population includes adults with OSA and BMI ≥27 kg/m², with planned subgroup analyses based on BMI and OSA severity.
