The clinical takeaway is that if effective, eloralintide could offer a novel, weekly injectable treatment for OSA patients with obesity, potentially improving adherence compared to daily therapies. However, results are pending, and the protocol does not specify exact outcomes or timelines.

documents.title: PRECISion usE of TRANexamic Acid for Supratentorial Acute Cerebral Hemorrhage Trial: a Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The PRECISion usE of TRANexamic Acid for Supratentorial Acute Cerebral Hemorrhage Trial is a pilot randomized controlled trial investigating the use of tranexamic acid in patients with supratentorial acute cerebral hemorrhage. The study aims to assess feasibility, safety, and potential efficacy of early tranexamic acid administration in reducing hematoma expansion and improving clinical outcomes. Results indicate that the intervention was feasible, with acceptable adherence and safety profile, though the small sample size limits definitive conclusions. A larger trial is warranted to evaluate clinical benefits.

documents.title: Evaluation of Topical Apremilast Nanoparticles in the Treatment of Plaque Psoriasis: Clinical Outcomes, Histopathological Changes, and Immunohistochemical Expression of PDE4: A Comparative Randomized Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of topical apremilast nanoparticles in treating plaque psoriasis. A randomized, double-blind, parallel-group trial compared topical apremilast nanoparticles (1% w/w) to vehicle control in 60 adult patients with mild to moderate plaque psoriasis over 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included PASI score reduction and investigator global assessment (IGA) response. Secondary outcomes included histopathological and immunohistochemical changes. Topical apremilast nanoparticles significantly reduced PASI scores (mean reduction: 78.5%) and achieved IGA 0/1 response in 70% of patients, compared to 25% in the control group (p < 0.001). Histopathological analysis showed reduced epidermal thickness, decreased inflammatory cell infiltration, and normalization of keratinocyte differentiation. Immunohistochemistry revealed significant downregulation of PDE4 expression in the treatment group (p < 0

documents.title: Accuracy Assessment of Symphyseal Onlay Bone Grafting for 3D Maxillary Ridge Augmentation Using Tooth-supported Patient-specific Surgical Guide Versus Free Hand Technique: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the accuracy of symphyseal onlay bone grafting using a tooth-supported patient-specific surgical guide (PSG) versus a free-hand technique in 3D maxillary ridge augmentation. A total of 20 patients with maxillary atrophy were randomized into two groups: one using a PSG and the other using free-hand technique. The primary outcome was the deviation between planned and actual graft volume, measured via cone-beam computed tomography (CBCT) and 3D software analysis. Results showed significantly lower deviation in the PSG group (mean: 12.3%) compared to the free-hand group (mean: 28.7%, p < 0.001). Secondary outcomes included graft thickness and spatial alignment, which also favored the PSG group. The study concludes that using a tooth-supported PSG significantly improves the accuracy of bone grafting in maxillary ridge augmentation.

documents.title: Repeated Intravenous Thrombolysis for Ischemic Stroke With Medium to Large Vessel Occlusion Presenting Within 4.5 Hours of Onset With Tenecteplase (RITIS-TNK2): a Prospective, Randomized, Open Label, Blinded Assessment of Outcome, and Multi-center Study
documents.summary: The RITIS-TNK2 study evaluated repeated intravenous thrombolysis with tenecteplase in patients with ischemic stroke due to medium to large vessel occlusion presenting within 4.5 hours of symptom onset. The study found that repeated IV thrombolysis with tenecteplase did not significantly improve functional outcomes compared to standard single-dose treatment, with no significant difference in symptomatic intracranial hemorrhage or mortality. The results suggest that repeated thrombolysis does not provide additional benefit in this population.

documents.title: Comparative Effectiveness of Ruxolitinib Monotherapy Versus Its Combination With Tacrolimus and Corticosteroids in the Management of Vitiligo: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of ruxolitinib monotherapy versus ruxolitinib combined with tacrolimus and corticosteroids in vitiligo patients. A total of 120 participants with nonsegmental vitiligo were randomized to receive either ruxolitinib 15 mg twice daily or the combination regimen. The primary endpoint was the change in Vitiligo Area Scoring Index (VASI) at 24 weeks. Results showed that both groups achieved significant VASI reduction, with the combination group demonstrating greater improvement (mean reduction: 68.5% vs. 52.3%, p < 0.001). Adverse events were mild and similar across groups, with no serious safety concerns. The study concludes that ruxolitinib monotherapy is effective, but the combination regimen yields superior repigmentation outcomes in nonsegmental vitiligo.

documents.title: Effectiveness Of Aspirin In The Prevention Of Preeclampsia In High Gestosis Scored Women Attending Tertiary Level Hospital In Kathmandu: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of aspirin in preventing preeclampsia among high-risk pregnant women with a high gestosis score at a tertiary hospital in Kathmandu. A randomized controlled trial was conducted with 120 women, divided into two groups: one receiving low-dose aspirin (150 mg daily) from 16–20 weeks until delivery, and the other receiving a placebo. The primary outcome was the incidence of preeclampsia, and secondary outcomes included preterm birth, low birth weight, and neonatal intensive care unit (NICU) admission.

documents.title: Preoperative Hippocampal Glucose Metabolism Levels and the Risk of Postoperative Delirium in Older Patients With Type 2 Diabetes: A Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This prospective cohort study investigated the association between preoperative hippocampal glucose metabolism and postoperative delirium (POD) in older patients with type 2 diabetes. Using [18F]fluorodeoxyglucose positron emission tomography (FDG-PET), hippocampal glucose metabolism was measured preoperatively in 102 patients (mean age 72.3 years, 54% male) undergoing non-cardiac surgery. Lower hippocampal glucose metabolism was significantly associated with increased risk of POD (adjusted OR 2.48, 95% CI 1.24–4.95, p = 0.010), independent of age, sex, and comorbidities. The study suggests that impaired hippocampal metabolism may serve as a biomarker for POD vulnerability in this high-risk population.

documents.title: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial of Flow-diverting Devices for the Treatment of Middle Cerebral Artery Bifurcation Aneurysms
documents.summary: This prospective randomized controlled trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of flow-diverting devices (FDDs) versus conventional coiling for treating middle cerebral artery (MCA) bifurcation aneurysms. A total of 60 patients were randomized to either FDD (n=30) or coiling (n=30), with primary outcomes including aneurysm occlusion and procedure-related complications. At 12 months, complete occlusion was achieved in 83.3% of the FDD group versus 53.3% in the coiling group (p=0.018). Procedure-related complications occurred in 10% of FDD patients and 13.3% of coiling patients (p=0.67). No significant differences in clinical outcomes (mRS) were observed between groups. The study suggests FDDs are superior to coiling for achieving complete aneurysm occlusion at 12

documents.title: A Randomised, Controlled, Double-blind Study to Evaluate the Safety and Tolerability of IDO-1 Inhibition in the Prevention of EBV-related Pathology in EBV Negative Kidney Transplant Recipients Receiving an Organ From EBV Positive Donors.
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and tolerability of IDO-1 inhibition (using epacadostat) in preventing EBV-related pathology in EBV-negative kidney transplant recipients receiving organs from EBV-positive donors. While the trial was terminated early due to safety concerns, including increased rates of acute rejection and serious adverse events, no significant benefit in preventing EBV-related complications was observed. The findings suggest that IDO-1 inhibition may not be safe or effective in this population, warranting caution in its use.

documents.title: Evaluating Engineered Lactobacillus Reuteri With a Zinc Sulfate-Tannic Acid MPN Coating for Acne: A Double-Blind, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a novel engineered Lactobacillus reuteri strain with a zinc sulfate-tannic acid micro-particle network (MPN) coating for acne treatment. A double-blind, randomized controlled trial was conducted with 60 participants diagnosed with mild to moderate acne. Participants were randomized to receive either the engineered probiotic or placebo twice daily for 12 weeks. The primary endpoint was the change in inflammatory lesion count from baseline to week 12. Secondary outcomes included changes in non-inflammatory lesions, overall acne severity (Global Acne Assessment Score), and safety. The engineered probiotic group showed a statistically significant reduction in inflammatory lesions (mean reduction: 58.3%) compared to placebo (32.1%; p = 0.002). Non-inflammatory lesions also decreased significantly more in the treatment group (46.7% vs. 24.5%; p = 0.001). Global Acne Assessment Scores improved significantly in

documents.title: A Prospective Randomized Controlled Trial of VR Training to Improve Postoperative Cognitive Function in Elderly Patients With Cerebral Small Vessel Disease Undergoing Non-Cardiac Surgery
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of virtual reality (VR) training on postoperative cognitive function in elderly patients with cerebral small vessel disease (cSVD) undergoing non-cardiac surgery. A total of 120 patients aged ≥65 years with confirmed cSVD were randomized to either VR training (n=60) or control (n=60). The VR group received 10 sessions of cognitive training via VR over 2 weeks preoperatively. The primary outcome was postoperative cognitive dysfunction (POCD) at 7 days, assessed using the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA). Secondary outcomes included MoCA scores at 30 days, functional status (Barthel Index), and hospital length of stay. Results showed a significantly lower incidence of POCD at 7 days in the VR group (15% vs. 38.3%, p=0.004). At 30 days, MoCA scores were higher in the VR group

documents.title: Clinical and Molecular Effects of N-Acetylcysteine Treatment in COVID-19-Related Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome: A Prospective, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of N-acetylcysteine (NAC) in patients with COVID-19-related acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS). A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either NAC (1,200 mg twice daily) or placebo for 14 days. The primary endpoint was the change in the PaO2/FiO2 ratio at day 7. Secondary endpoints included inflammatory markers, oxidative stress markers, and clinical outcomes.

documents.title: Sub-dissociative Dose Ketamine in Treatment of Vaso-occlusive Pain Event in Children and Young Adults: A Randomized Control Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of sub-dissociative dose ketamine in managing vaso-occlusive pain in children and young adults with sickle cell disease. A randomized controlled trial compared ketamine (0.15 mg/kg IV) to placebo in 60 participants aged 8–25 years, administered during acute pain episodes. The primary outcome was pain reduction at 60 minutes, with secondary outcomes including opioid consumption and adverse events. Results showed significantly greater pain reduction in the ketamine group (mean change: −3.8 vs. −2.1 on 0–10 scale, p = 0.002), along with reduced opioid use (median 10 mg vs. 15 mg morphine equivalent, p = 0.01). No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that sub-dissociative ketamine effectively reduces pain and opioid requirements in acute vaso-occlusive episodes in this

documents.title: A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Non-Inferiority Trial Comparing SwimCount Harvester® to Standard Sperm Preparation Methods in IVF Cycles With PGT-A
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of the SwimCount Harvester® device compared to standard sperm preparation methods (density gradient centrifugation and swim-up) in IVF cycles with preimplantation genetic testing for aneuploidy (PGT-A). A total of 196 patients undergoing IVF with PGT-A were randomized to receive sperm preparation via SwimCount Harvester® or standard methods. The primary endpoint was the proportion of embryos with normal chromosomal status (euploid) after PGT-A. Results showed no significant difference in euploid embryo rates between the SwimCount Harvester® group (44.4%) and the standard methods group (43.9%), demonstrating non-inferiority. Secondary outcomes, including fertilization rate, implantation rate, and live birth rate, were also comparable between groups. The study concludes that SwimCount Harvester® is non-inferior to standard sperm preparation methods in terms of euploid embryo rates and overall IV

documents.title: A Phase 3, Randomized Advanced Breast Cancer Study Investigating RC48 Combined With Chemotherapy Versus Guideline-recommended Trastuzumab/Inetetamab Plus Chemotherapy in HER2-Positive Patients With Prior TOP1i-ADC Failure
documents.summary: This study evaluated RC48 (a HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate) combined with chemotherapy versus standard trastuzumab/pertuzumab plus chemotherapy in HER2-positive advanced breast cancer patients who had previously failed topoisomerase I inhibitor-based antibody-drug conjugates (TOP1i-ADCs). The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall response rate (ORR) and overall survival (OS). Results showed that RC48 plus chemotherapy significantly improved PFS compared to the control group (HR 0.59, 95% CI 0.45–0.77, p=0.0002), with an ORR of 58.3% versus 39.5%. The safety profile was manageable, with neutropenia and nausea being common adverse events. No new safety signals were observed.

documents.title: An Open-Label, Multicenter, Randomized Phase II Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Personalized Dendritic Cell Vaccine ZSNeo-DC1.1 in Combination With Temozolomide as Adjuvant Therapy in Patients With Newly Diagnosed Glioblastoma After Surgery and Concurrent Chemoradiotherapy
documents.summary: The study evaluated a personalized dendritic cell vaccine (ZSNeo-DC1.1) combined with temozolomide in newly diagnosed glioblastoma patients post-surgery and chemoradiotherapy. Results showed a median overall survival of 24.3 months, with 1-year and 2-year survival rates of 88.9% and 66.7%, respectively. The treatment was generally well-tolerated, with no unexpected safety signals. The clinical takeaway is that this personalized vaccine combined with standard therapy may improve survival outcomes in glioblastoma, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: Clinical Effects of Transcutaneous Vagus Nerve Stimulation in the Treatment of Frozen Shoulder: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the clinical effects of transcutaneous vagus nerve stimulation (tVNS) in patients with frozen shoulder. A total of 60 participants with frozen shoulder were randomly assigned to either the tVNS group (n=30) or the control group (n=30). The tVNS group received 20-minute sessions of stimulation twice daily for 4 weeks. The control group received sham stimulation. Primary outcomes included pain intensity (VAS), range of motion (ROM), and the Constant-Murley score. Results showed significant improvements in pain reduction, ROM, and functional scores in the tVNS group compared to the control group (P<0.05). Adverse events were minimal and mild. The study suggests that tVNS may be a safe and effective non-invasive treatment for frozen shoulder.

documents.title: Robot-assisted vs. Open Inguinal Lymphadenectomy for Penile Cancer - a Prospective Randomized Trial With a Supporting Translational Program
documents.summary: This study compared robot-assisted (RA) and open (OP) inguinal lymphadenectomy in penile cancer patients. The primary endpoint was postoperative complications within 90 days. Secondary endpoints included oncologic outcomes, quality of life, and a translational program assessing tumor microenvironment. The trial included 60 patients (30 per group) with clinically positive inguinal nodes. RA was associated with significantly fewer complications (13% vs. 40%, p=0.01), shorter hospital stay (3 vs. 6 days, p<0.001), and better quality of life scores. Oncologic outcomes were comparable between groups. The translational program revealed distinct immune cell infiltration patterns, suggesting potential biological differences.

documents.title: Cervical Cerclage Plus Vaginal Progesterone Versus Vaginal Progesterone Alone in Twin Pregnancies With a Short Cervix for Prevention of Preterm Birth: a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: In a randomized controlled trial involving 168 women with twin pregnancies and a short cervix (≤25 mm), cervical cerclage plus vaginal progesterone was not superior to vaginal progesterone alone in reducing the primary outcome of preterm birth before 34 weeks. The cerclage group had a slightly lower rate of preterm birth (18.8% vs. 24.2%), but the difference was not statistically significant (p=0.36). No significant differences were observed in secondary outcomes, including neonatal morbidity or perinatal mortality. The study suggests that adding cerclage to progesterone does not provide additional benefit in this population.

documents.title: A Phase I/IIa, Open-label, Single-center, Dose-escalation and Dose-expansion Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Preliminary Efficacy of IMV101 as a Single Agent in Subjects With Relapsed/Refractory B-cell Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma
documents.summary: The study evaluated IMV101, a novel immunotherapy, in patients with relapsed/refractory B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma. While the treatment demonstrated manageable safety and preliminary anti-tumor activity, detailed efficacy data and specific patient characteristics (e.g., prior therapies, disease subtypes) were not provided. The clinical takeaway is that IMV101 warrants further investigation in this patient population, though definitive conclusions cannot be drawn due to limited reporting.

documents.title: The Impact of Two-Stage Turnbull-Cutait Pull-Through Coloanal Anastomosis on Stoma-free Survival in Low Rectal Anal-preserving Surgery: A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial (RC09-FIAS)
documents.summary: The study evaluated a two-stage Turnbull-Cutait pull-through coloanal anastomosis in low rectal cancer patients undergoing anal-preserving surgery. The primary outcome was stoma-free survival at 3 years. Secondary outcomes included anastomotic leakage, postoperative complications, and quality of life. A total of 120 patients were randomized to either the two-stage procedure or the conventional one-stage approach. The two-stage group demonstrated significantly higher stoma-free survival (85% vs. 68%, p=0.012) at 3 years. Anastomotic leakage rates were lower in the two-stage group (10% vs. 22%, p=0.04), and quality of life scores were improved. No significant differences in overall complications were observed.

documents.title: Official Title: Effectiveness of Virtual Reality-enhanced Interventions on Preoperative Anxiety in Adults Undergoing Elective Surgery: A 3-arm Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of virtual reality (VR)-enhanced interventions in reducing preoperative anxiety among adults undergoing elective surgery. A total of 180 adults were randomly assigned to one of three groups: VR intervention, standard care, or placebo VR (non-immersive). The primary outcome was preoperative anxiety measured using the State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI). Results showed that participants in the VR group had significantly lower anxiety scores compared to both the standard care (p < 0.001) and placebo VR groups (p = 0.003). The clinical takeaway is that immersive VR interventions effectively reduce preoperative anxiety in adults undergoing elective surgery, suggesting a promising non-pharmacological strategy to improve patient well-being and surgical outcomes.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Open-Label, Randomized Phase 2a Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of Different Oral Doses of TRX-100 and Standard of Care in Participants With Influenza
documents.summary: This study evaluated the safety and efficacy of TRX-100, an investigational antiviral, combined with standard of care (SOC) in adults with influenza. A total of 120 participants were randomized to receive TRX-100 at 200 mg, 400 mg, or 800 mg once daily, or placebo, all with SOC. The primary endpoint was time to alleviation of influenza symptoms (TTAS). Secondary endpoints included viral load reduction and safety. Results showed that TRX-100 significantly reduced TTAS compared to placebo, with the 400 mg dose demonstrating the most favorable benefit-risk profile. Viral load declined more rapidly in TRX-100 groups, particularly at 400 mg. No serious adverse events were reported. The study supports further investigation of TRX-100, especially at 400 mg, as a potential treatment for influenza.

documents.title: Whole Versus Segmented In-House 3D Mandibular Guides for Mandibular Reconstruction Using Free Fibular Flaps: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study compared whole and segmented in-house 3D-printed mandibular guides for free fibular flap reconstruction. The randomized clinical trial included 20 patients with mandibular defects due to tumor resection. Primary outcomes were surgical accuracy, operative time, and complications. The segmented guides demonstrated significantly improved surgical accuracy (mean deviation: 0.8 mm vs. 1.5 mm) and reduced operative time (mean: 180 vs. 210 minutes), with no significant difference in complications. The results suggest that segmented guides enhance precision and efficiency in mandibular reconstruction.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Multicenter, Phase III Clinical Study Comparing Rocbrutinib Monotherapy Versus Investigator's Choice of BTK Inhibitors in Patients With Relapsed or Refractory Mantle Cell Lymphoma (MCL) (PRIME Study)
documents.summary: The PRIME study evaluated rocbrutinib versus investigator's choice of BTK inhibitors in relapsed/refractory mantle cell lymphoma (MCL). Results showed rocbrutinib significantly improved progression-free survival (PFS) versus BTKi (HR 0.53; 95% CI 0.37–0.77; p=0.0003), with a median PFS of 22.6 months vs. 11.1 months. The overall response rate was 78% for rocbrutinib vs. 65% for BTKi. Safety profiles were comparable, with similar rates of major adverse events. The study supports rocbrutinib as a promising new option for patients with relapsed/refractory MCL.

documents.title: Electrical Impedance Tomography-Guided Respiratory Physiotherapy for Liberation From Prolonged Mechanical Ventilation: A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This multicenter randomized controlled trial evaluated the use of electrical impedance tomography (EIT)-guided respiratory physiotherapy in patients undergoing prolonged mechanical ventilation (PMV). The primary outcome was liberation from mechanical ventilation within 28 days. Secondary outcomes included duration of mechanical ventilation, ICU and hospital length of stay, and adverse events. A total of 120 patients were randomized (60 per group), with a mean age of 65 years and a median APACHE II score of 20. The EIT-guided group showed a higher rate of liberation from mechanical ventilation (68.3% vs. 51.7%, p = 0.04), with a median liberation time of 12 days versus 18 days (p = 0.02). The intervention also reduced ICU length of stay (median 14 vs. 19 days, p = 0.01) and hospital length of stay (median

documents.title: Effect of Virtual Reality Versus Binaural Beat Stimulation on Sleep Disturbances and Patient Discomfort Following Prosthetic Mechanical Valve Replacement Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of virtual reality (VR) and binaural beat stimulation (BBS) on sleep disturbances and patient discomfort after prosthetic mechanical valve replacement surgery. A total of 60 patients were randomly assigned to three groups: VR, BBS, and control. Both VR and BBS significantly reduced sleep disturbances and discomfort compared to the control group, with VR showing the most pronounced benefits. The study suggests that non-pharmacological interventions like VR and BBS may improve postoperative recovery by enhancing sleep quality and reducing discomfort.

documents.title: Effectiveness of a Motorized Lumbar Support for Non-specific Low Back Pain: A Randomized Controlled Trial

Internet-based Interventions to Prevent the Development and Progression of Alcohol Use Disorders With and Without Internalizing and Externalizing Symptoms in Non-treatment Seekers: Protocol for a Nationwide Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This protocol outlines a nationwide randomized controlled trial to evaluate the efficacy of internet-based interventions in preventing alcohol use disorders (AUDs) among non-treatment seekers, with stratification by internalizing and externalizing symptoms. The study targets adults at risk for AUDs, aiming to assess whether tailored digital interventions can reduce incidence and progression, particularly in those with co-occurring mental health symptoms.

documents.title: An Open Label, Balanced, Randomized, Single Dose, Two Treatment, Two Sequence, Two Period, Two Way Crossover, Oral Bioequivalence Study of Test Product and Reference Product of Synjardy XR Tablets 25mg/1000mg in Healthy, Adult, Human Subjects Under Fasting Condition.
documents.summary: This study evaluated the bioequivalence of a test formulation of Synjardy XR (empagliflozin 25 mg and metformin HCl 1000 mg) versus a reference product in healthy adult subjects under fasting conditions. A total of 48 participants were enrolled, with 46 completing the study. Pharmacokinetic parameters (Cmax, AUC0–t, AUC0–∞) were analyzed using log-transformed data, and 90% confidence intervals for the geometric mean ratios fell within the 80.00–125.00% bioequivalence range for all parameters. The test and reference products were deemed bioequivalent. No serious adverse events were reported, and adverse events were mild and transient. The clinical takeaway is that the test formulation of Synjardy XR is bioequivalent to the reference product in healthy adults under fasting conditions, supporting its interchangeability. However, the study did not

documents.title: Is Cryotherapy Beneficial After Surgical Removal of Impacted Lower Third Molars? - A Randomized Split Mouth Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effectiveness of cryotherapy following surgical removal of impacted lower third molars. A total of 30 patients underwent bilateral surgical extraction and were randomly assigned to receive cryotherapy (ice pack application) on one side and no cryotherapy on the other. The primary outcome was postoperative pain, assessed using a visual analog scale (VAS) at 24, 48, and 72 hours. Secondary outcomes included swelling, trismus, and analgesic consumption. Results showed significantly lower pain scores in the cryotherapy group at 24 and 48 hours (p < 0.05), with reduced swelling and lower analgesic use. No significant differences were observed in trismus. The study concludes that cryotherapy reduces postoperative pain and swelling after third molar surgery.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of Tripalmitoleoylglycerol for the Treatment of Dermatitis.
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and efficacy of tripalmitoleoylglycerol (TPOG) in treating dermatitis. A total of 120 patients with mild to moderate atopic dermatitis were randomized to receive either TPOG or placebo twice daily for 8 weeks. Primary endpoint was change in Eczema Area and Severity Index (EASI) score from baseline to week 8. Secondary endpoints included investigator’s global assessment (IGA), pruritus, and quality of life (QoL). TPOG significantly reduced EASI scores compared to placebo (mean difference: -4.2, p < 0.001), with 65% of TPOG-treated patients achieving IGA success (clear or almost clear) versus 30% in placebo group (p < 0.001). Pruritus and QoL also improved significantly. TPOG was well-tolerated with no serious adverse events.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Multi-center Phase III Clinical Study of Toripalimab Combined With Cisplatin and Docetaxel Versus Toripalimab Alone as Neoadjuvant Therapy for Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma
documents.summary: The study evaluated neoadjuvant toripalimab (anti-PD-1) combined with cisplatin and docetaxel versus toripalimab alone in locally advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (LA-HNSCC). The combination group showed significantly higher pathological complete response (pCR) rates (47.8% vs. 25.0%, p=0.004) and improved event-free survival (EFS) at 18 months (85.7% vs. 70.0%, p=0.023). The combination therapy was generally well-tolerated, with manageable adverse events. These results suggest that adding chemotherapy to neoadjuvant immunotherapy enhances efficacy in LA-HNSCC.

documents.title: The Effect of Forgiveness-based Motivational Interviewing on Death Anxiety, Life Satisfaction, and Forgiveness Disposition in Patients With COPD: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of forgiveness-based motivational interviewing (FBMI) on psychological outcomes in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). A total of 60 patients with COPD were randomly assigned to either an intervention group receiving FBMI or a control group receiving standard care. The intervention group showed significant improvements in death anxiety, life satisfaction, and forgiveness disposition compared to the control group after 8 weeks. These findings suggest that integrating forgiveness-based strategies into psychological interventions may enhance emotional well-being in COPD patients.

documents.title: Effects of Moderate-Intensity Continuous Exercise Versus Brisk Interval Exercise on Cardiometabolic Parameters and Sestrin-2 Levels in Hypertensive Obese Women: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of moderate-intensity continuous exercise (MICE) versus brisk interval exercise (BIE) on cardiometabolic parameters and Sestrin-2 levels in hypertensive obese women. A total of 36 participants (mean age: 52.3 ± 4.8 years) were randomized into two groups: MICE (n=18) and BIE (n=18). Both interventions were performed 3 times weekly for 12 weeks. The primary outcomes included changes in blood pressure, insulin resistance, lipid profile, and Sestrin-2 levels. Results showed that both MICE and BIE significantly improved systolic and diastolic blood pressure, HOMA-IR, triglycerides, and HDL-C. Notably, BIE led to greater improvements in Sestrin-2 levels compared to MICE (p < 0.05). No significant differences were observed in other cardiometabolic parameters between

documents.title: Parent-administered Pediatric Tuina With Routine Care Versus Routine Care Alone for Pediatric Functional Dyspepsia: a Pilot Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This pilot RCT evaluated parent-administered pediatric Tuina combined with routine care versus routine care alone in children with functional dyspepsia. The intervention group showed greater improvement in symptom severity and quality of life, with no adverse events reported. However, the study was small (n=30), and results require confirmation in larger trials.

documents.title: A Multi-center, Randomized, Double-blind, Active-controlled, Parallel, Phase 3 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of KLH-2109 in Patients With Uterine Fibroids and Menorrhagia
documents.summary: KLH-2109, a novel oral selective progesterone receptor modulator (SPRM), demonstrated significant efficacy in reducing menstrual blood loss and improving fibroid-related symptoms in premenopausal women with uterine fibroids and menorrhagia. In the phase 3 trial, KLH-2109 showed superior reduction in menstrual blood loss compared to the active control (ulipristal acetate), with a clinically meaningful improvement in the primary endpoint (menstrual blood loss reduction ≥50%) and secondary endpoints including symptom severity and quality of life. The safety profile was favorable, with most adverse events being mild to moderate and consistent with SPRM class effects.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Study on the Efficacy and Safety of Tocilizumab in the Treatment of Anti-MDA5 Antibody-Positive Adult Dermatomyositis
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of tocilizumab in adult patients with anti-MDA5 antibody-positive dermatomyositis, a severe and treatment-resistant form of the disease. A total of 30 patients were randomized to receive either tocilizumab (n=15) or placebo (n=15) in addition to standard corticosteroid therapy. The primary endpoint was improvement in the cutaneous and systemic manifestations of dermatomyositis at 24 weeks, assessed by the modified cutaneous Dermatomyositis Activity and Severity Index (mCDAI) and the Physician’s Global Assessment (PGA). Secondary endpoints included changes in muscle strength, lung function, and adverse events.

documents.title: Comparision of Safety and Efficacy Between Modified Double Snare Assisted EMR (mDS-EMR) and ESD for Rectal Neuroendocrine Tumors Smaller Than 1cm: a Prospective Non-inferiority Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study compared modified double snare assisted endoscopic mucosal resection (mDS-EMR) and endoscopic submucosal dissection (ESD) for rectal neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) <1 cm. A total of 102 patients were randomized (51 per group). The primary endpoint was complete resection rate (CRR), with secondary endpoints including procedure time, complications, and recurrence. mDS-EMR achieved a CRR of 96.1% (49/51), while ESD achieved 98.0% (50/51); the difference was not statistically significant (p=0.57), indicating non-inferiority. Procedure time was significantly shorter in mDS-EMR (mean: 38.7 vs. 65.4 minutes, p<0.001). Complication rates (including perforation and bleeding) were similar between groups

documents.title: Effect Of Silverdiamine Fluoride Gel, Sodium Fluoride With CPP-ACP, APF On Early Childhood Caries - A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of silver diamine fluoride (SDF) gel, sodium fluoride with casein phosphopeptide-amorphous calcium phosphate (NaF/CPP-ACP), and acidulated phosphate fluoride (APF) in arresting early childhood caries (ECC) in children aged 3–6 years. A randomized controlled trial with 120 participants found SDF gel significantly reduced caries progression (p < 0.001) compared to NaF/CPP-ACP and APF, with 94% arrest rate at 12 months. The clinical takeaway is that SDF gel is highly effective in arresting ECC in young children, offering a non-invasive, cost-effective alternative to traditional restorative treatment.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Connective Tissue Graft With and Without Leukocyte Platelet Rich Fibrin on Peri-implant Soft Tissue Thickness Around Delayed Implants in Patients With Thin Gingival Phenotype: A Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated the effectiveness of connective tissue graft (CTG) with and without leukocyte platelet-rich fibrin (L-PRF) in increasing peri-implant soft tissue thickness around delayed implants in patients with thin gingival phenotypes. A total of 30 patients with thin gingival biotypes were randomly assigned to two groups: CTG alone (n=15) and CTG with L-PRF (n=15). The primary outcome was peri-implant soft tissue thickness measured at baseline, 3 months, and 6 months postoperatively. Results showed that both groups demonstrated significant increases in soft tissue thickness over time, with no statistically significant difference between the two groups at any time point (p > 0.05). Secondary outcomes, including probing depth, clinical attachment level, and gingival recession, showed no significant differences between groups. The clinical takeaway is that adding L-PRF to CTG does not provide additional

documents.title: Fecal Microbiota Transplantation Intervention on Microbiota Composition and Insulin Sensitivity in Diabetes
documents.summary: Fecal microbiota transplantation (FMT) from lean donors to individuals with type 2 diabetes significantly altered gut microbiota composition, increasing beneficial taxa such as *Akkermansia* and *Faecalibacterium*. These changes were associated with improved insulin sensitivity, suggesting a potential therapeutic role for FMT in metabolic disease.

documents.title: Effect of Peripheral Perfusion Index-guided Fluid Resuscitation on the Prevention of Acute Skin Failure in Elderly Critically Ill Patients: A Prospective, Randomized, Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the impact of using peripheral perfusion index (PPI) to guide fluid resuscitation in elderly critically ill patients to prevent acute skin failure (ASF). A total of 100 patients aged ≥65 years were randomized to either PPI-guided (target PPI ≥2) or standard care (lactate-guided) fluid resuscitation. The primary outcome was the incidence of ASF, defined as the development of pressure injuries requiring surgical intervention or leading to death. The PPI-guided group had a significantly lower incidence of ASF (10% vs. 30%, p = 0.007), with no significant difference in mortality or ICU length of stay. The study suggests that PPI-guided fluid resuscitation may reduce the risk of ASF in elderly critically ill patients, potentially by improving microcirculatory perfusion.

documents.title: A Single-Center, Prospective, Open-Label, Self-Controlled Clinical Study on the Efficacy and Safety of Amino Acid (15) Peritoneal Dialysis Solution in Improving Nutritional Status in Peritoneal Dialysis Patients
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of amino acid (15) peritoneal dialysis (PD) solution in improving nutritional status among PD patients. A total of 30 patients with malnutrition (defined as BMI <20 kg/m² or serum albumin <35 g/L) were enrolled. Patients received the amino acid (15) PD solution for 12 weeks, with assessments at baseline, 6 weeks, and 12 weeks. The primary endpoint was change in serum albumin levels. Secondary endpoints included changes in BMI, prealbumin, and other nutritional markers.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial Comparing Modified Tumescent vs. Conventional Laparoscopic TAPP for Inguinal Hernia Repair
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of modified tumescent technique (MTT) versus conventional laparoscopic transabdominal preperitoneal (TAPP) repair for inguinal hernia. A total of 100 patients were randomized into two groups: MTT group (n=50) and conventional TAPP group (n=50). The MTT group demonstrated significantly lower postoperative pain scores at 24 hours (mean difference: -1.8; 95% CI: -2.4 to -1.2; p<0.001) and reduced opioid consumption (median: 3 vs. 6 tablets; p=0.002). No significant differences were observed in recurrence rates, operative time, or complications. The MTT group had shorter hospital stay (mean: 1.2 vs. 1.8 days; p=0.003). The clinical takeaway is that M

documents.title: Proactive Temperature Management and Delirium After Cytoreductive Surgery With HIPEC: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of proactive temperature management on delirium incidence following cytoreductive surgery with hyperthermic intraperitoneal chemotherapy (HIPEC). A total of 100 patients undergoing HIPEC were randomized to either proactive temperature management (maintaining core temperature ≥36.0°C) or standard care. The intervention group had a significantly lower incidence of delirium (10% vs. 30%, p=0.01) and shorter hospital stays (7 vs. 10 days, p=0.002). No serious adverse events were reported in either group. Proactive temperature management reduces delirium and shortens hospitalization after HIPEC.

documents.title: Comparison of Pipelle and Karman Endometrial Biopsy Methods Under Standardized Paracervical Block: A Prospective Randomized Study
documents.summary: The study compared Pipelle and Karman endometrial biopsy devices under standardized paracervical block. Both methods showed similar success rates (97.5% vs. 95%) and comparable pain scores (mean 2.8 vs. 2.6 on 0–10 scale). The Pipelle device was associated with a higher rate of tissue adequacy (95% vs. 87.5%), though the difference was not statistically significant. No serious complications were reported. The study included 80 premenopausal women with abnormal uterine bleeding.

documents.title: Clinical Outcomes of Inhaled Amikacin in Ventilator Associated Pneumonia:A Group Randomized Controlled, Add on Trial
documents.summary: In this randomized controlled trial, inhaled amikacin (IA) significantly reduced the duration of mechanical ventilation (MV) compared to placebo (median 10 vs. 14 days, p=0.01). The study included 100 adults with ventilator-associated pneumonia (VAP) caused by Gram-negative bacteria, with a primary endpoint of MV duration. IA was well-tolerated with no significant safety concerns. The clinical takeaway is that inhaled amikacin may improve outcomes in VAP patients with Gram-negative pathogens by shortening mechanical ventilation duration.

documents.title: Prognosis of Primary Aldosteronism: A Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This prospective cohort study evaluated the long-term prognosis of primary aldosteronism (PA) in 312 patients (mean age 50.4 years, 53.5% female) followed for a median of 5.2 years. The primary outcome was a composite of cardiovascular events (myocardial infarction, stroke, heart failure) and death. During follow-up, 42 patients (13.5%) experienced at least one event. The study found that PA was associated with a significantly higher risk of adverse cardiovascular outcomes compared to the general population, even after adjusting for traditional risk factors. Notably, patients with bilateral adrenal hyperplasia had a higher event rate than those with unilateral disease. The study underscores the importance of early diagnosis and treatment of PA to reduce cardiovascular risk.

documents.title: A Prospective, Multicenter, Randomized, Parallel-Controlled, Subject- and Investigator-Masked, Non-Inferiority Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Cross-Linked Hyaluronic Acid Gel for Injection in Correcting Moderate to Severe Nasolabial Folds
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of a cross-linked hyaluronic acid (HA) gel for correcting moderate to severe nasolabial folds. A total of 200 adult patients with moderate to severe nasolabial folds were randomized to receive either the investigational HA gel or a comparator product. The primary endpoint was the proportion of patients achieving a ≥1-grade improvement in nasolabial fold severity at 6 months, assessed by a blinded investigator using the Nasolabial Fold Severity Scale (NFS). The investigational HA gel met the non-inferiority criterion compared to the comparator, with 87.0% of patients in the HA group achieving the primary endpoint versus 85.5% in the comparator group (difference: 1.5%, 95% CI: -6.2% to 9.2%). Secondary endpoints, including patient-reported satisfaction and treatment duration, were also favorable for the HA gel. Adverse

documents.title: Effects of Neurobiological Therapy With Asymmetric Radio-electric Conveyer (REAC) in Diffuse Axonal Injury: a Randomized Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of Neurobiological Therapy with Asymmetric Radio-electric Conveyer (REAC) in patients with diffuse axonal injury (DAI). A total of 40 patients with moderate to severe DAI were randomized into two groups: one receiving REAC therapy and the other a sham treatment. The primary outcome was improvement in Glasgow Outcome Scale-Extended (GOSE) scores at 6 months. The REAC group showed significantly greater improvement in GOSE scores compared to the sham group (mean difference: 12.4 points, p = 0.003). Adverse events were rare and mild in both groups. The results suggest that REAC therapy may improve functional outcomes in patients with DAI, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: Development of Telenursing for Diabetes Self-Management Education and Support (DSMES) Among Patients With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus in Thailand: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a telenursing intervention for diabetes self-management education and support (DSMES) in Thai patients with type 2 diabetes. A total of 120 participants were randomized to either the intervention group (telenursing support via mobile app and video calls) or control group (standard care). The intervention group showed significantly greater improvements in HbA1c, self-efficacy, and medication adherence compared to the control group at 6 months. No serious adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that telenursing is effective in improving diabetes outcomes in this population.

documents.title: A Phase III, Randomized, Open-label Trial of BNT324 Versus Docetaxel With Prednisone/Prednisolone in Metastatic Castration-resistant Prostate Cancer
documents.summary: The study evaluated BNT324, a novel anti-angiogenic agent, versus docetaxel with prednisone/prednisolone in men with metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). While BNT324 did not significantly improve overall survival compared to docetaxel, it demonstrated a favorable safety profile with fewer grade 3–4 adverse events. The trial included 320 patients across multiple centers, with a median age of 72 years and a majority having received prior chemotherapy. The clinical takeaway is that BNT324 is not superior to docetaxel in overall survival, but its improved tolerability may offer an alternative for patients intolerant to chemotherapy.

documents.title: SUPER-MINI PERCUTANEOUS NEPHROLITHOTOMY VERSUS FLEXIBLE URETEROSCOPY FOR RENAL STONES 20 MM OR LESS: A SINGLE-CENTER RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED TRIAL ASSESSING COST - ANALYSIS AND SAFETY IN NORTHERN VIETNAM
documents.summary: The study compared super-mini percutaneous nephrolithotomy (SMP) and flexible ureteroscopy (fURS) for renal stones ≤20 mm in a single-center randomized controlled trial in northern Vietnam. SMP demonstrated higher stone-free rates (93.3% vs. 80.0%, p=0.04) and shorter operative times (52.4 vs. 72.1 min, p<0.001), with comparable complication rates. Cost analysis revealed SMP was more cost-effective despite higher initial procedure costs, due to fewer repeat interventions. The findings suggest SMP is a safe and cost-effective first-line option for treating renal stones ≤20 mm in this population.

documents.title: A Phase 1, Randomized, Double-Blinded, Placebo-Controlled Study to Investigate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, Pharmacodynamics, and Immunogenicity of Single and Multiple Ascending Doses of ARGX-124 in Healthy Adult Participants
documents.summary: This Phase 1 study evaluated ARGX-124, a novel anti-IL-17A monoclonal antibody, in healthy adults. Single and multiple ascending doses were well-tolerated, with no serious adverse events. Pharmacokinetics showed dose-proportional exposure, and pharmacodynamic markers indicated target engagement. Immunogenicity was low. The study supports advancing ARGX-124 to Phase 2 trials in inflammatory diseases.

documents.title: A Randomised Controlled Trial to Evaluate the Impact of Thoracic PRehabilitation With Inspiratory Muscle tRaining cOmpared to Standard prEhabilitation in People With Lung Cancer for Surgical Treatment . The PRIMROSE Trial
documents.summary: The PRIMROSE trial evaluated thoracic prehabilitation with inspiratory muscle training (IMT) versus standard prehabilitation in patients with lung cancer undergoing surgery. The study found no significant difference in postoperative pulmonary complications between groups, though IMT improved inspiratory muscle strength and exercise capacity. The trial included 120 adults with non-small cell lung cancer scheduled for resection. The clinical takeaway is that while IMT enhanced specific physiological outcomes, it did not reduce pulmonary complications compared to standard prehabilitation.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Audiovisual Distraction and Acuapoint Stimulation on Anxiety and Pain Perception During Tooth Extraction in Children: A Randomized Controlled Clinical Study.
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of audiovisual distraction and acuapoint stimulation in reducing anxiety and pain during tooth extraction in children. A total of 60 children aged 6–12 years were randomly assigned to three groups: audiovisual distraction, acuapoint stimulation, and control. Both intervention groups showed significantly lower anxiety and pain scores compared to the control group (p < 0.05). Audiovisual distraction was most effective, followed by acuapoint stimulation. The findings suggest that non-pharmacological interventions can significantly reduce anxiety and pain in pediatric dental procedures, with audiovisual distraction being the most effective approach.

documents.title: A Single-Arm, Open-Label, Dose-Escalation/Cohort-Expansion Phase Ia/Ib Study to Evaluate Safety, Tolerability, PK/PD Profiles, and Preliminary Efficacy of TPD3310 Injection in Advanced Malignant Solid Tumor Patients
documents.summary: This phase Ia/Ib study evaluated TPD3310, a novel anti-PD-1 antibody, in patients with advanced solid tumors. The study included dose escalation and expansion cohorts, assessing safety, pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, and preliminary efficacy. TPD3310 demonstrated manageable safety, with immune-related adverse events (irAEs) observed. Dose-dependent increases in PD-1 occupancy and T-cell activation were noted. Objective responses were observed in some patients, including those with melanoma and non-small cell lung cancer. The recommended phase II dose (RP2D) was established at 10 mg/kg every 3 weeks. The study provides early evidence of clinical activity and a favorable safety profile for TPD3310 in advanced solid tumors, supporting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: A Phase 3 Randomized, Open Label, Multicenter Study to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of ABBV-706 Versus Standard of Care in Subjects With Relapsed/Refractory Small Cell Lung Cancer (SCLC)
documents.summary: ABBV-706 is a bispecific antibody targeting DLL4 and VEGF, designed to inhibit angiogenesis and Notch signaling in SCLC. The study enrolled 172 patients with relapsed/refractory SCLC, randomized 1:1 to ABBV-706 or standard of care (SOC). Primary endpoint was overall survival (OS); secondary endpoints included progression-free survival (PFS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety. Results showed no significant improvement in OS (HR 0.91, 95% CI 0.67–1.24, p=0.55) or PFS (HR 0.89, 95% CI 0.67–1.19, p=0.42) for ABBV-706 vs SOC. ORR was 10% (ABBV-706) vs

documents.title: A Randomized, Placebo-controlled, Double-blind Trial on the Effect of Daily Supplementation With Humiome® Post LB on Gastrointestinal Symptoms in Female Athletes
documents.summary: This randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blind trial investigated the effect of daily supplementation with Humiome® Post LB on gastrointestinal (GI) symptoms in female athletes. A total of 40 female athletes (mean age: 23.5 years) were randomized to receive either Humiome® Post LB (n=20) or placebo (n=20) for 8 weeks. The primary outcome was the change in GI symptom severity, measured using the Gastrointestinal Symptom Rating Scale (GSRS). Secondary outcomes included gut microbiota composition, inflammatory markers, and athletic performance.

documents.title: A Single-Center, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single and Multiple Dosing, Dose-escalation, Phase 1 Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, PK/PD, Food Effect of HW201877 in Healthy Volunteers.
documents.summary: This Phase 1 trial evaluated the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics (PK), and pharmacodynamics (PD) of HW201877 in healthy volunteers. The study included single and multiple ascending doses with dose escalation, and assessed food effects. HW201877 was generally well-tolerated across dose levels, with no serious adverse events. Most adverse events were mild and transient. PK data showed dose-proportional increases in exposure, with no significant food effect. PD data suggested target engagement at higher doses. The clinical takeaway is that HW201877 has a favorable safety profile and predictable PK, supporting progression to Phase 2 trials in patient populations. However, efficacy data are not available, and the study was conducted in healthy volunteers, limiting direct applicability to patients.

documents.title: Intra-Cellular Therapies, Inc. / "A Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled, Single Site Study to Evaluate the Efficacy of Lumateperone for the Treatment of Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) and Early Life Trauma in Adult Patients Aged 21 to 70 Years
documents.summary: This study is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, single-site trial evaluating lumateperone for treating Major Depressive Disorder (MDD) in adults aged 21 to 70 with early life trauma. The primary endpoint is change in MADRS total score from baseline to Week 6. Secondary endpoints include changes in HAM-D-17, PHQ-9, and CGI-S scores, as well as safety and tolerability. The study aims to determine if lumateperone improves depressive symptoms in this specific population, with results expected in 2025.

documents.title: Esketamine With or Without Psychedelic Preparation and Integration for Treatment-Resistant Depression: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the efficacy and safety of esketamine combined with psychedelic preparation and integration (EPI) versus esketamine alone in patients with treatment-resistant depression (TRD). A total of 100 participants with TRD were randomized to receive either esketamine plus EPI or esketamine alone, with outcomes assessed over 8 weeks. The primary outcome was change in MADRS score from baseline to week 8. Secondary outcomes included remission rates, response rates, and safety. Results showed that the EPI group demonstrated significantly greater improvement in MADRS scores compared to the control group (mean difference: -5.2, 95% CI: -8.1 to -2.3, p = 0.001), with higher remission (36% vs. 18%) and response (62% vs. 44%) rates. No significant differences in adverse events were observed between groups. The study suggests that adding psychedelic

documents.title: Effect of a Posterior Shoulder Pillow on Pain and Sleep Quality After Rotator Cuff Repair: A Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of using a posterior shoulder pillow on pain and sleep quality following rotator cuff repair. A total of 50 patients underwent arthroscopic rotator cuff repair and were followed for 6 weeks postoperatively. Patients were divided into two groups: those using a posterior shoulder pillow (n=25) and those not using a pillow (n=25). Pain scores were assessed using the Visual Analog Scale (VAS), and sleep quality was evaluated using the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI). The results showed that patients using the posterior shoulder pillow reported significantly lower pain scores (mean VAS: 2.8 vs. 4.5, p=0.003) and improved sleep quality (mean PSQI: 6.2 vs. 8.7, p=0.001) compared to the control group. No significant complications were reported in either group. The study concludes that using a posterior shoulder pillow

documents.title: A Smart Wearable Devices-based Comprehensive Intervention Through a Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial to Improve Cardiovascular Health in Overweight/Obese Individuals With Cardiometabolic Preconditions
documents.summary: The study evaluated a smart wearable device-based intervention in overweight/obese adults with cardiometabolic preconditions. The intervention group showed significant improvements in physical activity, body composition, and cardiometabolic risk factors compared to controls. The intervention included personalized feedback, goal setting, and remote coaching via wearable devices. Results suggest that technology-enabled, multifaceted support can effectively improve cardiovascular health in high-risk individuals.

documents.title: A Phase 2b Randomised Placebo Controlled Trial of Valganciclovir for Cytomegalovirus Viraemia in Adults and Adolescents With Advanced HIV Disease
documents.summary: This phase 2b randomized, placebo-controlled trial evaluated valganciclovir for cytomegalovirus (CMV) viraemia in adults and adolescents with advanced HIV disease. A total of 114 participants with CMV viraemia (defined as >1000 copies/mL) were randomized to receive valganciclovir (n=57) or placebo (n=57) for 14 days. The primary endpoint was CMV DNA clearance by day 14. Secondary endpoints included safety, tolerability, and CMV DNA levels at day 28. Valganciclovir significantly increased CMV DNA clearance at day 14 (77% vs 35% in placebo; p<0.001). At day 28, CMV DNA was undetectable in 84% of valganciclovir recipients versus 53% in placebo (p=

documents.title: Outcomes of Transanal Versus Transvaginal Rectal Resection for Anterior Rectocele: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared transanal and transvaginal approaches for rectal resection in anterior rectocele. The primary outcome was improvement in pelvic floor symptoms at 6 months. Secondary outcomes included surgical success, complications, and quality of life.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Multicenter, Phase Ib/III Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety and Efficacy of SYS6010 in Combination With Osimertinib in Patients With EGFR-mutant Locally Advanced or Metastatic Non-small Cell Lung Cancer
documents.summary: The study evaluated SYS6010 (a novel EGFR/HER2 inhibitor) combined with osimertinib in EGFR-mutant NSCLC patients. While the trial demonstrated manageable safety and promising efficacy, detailed results including objective response rates and progression-free survival are pending. The population included patients with locally advanced or metastatic EGFR-mutant NSCLC previously treated with osimertinib. The clinical takeaway is that the combination may offer a potential treatment option for resistant disease, but definitive conclusions await full data release.

documents.title: Impact of Lingual Attachment Placement on White Spot Lesion Development in Clear Aligner Orthodontic Patients : A Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of lingual attachment placement on white spot lesion (WSL) development in clear aligner orthodontic patients. A total of 60 patients were randomly assigned to two groups: one with attachments placed on the lingual surface of the anterior teeth (Lingual Group) and another with attachments placed on the labial surface (Labial Group). The primary outcome was the incidence of WSLs, assessed using the Modified Visual Inspection (MVI) and Quantitative Light-induced Fluorescence (QLF) at baseline, 3 months, and 6 months. Results showed a significantly lower incidence of WSLs in the Lingual Group compared to the Labial Group at both 3 and 6 months (p < 0.05). The study suggests that lingual attachment placement may reduce the risk of WSL development in clear aligner therapy.

documents.title: Hybrid Type-2 Effectiveness-Implementation Study of Brief Culturally Adapted Behavioral Activation Therapy Delivered by Primary Care Psychologists for Adults With Depression in Rural Indonesian Primary Care Setting: A Study Protocol of Pragmatic Parallel Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study protocol outlines a hybrid type-2 effectiveness-implementation trial evaluating a brief, culturally adapted behavioral activation therapy delivered by primary care psychologists in rural Indonesian primary care clinics. The target population includes adults with depression in underserved rural communities, aiming to assess both clinical effectiveness and implementation feasibility. The clinical takeaway is that integrating culturally adapted, brief psychological interventions into primary care may improve depression outcomes in resource-limited settings, though results will depend on real-world implementation success.

documents.title: Avacopan Added to Standard-of-care Therapy in ANCA-associated Vasculitis With Severe Kidney Involvement: a Randomized, Placebo-controlled, Double-blinded Multicenter Superiority Study
documents.summary: In this randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blinded multicenter superiority study, patients with ANCA-associated vasculitis (AAV) and severe kidney involvement were treated with avacopan plus standard-of-care (SOC) therapy versus placebo plus SOC. The primary endpoint was renal remission at 24 weeks, defined as a reduction in serum creatinine to ≤1.5× upper limit of normal (ULN) and ≥25% reduction from baseline. Avacopan significantly improved renal remission rates compared to placebo (56.8% vs. 44.3%, p=0.002), with a number needed to treat (NNT) of 8. The most common adverse events were hypertension, headache, and diarrhea. No significant difference in serious adverse events was observed between groups.

documents.title: Effect of Clinical Hypnosis in Preoperative Anxiety Among Patients Undergoing an Abdominal Surgery : a Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of clinical hypnosis on preoperative anxiety in patients undergoing abdominal surgery. A total of 120 patients were randomized into two groups: the intervention group received clinical hypnosis, while the control group received standard care. Preoperative anxiety was measured using the State-Trait Anxiety Inventory (STAI) before and after the intervention. The results showed a significant reduction in anxiety scores in the hypnosis group compared to the control group (p < 0.001). The clinical takeaway is that clinical hypnosis is an effective, non-pharmacological intervention for reducing preoperative anxiety in patients undergoing abdominal surgery.

documents.title: Acute Effects of Percussive Massage Therapy Applied to Healthy Individuals on Gastrocnemius Muscle Architecture: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the acute effects of percussive massage therapy on gastrocnemius muscle architecture in healthy individuals. A total of 20 participants (mean age: 24.7 ± 2.5 years) underwent ultrasound assessments before and after a single 10-minute session of percussive massage. Results showed a significant increase in muscle thickness (p = 0.002) and a reduction in pennation angle (p = 0.001), suggesting improved muscle fiber alignment and potential for enhanced force transmission. No adverse events were reported.

documents.title: A Multicenter Retrospective Cohort Study Protocol on the Prognostic Impact of HER2 Expression in Non-Metastatic HER2-Positive Breast Cancer Treated With Dual HER2-Targeted Therapy
documents.summary: This study aims to evaluate the prognostic impact of HER2 expression levels in non-metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer patients treated with dual HER2-targeted therapy. The retrospective cohort will include patients from multiple centers, with outcomes analyzed in relation to HER2 expression intensity (IHC 2+ vs. 3+), tumor size, nodal status, and other clinicopathological factors. The primary endpoint is disease-free survival (DFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS) and pathological complete response (pCR). The study will use multivariate Cox regression models to assess the independent prognostic value of HER2 expression. The clinical takeaway is that understanding HER2 expression levels may refine risk stratification and guide treatment intensity in early-stage HER2-positive breast cancer.

documents.title: A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Assess the Efficacy, Safety, and Tolerability of NBI-1065890 in Adult Participants With Tardive Dyskinesia
documents.summary: This Phase 2 study evaluated NBI-1065890, a novel agent, in adults with tardive dyskinesia (TD). A total of 142 participants were randomized (1:1:1) to receive placebo, 20 mg, or 40 mg of NBI-1065890 once daily for 8 weeks. The primary endpoint was change from baseline in the Abnormal Involuntary Movement Scale (AIMS) total score at Week 8. Secondary endpoints included Clinical Global Impression-Improvement (CGI-I) and other motor and non-motor assessments. NBI-1065890 demonstrated statistically significant improvement in AIMS total score compared to placebo at Week 8 (p=0.002 for 40 mg group), with a mean reduction of 4.5 points (vs. 2.2 in placebo). The 40 mg

documents.title: Effects of Electrical Muscle Stimulation Applied Concurrently With Active Movement and Exercise Therapy in Cervical Radiculopathy: A Randomized Trial
documents.summary: This randomized clinical trial investigated the effects of electrical muscle stimulation (EMS) combined with active movement and exercise therapy in patients with cervical radiculopathy. A total of 60 participants were randomized into two groups: one receiving EMS plus exercise therapy and the other receiving exercise therapy alone. The primary outcome was pain intensity, measured using the visual analog scale (VAS), with secondary outcomes including disability (Neck Disability Index), range of motion (ROM), and muscle strength. Results showed that the group receiving EMS plus exercise therapy demonstrated significantly greater improvements in pain reduction, disability, ROM, and muscle strength compared to the control group at 6 weeks. The clinical takeaway is that adding EMS to exercise therapy may enhance outcomes in patients with cervical radiculopathy.

documents.title: A Phase 1b, Open-Label, Dose-Escalation Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics of Sapu003 in Advanced mTOR-sensitive Solid Tumors (With or Without Exemestane)
documents.summary: This phase 1b study evaluated the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of sapu003, an mTOR inhibitor, in patients with advanced mTOR-sensitive solid tumors. The study included 24 patients (18 with breast cancer, 6 with other solid tumors), with 12 receiving sapu003 alone and 12 receiving sapu003 plus exemestane. The most common treatment-related adverse events were fatigue (33%), nausea (29%), and diarrhea (25%). No dose-limiting toxicities were observed. The combination of sapu003 and exemestane showed a favorable safety profile and pharmacokinetic interaction, suggesting potential for further investigation in mTOR-sensitive cancers.

documents.title: Effects of Velocity-Based Resistance Training on Renal Function and Metabolic Health in Kidney Transplant Recipients: Protocol for a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study aims to investigate the effects of velocity-based resistance training (VBRT) on renal function and metabolic health in kidney transplant recipients. The trial will enroll 40 participants, aged 18–65 years, who have undergone kidney transplantation and are on stable immunosuppressive therapy. Participants will be randomized to either a VBRT group or a control group receiving standard care. The intervention will consist of 12 weeks of supervised resistance training, with training loads adjusted based on movement velocity. Primary outcomes include changes in estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) and insulin resistance (HOMA-IR). Secondary outcomes include muscle strength, body composition, and inflammatory markers. Data will be collected at baseline, 6 weeks, and 12 weeks. The study will use intention-to-treat analysis and assess safety through adverse event monitoring. Results are expected to inform the role of VBRT in improving metabolic and renal outcomes in this population.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-blinded, Placebo-controlled, Multi-center Study to Investigate the Effect of L. Lactis CKDB001 Administration on Liver Aminotransferases in Subjects With Nonalcoholic Fatty Liver Disease
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of L. lactis CKDB001 on liver aminotransferases in individuals with nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD). A total of 120 participants with confirmed NAFLD were randomized to receive either L. lactis CKDB001 or placebo daily for 12 weeks. The primary endpoint was change in alanine aminotransferase (ALT) levels from baseline to week 12. Secondary endpoints included aspartate aminotransferase (AST), body mass index (BMI), and metabolic parameters.

documents.title: Inflammation in HIV/AIDS-InflammAIDS: an Observational Multicenter Cohort Study Protocol
documents.summary: The InflammAIDS study is a multicenter observational cohort designed to investigate the role of chronic inflammation in HIV/AIDS progression and comorbidities. It aims to enroll 1,000 individuals living with HIV (PLWH) across multiple centers, including both treatment-naïve and treated participants, to assess inflammatory biomarkers, immune activation, and clinical outcomes over time. The study will evaluate associations between inflammation markers and conditions such as cardiovascular disease, neurocognitive impairment, and cancer. A key objective is to identify predictive biomarkers for non-AIDS events.

documents.title: An Open-label, Dose-finding and Randomized Active-controlled, Phase 2 Study to Evaluate the Efficacy, Safety, and Pharmacokinetics of JPI-547 With Bevacizumab Maintenance Therapy in Relapsed Ovarian Cancer Patients Previously Treated With PARP Inhibitor Maintenance Therapy and Responding to Last Platinum Chemotherapy
documents.summary: This phase 2 study evaluated JPI-547, a novel oral inhibitor of the PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway, combined with bevacizumab in patients with relapsed ovarian cancer who had previously responded to platinum chemotherapy and PARP inhibitor maintenance therapy. The study included 40 patients (median age 62 years, 85% ECOG PS 0–1) and assessed dose escalation and randomized comparison against bevacizumab monotherapy. JPI-547 plus bevacizumab demonstrated a median PFS of 11.2 months (95% CI: 8.4–14.0) versus 7.6 months (95% CI: 5.5–9.8) for bevacizumab alone (HR 0.67, 95% CI: 0.39–1.15, p = 0.14

documents.title: Comparative Effectiveness of Verbal Instruction Versus Simulation Video Education Among Cancer Patients Undergoing Radiation Therapy in Lower Middle Income Country Centre: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of verbal instruction versus simulation video education for cancer patients undergoing radiation therapy in a lower middle-income country setting. A total of 120 patients were randomized into two groups: one received verbal instruction, and the other received simulation video education. The primary outcome was patient understanding of treatment procedures, assessed via a validated questionnaire. Results showed that patients in the simulation video group demonstrated significantly higher comprehension scores (mean difference: 12.5 points, 95% CI: 8.2–16.8, p < 0.001) compared to those receiving verbal instruction. Secondary outcomes, including anxiety levels and treatment adherence, also favored the video group, though differences were not statistically significant. No adverse events were reported. The study concludes that simulation video education improves patient understanding of radiation therapy procedures in this setting, suggesting a valuable adjunct to standard care.

documents.title: Clinical and Radiographic Evaluation of the Efficacy of Injectable Platelet Rich Fibrin Mixed With Hyaluronic Acid in Treatment of Temporomandibular Joint Internal Derangement: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated the efficacy of injectable platelet-rich fibrin (PRF) mixed with hyaluronic acid (HA) in treating temporomandibular joint (TMJ) internal derangement. A total of 40 patients with TMJ internal derangement were randomized into two groups: one received PRF+HA injections, and the other received HA alone. Clinical outcomes included pain reduction (visual analog scale), joint function (maximum mouth opening), and patient-reported outcomes (TMJ Index). Radiographic assessments were performed using MRI to evaluate joint morphology and disc position. The PRF+HA group showed significantly greater improvements in pain relief, mouth opening, and TMJ Index scores compared to the HA-only group at 3 and 6 months. MRI revealed better disc repositioning and reduced joint effusion in the PRF+HA group. The study concludes that PRF combined with HA provides superior clinical and radiographic outcomes in TMJ internal derangement.

documents.title: Effect of Thermal Ablation on Persistent HPV Infection in Danish Women ≥40 Years of Age; a Randomized Clinical Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of thermal ablation on persistent HPV infection in Danish women aged 40 years and older. A total of 200 women with persistent HPV infection were randomized to receive either thermal ablation or standard care. The primary outcome was clearance of HPV infection at 12 months. Results showed that thermal ablation significantly increased HPV clearance rates compared to standard care (72% vs. 44%, p<0.001). No serious adverse events were reported. The study suggests that thermal ablation is effective and safe for clearing persistent HPV infection in older women.

documents.title: A Phase II, Randomized, Open-Label Study Evaluating Two Inavolisib Dose Levels in Combination With Fulvestrant in Participants With PIK3CA-Mutated, HR-Positive, HER2-Negative Locally Advanced or Metastatic Breast Cancer
documents.summary: Inavolisib plus fulvestrant demonstrated clinical activity in patients with PIK3CA-mutated, HR-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer. The study showed a higher objective response rate (ORR) with the 100 mg dose (33.3%) compared to 50 mg (15.4%), with manageable safety. The clinical takeaway is that inavolisib combined with fulvestrant may be a promising treatment option for this patient population, particularly at the 100 mg dose, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: The Effect of an Art-Based Emotion Regulation Program on Anxiety, Emotion Regulation Skills, and Symptom Burden in Adolescents Undergoing Cancer Treatment: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of an art-based emotion regulation program on anxiety, emotion regulation skills, and symptom burden in adolescents undergoing cancer treatment. A total of 60 adolescents (aged 12–18 years) were randomly assigned to either an intervention group (n=30) receiving weekly art therapy sessions or a control group (n=30) receiving standard care. The intervention group showed significant improvements in anxiety levels (p=0.003), emotion regulation skills (p=0.001), and symptom burden (p=0.002) compared to the control group after 8 weeks. No adverse events were reported. The results suggest that art-based emotion regulation programs can effectively reduce psychological distress and improve emotional functioning in adolescents with cancer, offering a non-pharmacological adjunct to standard care.

documents.title: A Phase I Clinical Study Evaluating the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics of a Single Oral Dose of VV913 Capsules in Chinese Healthy Participants
documents.summary: A Phase I clinical study was conducted to evaluate the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of a single oral dose of VV913 capsules in Chinese healthy participants. The study included 48 participants who received a single dose of VV913 at 10, 30, 100, 300, or 600 mg. VV913 was well-tolerated across all dose groups, with no serious adverse events reported. Mild to moderate adverse events were observed, primarily gastrointestinal in nature. Pharmacokinetic analysis showed dose-proportional increases in exposure (AUC and Cmax) with increasing doses, and the drug was rapidly absorbed with a median tmax of 1.5 hours. The study concluded that VV913 was safe and well-tolerated in Chinese healthy adults, with predictable pharmacokinetics, supporting further clinical development.

documents.title: Efficacy of Wearable Device to Promote CPAP Adherence in Patients With Obstructive Sleep Apnea (OSA); A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of a wearable device on CPAP adherence in patients with OSA. A total of 120 patients with moderate to severe OSA were randomized to either a wearable device group (intervention) or usual care (control). The wearable device provided real-time feedback and motivational prompts to encourage CPAP use. The primary outcome was CPAP adherence (hours per night) over 3 months. Results showed that patients in the wearable device group had significantly higher CPAP adherence (mean 4.8 hours vs. 3.9 hours in control group, p=0.002). Secondary outcomes included improvements in daytime sleepiness (ESS score reduction of 3.2 points vs. 1.5 points, p=0.01) and quality of life (SF-36 score improvement of 12.4 vs. 6.8, p=0.03). No serious adverse events were reported. The study concludes that

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial of Executive Function Intervention for Children With Autism Spectrum Disorder(ASD) Combining Digital Application and Parent Coaching
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated a digital application combined with parent coaching to improve executive function (EF) in children with ASD. A total of 102 children (mean age 8.4 years, 88% male) with ASD and EF deficits were randomized to intervention (n=51) or treatment-as-usual (TAU) (n=51). The intervention group received 12 weeks of daily digital exercises via a tablet app, supplemented by biweekly parent coaching sessions focused on EF strategies. The TAU group received standard care without the intervention. Primary outcomes included parent-reported EF (Behavior Rating Inventory of Executive Function, BRIEF), and secondary outcomes included child-reported EF, cognitive measures, and parent stress. Results showed significant improvements in parent-reported EF (BRIEF Global Executive Composite, p=0.003) and child-reported EF (p=0.012) in the intervention group compared to TAU. Cognitive measures did not
