documents.title: The Effect of a Digital Literacy Program on Problematic Internet Use, Emotion Regulation, and Loneliness in Adolescents: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated a digital literacy program among 12- to 18-year-old adolescents in a school setting. Results showed significant reductions in problematic internet use, improvements in emotion regulation, and decreased loneliness compared to the control group. The intervention was delivered over eight weekly sessions and included interactive modules on digital well-being, self-regulation, and healthy online behaviors. These findings suggest that structured digital literacy programs can effectively address key psychological challenges in adolescents linked to excessive internet use.

documents.title: Circadian Modulation of Neurophysiological Responses to Therapeutic Massage Modalities in Taekwondo Athletes: A Randomized Crossover Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of therapeutic massage on neurophysiological responses in Taekwondo athletes across different circadian times. Using a randomized crossover design, participants received massage at 08:00 and 20:00 hours, with outcomes measured via EEG, HRV, and salivary cortisol. Results showed significant circadian modulation: massage at 08:00 enhanced alpha wave activity and HRV, indicating greater parasympathetic activation, while 20:00 massage reduced cortisol levels more effectively. No significant differences in EEG or HRV were observed between time points. The study suggests that timing of massage may influence its physiological effects, with morning sessions promoting relaxation and evening sessions aiding recovery.

documents.title: Randomized Trial on DBS Programming Based on Biomarkers vs. Standard Monopolar Review
documents.summary: This study evaluated a novel deep brain stimulation (DBS) programming approach using real-time biomarker feedback (local field potentials, LFPs) compared to standard monopolar review in Parkinson’s disease (PD) patients. The trial randomized 40 participants to either biomarker-guided programming (n=20) or standard care (n=20), with outcomes assessed at 3 months. Biomarker-guided programming significantly improved motor scores (UPDRS-III) by 18.5 points (95% CI: 12.1–24.9) versus 7.2 points (95% CI: 3.1–11.3) in the control group (p=0.001). No significant differences were observed in adverse events. The clinical takeaway is that biomarker-guided DBS programming leads to superior motor outcomes compared to standard monopolar review, suggesting a potential paradigm shift in DBS

documents.title: Effectiveness of the Online Video-feedback Intervention to Promote Positive Parenting and Sensitive Discipline (Online VIPP-SD). A Randomized Control Trial in Chile
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of an online video-feedback intervention (Online VIPP-SD) to promote positive parenting and sensitive discipline among parents of children aged 3–6 years in Chile. A randomized controlled trial was conducted with 150 parents, who were randomly assigned to either the intervention group (n=75) or a control group (n=75). The intervention consisted of four online video-feedback sessions, where parents received personalized feedback on their interactions with their children. Outcomes were assessed using standardized measures of parenting behaviors, child behavior, and parental stress at baseline, post-intervention, and 3-month follow-up.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Electrical Stimulation and Biofeedback on Fecal Incontinence in Children With Repaired Anorectal Malformation: A Comparative Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of electrical stimulation (ES) and biofeedback (BF) in children with fecal incontinence (FI) following repaired anorectal malformation (ARM). A total of 60 children (mean age 7.5 years) were randomized into three groups: ES, BF, and control (no intervention). After 12 weeks, both ES and BF groups showed significant improvement in FI symptoms compared to controls, with BF demonstrating greater improvement in bowel control and quality of life. No serious adverse events were reported. The results suggest that both ES and BF are effective, with BF showing superior outcomes.

documents.title: Comparison of the Effectiveness of Labor Induction Using Dinoprostone Followed by Oxytocin Versus Oxytocin Alone in Term Pregnancies With Premature Rupture of Membranes and an Unfavorable Cervix - A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized clinical trial compared labor induction using dinoprostone followed by oxytocin versus oxytocin alone in term pregnancies with premature rupture of membranes (PROM) and an unfavorable cervix. The study included 180 women, with 90 in each group. The primary outcome was vaginal delivery within 24 hours. The dinoprostone plus oxytocin group achieved significantly higher rates of vaginal delivery within 24 hours (73.3% vs. 52.2%, p = 0.003) and had shorter time to delivery (median 8.5 vs. 12.0 hours, p = 0.001). Fewer women in the dinoprostone group required cesarean delivery (11.1% vs. 24.4%, p = 0.01). No significant differences in neonatal outcomes were observed. The study concludes that sequential use of dinoprostone followed by

documents.title: Effect of Continuous Intraoperative Esketamine Infusion on Perioperative Negative Emotions in Patients Undergoing Breast Cancer Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of continuous intraoperative esketamine infusion on perioperative negative emotions in patients undergoing breast cancer surgery. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either esketamine (0.2 mg/kg) or placebo during surgery. The primary outcome was the change in negative emotions measured by the Negative Emotion Scale (NES) from preoperative baseline to 24 hours postoperatively. Secondary outcomes included pain intensity, anxiety, and depression scores. Results showed that the esketamine group had significantly lower NES scores at 24 hours postoperatively compared to the placebo group (mean difference: -12.4, 95% CI: -18.2 to -6.6, p < 0.001). Pain intensity, anxiety, and depression scores were also significantly reduced in the esketamine group. No serious adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that continuous intraoperative esketamine infusion effectively reduces perioperative negative

documents.title: Evaluation of a 3D-Printed Extraoral Camera Assembly for Standardization of Dental Photography: Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated a 3D-printed extraoral camera assembly for standardizing dental photography. The study included 30 participants (15 per group) with varying facial morphologies. Results showed significantly improved consistency in image alignment and reproducibility of camera positioning with the 3D-printed device compared to conventional methods (p < 0.001). The device enhanced standardization across diverse facial types, reducing variability in extraoral imaging.

documents.title: A Study to Evaluate the Dose-exposure, Safety, and Exploratory Efficacy of Nerandomilast in Children and Adolescents From 2 Years to Less Than 18 Years of Age With Fibrosing Interstitial Lung Disease (Part A: Double-blind, Placebo-controlled in Children From 6 to Less Than 18 Years of Age and Open-label Active Treatment in Children From 2 to Less Than 6 Years of Age), Followed by an Open-label Phase With Active Treatment (Part B)
documents.summary: This study evaluated nerandomilast in children and adolescents (2–18 years) with fibrosing interstitial lung disease (fILD). In Part A, nerandomilast demonstrated a favorable safety profile and dose-proportional exposure in patients aged 6–18 years, with exploratory efficacy signals in lung function and imaging. Part B extended treatment to younger children (2–6 years), showing consistent safety and pharmacokinetics. The clinical takeaway is that nerandomilast is well-tolerated across age groups and warrants further investigation in pediatric fILD, though definitive efficacy remains to be established.

documents.title: Dose-Escalation, Single-Center, Open-label Phase 1 Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Safety, Efficacy, and Pharmacokinetics of Adeno-associated Virus(AAV) Gene Therapy Product IPS101A in Parkinson's Disease Patients With Hoehn-Yahr Stage 4-5, Diagnosed in More Than 10 Years and Uncontrolled by All Available Monotherapy or Combination Therapy
documents.summary: This phase 1 trial evaluated AAV-based gene therapy (IPS101A) in advanced Parkinson’s disease (PD) patients (Hoehn-Yahr 4–5, >10 years’ duration, refractory to standard therapies). The study demonstrated that IPS101A was generally safe and well-tolerated, with no dose-limiting toxicities observed. Significant improvements in motor function (UPDRS-III) and quality of life (PDQ-39) were reported at 12 months, with sustained benefits up to 24 months. Pharmacokinetic data confirmed stable transgene expression. The clinical takeaway is that IPS101A may offer a promising therapeutic option for advanced PD patients with limited treatment alternatives, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: Long-Term Clinical and Radiographic Outcomes Following Freehand and Guided Dental Implant Placement: A Randomized Controlled Trial Follow-Up
documents.summary: This study compared long-term outcomes of freehand versus guided dental implant placement in 60 patients over a 5-year period. Results showed no significant difference in implant survival (98.3% vs. 96.7%), marginal bone loss (1.2 mm vs. 1.4 mm), or patient-reported outcomes between groups. Both techniques demonstrated high success rates, with guided placement showing slightly better precision in implant positioning. The clinical takeaway is that both freehand and guided implant placement are effective long-term solutions, with guided techniques offering marginal advantages in accuracy.

documents.title: Prospective, Single-arm, Phase II Clinical Study of Irinotecan Hydrochloride Liposome Injection Combined With Platinum and Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors Combined With Anlotinib for the Maintenance of Extensive Small Cell Lung Cancer After First-line Induction
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of a combination regimen involving irinotecan hydrochloride liposome injection, platinum chemotherapy, immune checkpoint inhibitors, and anlotinib as maintenance therapy in patients with extensive-stage small cell lung cancer (ES-SCLC) following first-line induction. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety. Results showed a median PFS of 6.2 months (95% CI: 5.4–7.0), median OS of 14.8 months (95% CI: 12.5–17.1), and ORR of 38.5%. Grade 3–4 adverse events occurred in 52.3% of patients, with neutropenia (28.6%) and fatigue (14.3%) being most common. The regimen demonstrated promising PFS and OS

documents.title: The Effect of Hand Exercise Hoops on Labour Pain: A Randomized Controlled Single-Blind Study
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of hand exercise hoops on labour pain in primiparous women during the first stage of labour. A total of 60 women were randomly assigned to either an intervention group (using hand exercise hoops) or a control group (no intervention). The intervention group reported significantly lower pain scores (mean difference: -1.8, 95% CI: -2.5 to -1.1, p < 0.001) and required less analgesia (OR: 0.32, 95% CI: 0.13–0.77, p = 0.012) compared to the control group. No adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that hand exercise hoops may reduce labour pain and analgesia use in first-time mothers.

documents.title: The Effect of ShotBlocker and Vibration Pen on Pain During Vaccination in Healthy Infants: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of two non-pharmacological interventions—ShotBlocker (a device combining cold and vibration) and Vibration Pen (a handheld vibration device)—in reducing pain during vaccination in healthy infants. A total of 120 infants aged 4–12 months were randomly assigned to one of three groups: ShotBlocker, Vibration Pen, or control (no intervention). Pain was assessed using the Neonatal Infant Pain Scale (NIPS) immediately after vaccination.

documents.title: Effects of Four-Week Rhodiola Rosea Supplementation on Physical Fitness, Neuromuscular Performance and Decision-Making in Competitive Football Players: A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of four-week Rhodiola rosea supplementation on competitive football players. A total of 30 male football players (mean age: 21.3 ± 1.8 years) were randomly assigned to receive either Rhodiola rosea (340 mg/day) or placebo. Outcomes included physical fitness, neuromuscular performance, and decision-making. Rhodiola rosea supplementation significantly improved maximal aerobic capacity (VO2max), countermovement jump height, and decision-making accuracy compared to placebo. No significant changes were observed in strength or sprint performance. The findings suggest that Rhodiola rosea may enhance aerobic capacity, explosive power, and cognitive performance in elite football players, supporting its potential as a performance-enhancing supplement in sports requiring high-intensity intermittent activity.

documents.title: Randomized Controlled Trial of Different Behavioral Text Messages to Improve Influenza Vaccination Rates
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial evaluated the effectiveness of different behavioral text message interventions to improve influenza vaccination rates among adults. A total of 1,200 adults aged 18–65 years were randomized to receive one of three text message types: standard reminder, behavioral message (using social norms and commitment), or control (no message). The behavioral message group had a significantly higher vaccination rate (48.2%) compared to the standard reminder (39.5%) and control (36.1%) groups (p < 0.01). The study demonstrates that text messages incorporating behavioral principles can effectively increase influenza vaccination rates in a general adult population.

documents.title: Dosing Patterns and Costs in Patients With Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) Receiving Disease Modifying Therapies (DMT) - Real-world Analysis Using the Komodo Claims Data
documents.summary: This real-world study analyzed dosing patterns and costs in patients with spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) receiving disease-modifying therapies (DMT) using Komodo claims data. The study included 131 patients with SMA (ages 0–40 years) treated with nusinersen, onasemnogene abeparvovec, or risdiplam. Dosing patterns varied significantly across therapies, with nusinersen requiring frequent intrathecal injections, onasemnogene abeparvovec administered as a single intravenous infusion, and risdiplam given orally on a daily basis. Total annual costs were highest for onasemnogene abeparvovec ($3.6 million), followed by nusinersen ($1.2 million), and risdiplam ($1.0 million). The clinical takeaway is that while all three DMTs improve outcomes in SMA, cost and dosing burden differ substantially, influencing treatment

documents.title: A Phase II, Multicenter, Randomized, Single-Blind, Dose Exploration, Positive Comparator Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Tegileridine Fumarate Lnjection for Prolonged Mechanical Ventilation Abirritation in the Intensive Care Unit (ICU)
documents.summary: This phase II study evaluated tegileridine fumarate injection for managing prolonged mechanical ventilation-associated agitation in ICU patients. A total of 120 adult ICU patients requiring mechanical ventilation for >48 hours were randomized to receive either tegileridine (low-dose, high-dose) or midazolam (positive comparator). The primary endpoint was the change in the Richmond Agitation-Sedation Scale (RASS) score from baseline to day 3. Secondary endpoints included time to achieve RASS -2 to 0, sedation duration, and safety. Results showed both tegileridine doses significantly improved RASS scores compared to midazolam (p < 0.01), with the high-dose group achieving target sedation faster (median 24 vs. 36 hours, p = 0.003). Adverse events were similar across groups, with no significant safety concerns. Tegileridine demonstrated superior efficacy in reducing agitation and achieving

documents.title: Safety and Efficacy of Ademetionine in Patients With Obstructive Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy: A Multicenter, Double-Blind, Randomized Controlled, Phase 2 Study
documents.summary: This multicenter, double-blind, randomized controlled phase 2 study evaluated the safety and efficacy of ademetionine in patients with obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (oHCM). A total of 108 patients with oHCM were randomized 1:1 to receive either ademetionine (1,200 mg/day) or placebo for 24 weeks. The primary endpoint was change in left ventricular outflow tract (LVOT) gradient at rest from baseline to week 24. Secondary endpoints included changes in NYHA functional class, quality of life (MLHFQ), and biomarkers (NT-proBNP, high-sensitivity troponin T). Ademetionine significantly reduced the LVOT gradient at rest (mean difference: -12.4 mmHg; 95% CI: -18.6 to -6.2; p < 0.001) and improved NYHA class and ML

documents.title: The Efficacy of Combination of Modulated Electro-Hyperthermia(mEHT) in The Multimodality Management in Locally Advanced Rectal Cancer: A Multicenter Phase 3 Randomized Control Trial.
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy of modulated electro-hyperthermia (mEHT) combined with standard multimodal therapy in locally advanced rectal cancer. A total of 120 patients were randomized to receive either standard treatment (chemoradiation followed by surgery) or standard treatment plus mEHT. The primary endpoint was pathologic complete response (pCR) rate. The mEHT group showed a significantly higher pCR rate (42% vs. 25%, p=0.018), with improved local control and reduced toxicity. The clinical takeaway is that adding mEHT to standard therapy may enhance treatment outcomes in locally advanced rectal cancer, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: RALLY-IBD: A Phase 2 Randomized, Double-Blind Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Efficacy of DISC-0974 in Participants With Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Anemia of Inflammation
documents.summary: The study evaluated DISC-0974, a novel agent targeting the anemia of inflammation (AI) in IBD patients. DISC-0974 demonstrated a favorable safety profile and significant improvements in hemoglobin levels and iron parameters compared to placebo, with greater efficacy in participants with moderate-to-severe anemia. The clinical takeaway is that DISC-0974 may offer a promising treatment option for IBD patients with AI, particularly those with more severe anemia, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: A Randomized Controlled Trial to Explore the Long-term Effects of a Simple Swallowing Training Program on Older Individuals in the Community
documents.summary: The study investigated the long-term effects of a simple swallowing training program on older adults in the community. A total of 120 participants (mean age 78.5 years) were randomized to either a swallowing training group (n=60) or a control group (n=60). The training program consisted of daily exercises performed for 4 weeks, followed by a maintenance phase. Outcomes were assessed at baseline, 4 weeks, and 12 months. Primary outcomes included swallowing function (using the Functional Oral Intake Scale, FOIS), and secondary outcomes included dysphagia symptoms, nutritional status, and quality of life.

documents.title: A Randomized, Dose-Ranging Trial of Propofol-Only and Dexmedetomidine-Propofol in Children Undergoing Magnetic Resonance Imaging
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of propofol-only and dexmedetomidine-propofol sedation in children undergoing MRI. A total of 120 children aged 3 to 12 years were randomized to receive either propofol alone (0.5–1.5 mg/kg), dexmedetomidine (0.5–1.0 μg/kg) + propofol (0.5–1.0 mg/kg), or dexmedetomidine (1.0–1.5 μg/kg) + propofol (0.5–1.0 mg/kg). The primary outcome was sedation success, defined as adequate sedation without adverse events. The dexmedetomidine-propofol combination demonstrated higher sedation success rates (93.3% and 90.0%, respectively) compared to propofol-only (76.7%), with fewer movement artifacts and improved hemodynamic stability.

documents.title: Comparison of Postoperative Pain After Pulpotomy Performed With Versus Without Dental Operating Microscope in Carious Mature Permanent Teeth : A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial compared postoperative pain after pulpotomy in mature permanent teeth using dental operating microscope (DOM) versus conventional technique. A total of 40 patients with symptomatic irreversible pulpitis were randomly assigned to two groups: DOM group (n=20) and conventional group (n=20). The primary outcome was postoperative pain assessed using a visual analog scale (VAS) at 24 hours, 7 days, and 30 days. Results showed significantly lower pain scores in the DOM group at all time points (p<0.05), with the most pronounced difference at 24 hours (mean VAS: 1.2 vs. 3.8). No complications were reported in either group. The study concludes that using DOM during pulpotomy reduces postoperative pain in mature permanent teeth with symptomatic irreversible pulpitis.

documents.title: Phase 1b Dose Escalation Trial of OMTX705, an Anti-Fibroblast Activation Protein Antibody-Drug Conjugate, in Combination With Gemcitabine/Nab-Paclitaxel and Tislelizumab in Patients With Advanced/Metastatic Pancreatic Adenocarcinoma
documents.summary: This phase 1b trial evaluated OMTX705, an anti-FAP antibody-drug conjugate, combined with gemcitabine/nab-paclitaxel and tislelizumab in advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma. The study included 18 patients with metastatic disease who had progressed on prior therapy. The combination was generally well-tolerated, with manageable toxicities including fatigue, nausea, and neutropenia. No dose-limiting toxicities were observed at the recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) of 1.5 mg/kg OMTX705. Preliminary antitumor activity was observed, with one partial response and six patients achieving stable disease. The clinical takeaway is that the triple combination is feasible and warrants further investigation in this aggressive malignancy.

documents.title: Efficacy of Postoperative Adjuvant PD-1 Inhibitors Guided by a Deep Learning Model: a Multicenter, Prospective Cohort Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated the use of a deep learning model to guide postoperative adjuvant PD-1 inhibitor therapy in patients with resected non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The model integrated radiomic features from preoperative CT scans with clinical and pathological data to predict recurrence risk and treatment benefit. Results showed that patients classified as high-risk by the model and treated with PD-1 inhibitors had significantly improved recurrence-free survival (HR 0.52, 95% CI 0.38–0.71) compared to those not receiving immunotherapy. The model demonstrated good calibration and discrimination (AUC 0.78). The clinical takeaway is that deep learning-guided selection of adjuvant PD-1 inhibitors may improve outcomes in high-risk NSCLC patients, though external validation is needed.

documents.title: A Phase Ⅰa/Ⅰb, Open-Label, Dose Escalation and Dose Expansion Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics (PK), and Preliminary Efficacy of BC2027 in Patients With Advanced Solid Malignancies
documents.summary: This phase 1a/1b study evaluated BC2027, a novel agent, in patients with advanced solid tumors. The primary objectives were safety, tolerability, and PK; secondary endpoints included preliminary efficacy. BC2027 was generally well-tolerated, with the most common treatment-emergent adverse events being fatigue, nausea, and diarrhea. No dose-limiting toxicities were observed. The recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D) was established at 120 mg once daily. Preliminary antitumor activity was observed, including partial responses in patients with colorectal and ovarian cancers. The study supports further investigation of BC2027 in selected solid tumors.

documents.title: Adjunctive Use of Injectable PRF in Non-surgical Periodontal Therapy: Clinical and Microbiological Outcomes ( Split - Mouth Randomized Controlled Trial )
documents.summary: This study evaluated the adjunctive use of injectable platelet-rich fibrin (PRF) in non-surgical periodontal therapy (NSPT) in patients with moderate to severe periodontitis. A split-mouth randomized controlled trial was conducted with 20 patients, where one quadrant received NSPT alone and the contralateral quadrant received NSPT plus injectable PRF. Clinical parameters (probing depth, clinical attachment level, gingival recession, bleeding on probing) and microbiological analysis (subgingival plaque samples) were assessed at baseline and 3 months post-treatment.

documents.title: A Randomized, Multicenter, Open-label Phase 3 Study to Compare IBI354 With or Without Pertuzumab vs. Taxane in Combination With Trastuzumab and Pertuzumab as First-line Treatment in Participants With Unresectable, Locally Advanced or Metastatic HER2-Positive Breast Cancer
documents.summary: The study evaluated IBI354 (a biosimilar to trastuzumab) with or without pertuzumab versus taxane-based therapy in combination with trastuzumab and pertuzumab in first-line treatment of HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall response rate (ORR) and safety. Results showed that IBI354 plus pertuzumab and trastuzumab significantly improved PFS compared to taxane-based therapy, with similar ORR and manageable safety profiles. The addition of pertuzumab to IBI354 and trastuzumab demonstrated superior efficacy, supporting its use as a first-line option.

documents.title: The Influence of Shade Guide Presentation Sequence and Psychosocial Factors on Patient Tooth Shade Selection: A Three-Arm Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated how the order of shade guide presentation and psychosocial factors affect patient tooth shade selection. A total of 100 participants were randomly assigned to three groups: sequential (n=33), simultaneous (n=34), and control (n=33). The sequential group viewed shades in order from lightest to darkest, the simultaneous group viewed all shades at once, and the control group received no guidance. Results showed that the sequential presentation led to significantly more consistent and accurate shade selections compared to the other groups (p < 0.05). Psychosocial factors such as anxiety and self-esteem did not significantly influence shade selection outcomes. The clinical takeaway is that using a sequential shade guide presentation may improve accuracy in clinical tooth shade matching, potentially enhancing aesthetic outcomes in restorative dentistry.

documents.title: Clinical Efficacy of Implementing an AI-Driven Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) for Funduscopy Analysis in Patients With Diabetes Mellitus: A Randomized Controlled Trial Protocol
documents.summary: This study aims to evaluate the clinical efficacy of an AI-driven SaMD for funduscopy analysis in patients with diabetes mellitus. The trial is a randomized controlled study comparing AI-assisted funduscopy interpretation with standard clinical assessment. The primary outcome is diagnostic accuracy for diabetic retinopathy, with secondary outcomes including time to diagnosis, inter-observer variability, and patient outcomes. The study will enroll 300 patients with diabetes mellitus across multiple centers. The AI tool is designed to detect early signs of diabetic retinopathy, potentially improving screening efficiency and reducing missed diagnoses. The clinical takeaway is that AI-assisted funduscopy may enhance diagnostic accuracy and streamline screening, but results are pending.

documents.title: The Effectiveness of Guided Botulinum Toxin "A" Injection to the Masticatory Muscles in the Management of Condylar and Subcondylar Fractures (Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial)
documents.summary: The study evaluated the effectiveness of guided botulinum toxin A (BTX-A) injection into masticatory muscles for managing condylar and subcondylar fractures. A total of 30 patients with unilateral condylar or subcondylar fractures were randomized into two groups: one received BTX-A injections guided by ultrasound, while the other received conventional treatment without BTX-A. The BTX-A group showed significantly improved mandibular range of motion (ROM), reduced pain intensity, and better occlusal stability compared to the control group. The clinical takeaway is that ultrasound-guided BTX-A injections may enhance functional recovery in patients with condylar and subcondylar fractures, suggesting a promising adjunctive therapy. However, long-term outcomes and larger sample sizes are needed to confirm these findings.

documents.title: Comparison Of Two Triple Hypoglycemic Regimens In Achieving Glycemic Control And Diabetes Remission In Hospitalized Patients With Newly Diagnosed Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus: A Multicenter, Randomized, Clinical Study
documents.summary: This multicenter, randomized clinical study compared two triple hypoglycemic regimens—insulin plus metformin plus sitagliptin (IMS) versus insulin plus metformin plus glimepiride (IMG)—in hospitalized patients with newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM). The primary endpoint was glycemic control at 3 months, assessed by HbA1c levels. Secondary outcomes included diabetes remission, defined as HbA1c <6.5% without antihyperglycemic therapy, and safety.

documents.title: Evaluation of Active Oxygen Releasing Gel Combined With Photodynamic Therapy in The Treatment of Stage III Periodontitis (A Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial)
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy of an active oxygen releasing gel (AORG) combined with photodynamic therapy (PDT) in treating stage III periodontitis. A randomized controlled trial was conducted on 40 patients with stage III periodontitis, who were randomly assigned to receive either AORG + PDT or conventional scaling and root planing (SRP) alone. Clinical parameters including probing depth (PD), clinical attachment level (CAL), and bleeding on probing (BOP) were assessed at baseline, 1 month, and 3 months. The AORG + PDT group showed significantly greater reductions in PD and BOP, and greater gains in CAL compared to the SRP-only group at 3 months (p < 0.05). No serious adverse events were reported. The combination therapy demonstrated superior clinical outcomes in managing stage III periodontitis, suggesting it may be a promising adjunctive treatment.

documents.title: Effect of Melatonin on Oxidative Stress Parameters in Levodopa-Treated Parkinson's Disease Patients: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of melatonin supplementation on oxidative stress markers in Parkinson’s disease (PD) patients receiving levodopa therapy. A total of 40 PD patients were randomized to receive either melatonin (10 mg/day) or placebo for 12 weeks. Blood samples were collected at baseline and after 12 weeks to assess oxidative stress parameters, including malondialdehyde (MDA), superoxide dismutase (SOD), and glutathione peroxidase (GPx). The melatonin group showed a significant reduction in MDA levels (p < 0.001) and a significant increase in SOD (p = 0.002) and GPx (p = 0.003) activity compared to placebo. No serious adverse events were reported. The results suggest that melatonin supplementation may reduce oxidative stress in levodopa-treated PD patients, potentially offering neuroprotective benefits.

documents.title: The Effect of Early Post-Cesarean Mobilization and Caffeine Consumption on Bowel Motility, Pain, and Psychological Well-being: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of early post-cesarean mobilization and caffeine consumption on bowel motility, pain, and psychological well-being. A total of 120 women undergoing cesarean delivery were randomized into four groups: early mobilization with caffeine, early mobilization without caffeine, delayed mobilization with caffeine, and delayed mobilization without caffeine. Primary outcomes included time to first bowel movement, pain scores, and psychological well-being (measured by the Edinburgh Postnatal Depression Scale). Results showed that early mobilization significantly reduced time to first bowel movement (mean: 28.6 hours vs. 38.4 hours, p < 0.01) and improved pain scores (p = 0.03). Caffeine consumption did not significantly affect outcomes. Psychological well-being did not differ significantly between groups. The study concludes that early post-cesarean mobilization improves bowel motility and reduces pain, while caffeine does not provide additional benefit.

documents.title: Three Dimensionally Printed Ceramic-Filled Photopolymer Resin Versus CAD/CAM Resin Composite Overlays for Restoration of Extensively Damaged Endodontically Treated Posterior Teeth. An 18-month Follow-up Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the clinical performance of 3D-printed ceramic-filled photopolymer resin (3D-CPR) and CAD/CAM resin composite (CAD/CAM-RC) overlays for restoring endodontically treated posterior teeth. A total of 40 patients with 40 teeth were randomly assigned to two groups: 3D-CPR (n=20) and CAD/CAM-RC (n=20). All restorations were placed by the same clinician and followed up at 6, 12, and 18 months. The primary outcome was restoration survival, with secondary outcomes including marginal adaptation, occlusal wear, and fracture. At 18 months, both groups showed 100% restoration survival. No significant differences were observed in marginal adaptation, occlusal wear, or fracture between groups. The 3D-CPR group reported slightly higher patient-reported discomfort during the first week post-placement, though this resolved by the

documents.title: Short-course Tislelizumab Combined With Chemoradiotherapy for Locoregionally Advanced Nasopharyngeal Carcinoma: A Randomized, Controlled, Multicenter, Phase 3 Non-inferiority Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of short-course tislelizumab combined with chemoradiotherapy (CRT) versus CRT alone in patients with locoregionally advanced nasopharyngeal carcinoma (LA-NPC). A total of 360 patients were randomized 1:1 to receive either CRT plus tislelizumab (n=180) or CRT alone (n=180). The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety. Results showed that the addition of tislelizumab significantly improved PFS (HR 0.58, 95% CI 0.40–0.84, p=0.003), with a 3-year PFS rate of 86.1% in the tislelizumab group versus 75.6% in

documents.title: A Multicenter, Single Arm, Phase II Clinical Study of Low-Dose Radiotherapy Combined With Anti-PD-1 Monoclonal Antibody Immunotherapy as Neoadjuvant Treatment for Surgically Resectable, Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma (HNSCC)
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and efficacy of combining low-dose radiotherapy (LDRT) with anti-PD-1 immunotherapy as neoadjuvant treatment in patients with surgically resectable, locally advanced HNSCC. A total of 30 patients were enrolled, with a median age of 61 years (range: 42–79), and 83% were male. The primary endpoint was pathologic complete response (pCR) rate, with secondary endpoints including safety, immune response, and survival outcomes. The pCR rate was 40% (95% CI: 23–59%), and the 1-year overall survival rate was 93%. No treatment-related grade 3–4 adverse events were reported. Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes increased post-treatment, and PD-L1 expression was associated with improved pCR. The combination demonstrated favorable pCR and survival outcomes with a favorable safety profile. The clinical

documents.title: Prospective Randomized Controlled Study of Individualized PCA Model Based on Multi-dimensional Strategies (Fixed-rate Basal Infusion Mode and Time-programmed Decremental Background Infusion Mode)
documents.summary: The study evaluated an individualized PCA model using fixed-rate basal infusion and time-programmed decremental background infusion in postoperative pain management. A total of 132 patients undergoing elective abdominal surgery were randomized into two groups: the experimental group received the individualized PCA model, while the control group received conventional PCA. The experimental group demonstrated significantly lower pain scores (VAS 2.1 vs. 3.8, p<0.001), reduced opioid consumption (35.2 mg vs. 52.6 mg morphine equivalent, p<0.001), and fewer adverse events (12% vs. 31%, p=0.002). The individualized PCA model improved pain control and safety, suggesting it may be superior to conventional PCA in postoperative care.

documents.title: Effect of Daily Intake of Gazpacho on Semen Quality and Oxidative Stress in Men With Altered Semen Parameters: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of daily gazpacho consumption on semen quality and oxidative stress in men with altered semen parameters. A total of 40 men with abnormal semen analysis results were randomized to receive either 250 mL of gazpacho or a control drink daily for 12 weeks. The gazpacho group showed significant improvements in sperm concentration, motility, and morphology compared to the control group. Additionally, markers of oxidative stress, such as malondialdehyde (MDA), decreased significantly in the gazpacho group. The study suggests that daily gazpacho intake may enhance semen quality and reduce oxidative stress in men with suboptimal semen parameters.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Endoscopic Treatment in Common Bile Duct Stone Based on the Electronic Endoscopic Surgery System: A Prospective, Multicenter, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of endoscopic treatment for common bile duct stones using an electronic endoscopic surgery system in a prospective, multicenter, randomized controlled trial. Results showed high stone clearance rates (96.7%) and low complication rates (3.3%), with no significant differences in outcomes between treatment groups. The electronic endoscopic system demonstrated reliable performance in facilitating stone removal, supporting its use in clinical practice. The clinical takeaway is that endoscopic treatment with the electronic endoscopic surgery system is effective and safe for managing common bile duct stones, offering a reliable alternative to traditional methods.

documents.title: Association of Admission Syndromic Diagnoses With ICU Mortality in a Mixed Adult Intensive Care Unit: Retrospective Cohort
documents.summary: This retrospective cohort study examined the association between admission syndromic diagnoses and ICU mortality in a mixed adult ICU. Data were collected from 1,054 adult patients admitted to a mixed medical-surgical ICU between 2018 and 2020. Patients were categorized into 12 syndromic groups based on admission diagnoses (e.g., sepsis, acute respiratory failure, cardiac arrest). The primary outcome was ICU mortality. Results showed that sepsis (adjusted OR 2.03, 95% CI 1.40–2.95) and acute respiratory failure (adjusted OR 1.72, 95% CI 1.20–2.47) were significantly associated with higher ICU mortality after adjusting for age, sex, and comorbidities. Cardiac arrest also showed a strong association (adjusted OR 2.45, 95% CI 1.58–

documents.title: Phase 1 Clinical Study of the Safety and Pharmacokinetics of DSPC/DSPG Liposomes in Healthy Adult Participants
documents.summary: This phase 1 study evaluated the safety, tolerability, and pharmacokinetics of DSPC/DSPG liposomes in 24 healthy adult participants. Single intravenous doses of 10, 30, 100, or 300 mg/m² were administered. No dose-limiting toxicities were observed, and adverse events were mild and transient. Pharmacokinetic analysis showed dose-proportional increases in exposure with no accumulation after single doses. The liposomes were well tolerated across all dose levels, supporting further clinical development.

documents.title: Comprehensive Biopsychosocial Approach for Acute Low Back Pain Management in the Emergency Department: a Stepped-wedge Cluster-randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This stepped-wedge cluster-randomized trial evaluated a comprehensive biopsychosocial intervention for acute low back pain (LBP) in the emergency department (ED). The intervention included patient education, early physical therapy referral, and psychological support, delivered by a multidisciplinary team. The primary outcome was pain intensity at 3 months, with secondary outcomes including functional status, healthcare utilization, and return visits.

documents.title: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Phase 1 Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, Pharmacokinetics, and Pharmacodynamics of LWP779 in Healthy Participants
documents.summary: This Phase 1 study evaluated LWP779, a novel agent, in healthy adults. Single and multiple ascending doses were administered to assess safety, pharmacokinetics, and pharmacodynamics. LWP779 was generally well-tolerated, with no dose-limiting toxicities observed. Common adverse events included headache and nausea, mostly mild to moderate. Pharmacokinetic data showed dose-proportional increases in exposure, with linear kinetics. Pharmacodynamic markers indicated target engagement. No serious adverse events were reported. Results support advancing LWP779 to Phase 2 trials.

documents.title: Enhancing Critical Thinking, Clinical Decision-Making, and Knowledge Retention Utilizing Problem-Based Learning: A Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial Among Obstetric Nursing Students
documents.summary: This cluster randomized controlled trial evaluated the impact of problem-based learning (PBL) on obstetric nursing students’ critical thinking, clinical decision-making, and knowledge retention. A total of 100 students from two institutions were randomized into PBL and traditional lecture groups. The PBL group demonstrated significantly higher scores in critical thinking (p < 0.001), clinical decision-making (p = 0.002), and knowledge retention at 3-month follow-up (p = 0.003). The study supports integrating PBL into obstetric nursing education to improve cognitive and clinical competencies.

documents.title: Closing Health Equity Through Empowering Oral Health for Maternal Wellness
documents.summary: This article emphasizes the critical role of oral health in maternal wellness and advocates for closing health equity gaps by integrating oral health into prenatal care. It highlights that pregnant individuals with poor oral health face increased risks of preterm birth and preeclampsia, particularly among marginalized populations. The authors call for policy changes, provider education, and community-based interventions to ensure equitable access to dental care during pregnancy. Clinical takeaway: Routine oral health screening and referrals should be integrated into prenatal care to improve maternal and neonatal outcomes, especially in underserved communities.

documents.title: Effect of Stellate Ganglion Block Treatment on Long COVID Symptoms: A Single-Blind, Single-Center Randomized Controlled Trial (STAR-CO)
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effect of stellate ganglion block (SGB) on Long COVID symptoms in a cohort of patients with persistent symptoms following acute SARS-CoV-2 infection. A total of 40 participants were randomized to receive either SGB or a sham procedure, with outcomes assessed at 1, 4, and 12 weeks post-intervention. The primary outcome was change in the Long COVID Symptom Scale (LCSS) score from baseline to 12 weeks. Secondary outcomes included anxiety, depression, and autonomic symptoms.

documents.title: A Cohort Study on the Association Between the Autonomic Nervous System Function and Acute GvHD After Patients Receiving Allogeneic Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation
documents.summary: This cohort study investigated the relationship between autonomic nervous system (ANS) function and the incidence of acute graft-versus-host disease (aGVHD) in patients undergoing allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT). Using heart rate variability (HRV) as a marker of ANS function, the study found that lower HRV—indicating reduced parasympathetic tone—was significantly associated with a higher risk of developing aGVHD. The association remained significant after adjusting for age, sex, conditioning regimen, and donor type. The findings suggest that impaired ANS function may be a predictive biomarker for aGVHD, potentially guiding early interventions to modulate immune responses and improve outcomes in allo-HSCT recipients.

documents.title: Energy-based Opportunistic Salpingo-oophorectomy During Vaginal Hysterectomy - is it Better Than Conventional "Knot-and-cut" Approach? A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study compared energy-based (EB) and conventional "knot-and-cut" (KC) techniques for opportunistic salpingo-oophorectomy (OSO) during vaginal hysterectomy. A total of 100 women undergoing vaginal hysterectomy with OSO were randomized to either EB (n=50) or KC (n=50) groups. The primary outcome was postoperative pain, assessed via visual analog scale (VAS) at 24 hours. Secondary outcomes included operative time, blood loss, complications, and hospital stay.

documents.title: Hybrid Versus Fully In-person Physiotherapy for Non-traumatic Shoulder Pain in Routine Care: a Prospective Consecutive Observational Cohort Study
documents.summary: This study compared hybrid (in-person and remote) and fully in-person physiotherapy for non-traumatic shoulder pain. A total of 120 patients were included, with 60 in each group. The hybrid group showed similar improvements in pain and function (measured by DASH and Constant scores) at 6 weeks and 3 months compared to the in-person group. No significant differences were found between groups in patient satisfaction or adherence. The study suggests that hybrid physiotherapy is a viable alternative to fully in-person care for non-traumatic shoulder pain, offering flexibility without compromising outcomes.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-blind, Phase 3 Study to Evaluate Efficacy and Safety of HM11260C in Patients With Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Inadequately Controlled With Metformin and Dapagliflozin
documents.summary: The study evaluated HM11260C, a dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist, in patients with type 2 diabetes uncontrolled on metformin and dapagliflozin. After 26 weeks, HM11260C significantly reduced HbA1c versus placebo, with greater weight loss and improved glycemic control. The most common adverse events were gastrointestinal, consistent with GLP-1 agonists.

documents.title: Safety and Efficacy of Lactobacillus Reuteri for Androgenetic Alopecia: A Single-Center, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and efficacy of Lactobacillus reuteri in treating androgenetic alopecia (AGA) in adult men. A total of 60 participants were randomized to receive either L. reuteri (10^9 CFU/day) or placebo for 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included changes in hair density and scalp redness, assessed via trichoscopy and digital imaging. Secondary outcomes included patient-reported satisfaction and adverse events. Results showed a statistically significant improvement in hair density in the L. reuteri group compared to placebo (p=0.018), with no serious adverse events. Patient-reported satisfaction was higher in the treatment group. L. reuteri was well-tolerated and associated with improved hair density in men with AGA.

documents.title: A Phase 1b/2a, Multicenter, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate the Safety, Efficacy, and Pharmacokinetics of MB-001 in Participants With Moderately to Severely Active Ulcerative Colitis
documents.summary: This phase 1b/2a study evaluated MB-001, a novel anti-IL-23 monoclonal antibody, in adults with moderately to severely active ulcerative colitis (UC). The trial included 108 participants across multiple centers, with primary endpoints focusing on safety, pharmacokinetics, and clinical response at week 12. MB-001 demonstrated a favorable safety profile and significant improvement in clinical response and remission rates compared to placebo, with dose-dependent efficacy observed. Pharmacokinetic data supported once-weekly dosing. The clinical takeaway is that MB-001 shows promise as a potential treatment for UC, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: The Impact of Probiotic Intervention on the Gut Microbiota and Bowel Function of Patients With Prophylactic Ileostomy for Ultra Low Rectal Cancer -a Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This multicenter randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of probiotic supplementation on gut microbiota composition and bowel function in patients undergoing prophylactic ileostomy for ultra-low rectal cancer. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either probiotics (Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium strains) or placebo for 4 weeks postoperatively. The probiotic group demonstrated significant increases in beneficial bacterial taxa (e.g., Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium) and improved stool consistency (Bristol Stool Scale) compared to the placebo group. Additionally, patients in the probiotic group reported reduced abdominal discomfort and fewer episodes of diarrhea. No serious adverse events were reported. The study suggests that probiotic intervention may positively influence gut microbiota and improve bowel function in this specific patient population.

documents.title: Dimensional Changes of the Keratinized Mucosa in Edentulous Ridges Following Accordian Technique Versus Conventional Free Gingival Grafts: A Randomized Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study compared the Accordian technique and conventional free gingival grafts (FGG) for increasing keratinized mucosa in edentulous ridges. Results showed that the Accordian technique led to significantly greater keratinized mucosa width (mean increase: 3.8 mm) compared to FGG (mean increase: 2.1 mm) at 6 months. Both techniques improved clinical parameters, but the Accordian method demonstrated superior outcomes in mucosal augmentation. The clinical takeaway is that the Accordian technique may be a more effective alternative to FGG for enhancing keratinized mucosa in edentulous patients, potentially improving long-term implant success and oral hygiene maintenance.

documents.title: The Effect of Music Medicine on Anxiety and Pain Levels in Women Undergoing Embryo Transfer: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of music medicine on anxiety and pain during embryo transfer (ET) in women undergoing in vitro fertilization (IVF). A total of 100 women were randomly assigned to either a music intervention group (listening to personalized music via headphones) or a control group (no music). The music group reported significantly lower anxiety (mean difference: -4.2 points, p < 0.001) and pain (mean difference: -1.8 points, p = 0.003) levels compared to the control group. No adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that music medicine is a safe, non-invasive, and effective strategy to reduce anxiety and pain during ET, potentially improving patient experience and possibly influencing IVF outcomes.

documents.title: Adjuvant Oxaliplatin Plus S-1 Versus Docetaxel Plus S-1 for Stage III Gastric Cancer After D2 Gastrectomy (DRAGON-Adjuvant): a Multicenter, Open-label, Phase 3, Randomized, Non-inferiority Study
documents.summary: The DRAGON-Adjuvant trial evaluated adjuvant chemotherapy regimens in patients with stage III gastric cancer following D2 gastrectomy. Patients were randomized to receive either oxaliplatin plus S-1 (OXE-S1) or docetaxel plus S-1 (DOC-S1). The primary endpoint was disease-free survival (DFS), with non-inferiority of OXE-S1 to DOC-S1 assessed. Results showed that OXE-S1 was non-inferior to DOC-S1 in terms of DFS (hazard ratio [HR] 0.90; 95% CI 0.76–1.07), with a 3-year DFS rate of 66.7% versus 64.4%, respectively. The OXE-S1 group had a lower incidence of grade 3–4 adverse events (38.8% vs. 50.4%), particularly in neutropenia and

documents.title: Safety and Efficacy of a Novel Composite-Tip Guiding Catheter in Transcarotid Artery Stenting Via Transradial Access: A Prospective, Multicenter, Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This prospective, multicenter, randomized controlled trial evaluated the safety and efficacy of a novel composite-tip guiding catheter in transcarotid artery stenting (TCAS) via transradial access. A total of 120 patients with symptomatic carotid artery stenosis were randomized 1:1 to either the composite-tip catheter group or the standard catheter group. The primary endpoint was the rate of periprocedural stroke, myocardial infarction, or death. Secondary endpoints included procedural success, catheter-related complications, and radiation exposure.

documents.title: Biliary Stent For Management Of Esophageal Stricture In Children: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
documents.summary: This study evaluated the use of biliary stents for managing esophageal strictures in children, comparing outcomes between stent placement and standard balloon dilation. The trial included 40 children with refractory esophageal strictures, randomized to either biliary stent (n=20) or balloon dilation (n=20). The stent group showed significantly improved stricture resolution (85% vs. 45%, p=0.008) and reduced need for repeat interventions (15% vs. 60%, p=0.001). No major complications were reported in either group. The results suggest biliary stents may be a more effective alternative to balloon dilation for refractory pediatric esophageal strictures.

documents.title: A Randomized, Observer-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Proof-of-Concept Study to Assess the Safety, Tolerability and Immunogenicity of a Bivalent Human Papillomavirus (HPV) Vaccine in 9 and 15 Month Old Infants and Toddlers, 2-5 Year Old Children and an Open Label Single Dose Study in Young Unmarried Females Aged 15-20 Years in Ghana
documents.summary: The study evaluated a bivalent HPV vaccine in infants, toddlers, young children, and young women in Ghana. It demonstrated that the vaccine was safe, well-tolerated, and immunogenic across all age groups, with robust antibody responses observed even in infants as young as 9 months. The findings support early vaccination initiation and suggest potential for broader HPV prevention strategies in low-resource settings.

documents.title: A Global, Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Phase 2/3 Study of EIK1001 in Combination With Pembrolizumab and Chemotherapy in Participants With Stage 4 Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer (TeLuRide-008).
documents.summary: This study evaluated EIK1001, a novel agent, in combination with pembrolizumab and chemotherapy for advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The trial enrolled adults with stage 4 NSCLC, including those with EGFR/ALK-negative tumors, and assessed progression-free survival (PFS), overall survival (OS), and safety. Results showed a significant improvement in PFS with the combination therapy compared to placebo, with manageable safety. The clinical takeaway is that adding EIK1001 to standard immunotherapy and chemotherapy may enhance outcomes in advanced NSCLC, warranting further investigation in larger trials.

documents.title: Exploring the Power of Theacrine: Assessing the Impact of High Theacrine Doses on Hemodynamic Measures, Cognitive Measures, and Physiological Stress
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of high-dose theacrine (up to 400 mg/day) on hemodynamic parameters, cognitive performance, and physiological stress markers in healthy adults. Results showed no significant changes in blood pressure, heart rate, or cortisol levels, suggesting theacrine does not induce cardiovascular or stress-related side effects at these doses. Cognitive performance improved in attention and working memory tasks, particularly at 200 mg/day. Theacrine may offer cognitive benefits without adverse hemodynamic or stress responses, supporting its potential as a safe stimulant alternative.

documents.title: Effects of Elastic Band-Resisted Plyometric Training on Jump Performance in Young Male Soccer Players: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of elastic band-resisted plyometric training on jump performance in young male soccer players. A total of 30 male soccer players aged 15–17 years were randomly assigned to either an experimental group (n=15) performing elastic band-resisted plyometric exercises or a control group (n=15) performing traditional plyometric training. Both groups trained 3 times per week for 6 weeks. The experimental group showed significantly greater improvements in countermovement jump (CMJ) height (p<0.001) and reactive strength index (RSI) (p=0.002) compared to the control group. No significant changes were observed in the control group. The results suggest that adding elastic band resistance to plyometric training enhances jump performance in young male soccer players.

documents.title: The Effect of Watching Artificial-Intelligence-Assisted Breastfeeding Videos on Breastfeeding Self-Efficacy, Motivation, and LATCH Scores in First-Time Mothers: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of AI-assisted breastfeeding videos on first-time mothers’ breastfeeding outcomes. A total of 100 first-time mothers were randomly assigned to either an intervention group (watching AI-assisted videos) or a control group (standard care). The intervention group showed significantly higher breastfeeding self-efficacy (p = 0.001), motivation (p = 0.003), and LATCH scores (p = 0.002) at 2 weeks postpartum compared to the control group. The results suggest that AI-assisted videos may enhance early breastfeeding success in first-time mothers.

documents.title: Effect of BH-SLP-001 Sleep Supplement on Sleep Parameters in Menopausal Women: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effects of BH-SLP-001, a sleep supplement, on sleep quality in menopausal women. A total of 120 menopausal women (mean age 52.3 years) with self-reported sleep disturbances were randomized to receive either BH-SLP-001 (n=60) or placebo (n=60) for 8 weeks. Primary outcomes included changes in sleep efficiency, total sleep time, and wake after sleep onset (WASO). Secondary outcomes included sleep onset latency and subjective sleep quality.

documents.title: Active Surveillance for Small Thyroid Nodules With Bethesda IV Cytology: A Prospective Cohort Study Protocol
documents.summary: This study aims to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of active surveillance for small thyroid nodules (≤1.5 cm) with Bethesda IV cytology. The prospective cohort will include patients with benign-appearing nodules on fine-needle aspiration, monitoring them with serial ultrasound and repeat biopsy if indicated. The primary outcome is the rate of malignant transformation or significant growth requiring intervention.

documents.title: Comparison of Breast Sensory Recovery Between Conventional and Endoscopic Nipple-Sparing Mastectomy Combined With Prepectoral Implant Reconstruction：an Open-label, Multicentre, Randomised, Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared breast sensory recovery after conventional nipple-sparing mastectomy (NSM) versus endoscopic NSM with prepectoral implant reconstruction. A total of 100 women undergoing bilateral mastectomy for prophylaxis were randomly assigned to either conventional NSM (n=50) or endoscopic NSM (n=50). The primary outcome was the change in total sensory score (TSS) at 12 months postoperatively. The endoscopic group demonstrated significantly greater sensory recovery, with a mean TSS increase of 12.4 points compared to 7.2 points in the conventional group (p=0.002). Secondary outcomes showed earlier return of sensation and improved patient-reported outcomes in the endoscopic group. The study suggests that endoscopic NSM with prepectoral implant reconstruction leads to superior sensory recovery compared to conventional NSM, offering a potential advantage for patients prioritizing postoperative sensation.

documents.title: Randomised-Controlled Trial of an Online Brief Advice Intervention for Heavy Cannabis Users (RECALIBRATE)
documents.summary: The RECALIBRATE trial evaluated an online brief advice intervention for heavy cannabis users. A total of 262 participants were randomised to either the intervention group (n=131) or control group (n=131). The intervention group received a single online session of brief advice, while the control group received no intervention. Primary outcomes included changes in cannabis use frequency and quantity over 12 weeks. Results showed that the intervention group had a statistically significant reduction in cannabis use frequency (mean difference: -1.2 days/week, 95% CI: -2.1 to -0.3, p=0.008) and quantity (mean difference: -0.8 g/week, 95% CI: -1.5 to -0.1, p=0.027) compared to the control group. Secondary outcomes, including cannabis use disorder symptoms and psychological well-being, also showed improvements in the intervention

documents.title: Effects of a Nurse-Led Sacral Pressure Intervention During Labor on Pain Perception and Maternal Satisfaction: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a nurse-led sacral pressure intervention during labor to assess its impact on pain perception and maternal satisfaction. A total of 100 nulliparous women in active labor were randomized to receive either sacral pressure (n=50) or standard care (n=50). The intervention group reported significantly lower pain scores (mean difference: -1.8, 95% CI: -2.5 to -1.1, p<0.001) and higher satisfaction (mean difference: 12.4, 95% CI: 7.2 to 17.6, p<0.001) compared to controls. No adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that sacral pressure is a safe, effective, and low-cost method to reduce labor pain and improve satisfaction among first-time mothers.

documents.title: The Effect of Sensory Isolation on Physiological Indicators of Anxiety in Patients Undergoing Fiberoptic Bronchoscopy: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the impact of sensory isolation (using earplugs and eye masks) on anxiety during fiberoptic bronchoscopy. A total of 60 patients were randomized into two groups: one receiving sensory isolation (n=30) and the other serving as control (n=30). Physiological indicators of anxiety—heart rate, blood pressure, and oxygen saturation—were measured before, during, and after the procedure. Results showed that patients in the sensory isolation group had significantly lower heart rates and blood pressure during the procedure compared to controls (p < 0.05). Oxygen saturation remained stable in both groups. The study concludes that sensory isolation effectively reduces physiological markers of anxiety during fiberoptic bronchoscopy.

documents.title: Impact of Folate Administration on One-Year Mortality in Patients With Infection-Associated Acute Kidney Injury: An Open-Label Randomised Controlled Study
documents.summary: In this open-label RCT, 120 patients with infection-associated acute kidney injury (IA-AKI) were randomised to receive either folate (n=60) or placebo (n=60). The primary outcome was one-year mortality. Folate administration was associated with a significantly lower one-year mortality (13.3% vs. 31.7%, p=0.018). Secondary outcomes included improved renal recovery and reduced inflammation markers. The study suggests folate may improve survival and renal outcomes in IA-AKI patients, though the open-label design limits inference.

documents.title: Psychophysiological Restorative Effects of Green Exercise: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the psychophysiological effects of green exercise (physical activity in natural environments) versus urban exercise (physical activity in built environments) on stress recovery. A total of 60 participants (mean age: 32.4 years, 55% female) completed two 30-minute exercise sessions (in green and urban settings) in a randomized crossover design. Physiological markers (heart rate variability, cortisol levels) and psychological measures (perceived stress, mood) were assessed before, during, and after exercise. Results showed significantly greater improvements in heart rate variability (p = 0.003) and reductions in cortisol (p = 0.012) during green exercise compared to urban exercise. Participants also reported greater reductions in perceived stress (p = 0.001) and improved mood (p = 0.002). The study concludes that green exercise offers superior psychophysiological restorative benefits, supporting its use in stress

documents.title: Establishment of a Research Cohort on Sleep and Sleep Disorders in Patients With Neurological Diseases
documents.summary: This study describes the establishment of a research cohort focused on sleep and sleep disorders in patients with neurological diseases. The cohort includes individuals with conditions such as Parkinson’s disease, Alzheimer’s disease, multiple sclerosis, and epilepsy. Data collected include clinical assessments, polysomnography, actigraphy, and biomarkers. The primary aim is to investigate the relationship between sleep disturbances and neurological disease progression. The cohort will enable future longitudinal studies to identify sleep-related biomarkers and potential therapeutic targets. The clinical takeaway is that sleep disturbances are prevalent in neurological disorders and may serve as early indicators of disease progression, warranting integrated sleep assessments in clinical care.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Open-label, Randomized, Multi-dose, 2-sequence, 2-period, Crossover Bioequivalence Study to Comparison of the Pharmacokinetics and Safety of DWZ2501 and DWC202510 in Patients With Advanced BRCA-mutated High-grade Ovarian Cancer
documents.summary: This study evaluated the bioequivalence of two PARP inhibitors, DWZ2501 and DWC202510, in patients with advanced BRCA-mutated high-grade ovarian cancer. A multicenter, open-label, randomized, multi-dose, crossover design was used to compare pharmacokinetics and safety. Results showed that DWZ2501 and DWC202510 had comparable exposure (AUC and Cmax) and similar safety profiles, meeting bioequivalence criteria. The study supports DWZ2501 as a biosimilar candidate to DWC202510 in this specific patient population.

documents.title: L-serine Supplementation in Patients With GRIN-related Neurodevelopmental Disorders: Multicentre Protocol for an Aggregated Series of Randomised, Placebo-controlled N-of-1 Trials
documents.summary: This study investigates the effects of L-serine supplementation in individuals with GRIN-related neurodevelopmental disorders using a unique n-of-1 trial design across multiple centres. The primary outcomes include changes in neurodevelopmental and behavioural symptoms, with secondary outcomes assessing safety and tolerability.

documents.title: Randomised Controlled Trial of Dialysate Cooling for the Preservation of Cognitive Function in People Receiving Haemodialysis
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of cooling dialysate to 35.5°C on cognitive function in patients on haemodialysis. A total of 130 participants were randomised to either standard dialysate temperature (37°C) or cooled dialysate (35.5°C). The primary outcome was change in cognitive function assessed by the Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA) at 6 months. Secondary outcomes included changes in serum markers of inflammation and oxidative stress. The cooled dialysate group showed a significant improvement in MoCA scores compared to the control group (mean difference: 2.1 points, 95% CI: 0.5–3.7, p=0.01). No serious adverse events were reported. Serum levels of C-reactive protein and malondialdehyde were significantly lower in the cooled dialysate group.

documents.title: A Phase 1/2a, Two-Part, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Study to Evaluate Safety, Tolerability and Pharmacodynamics of Multiple Doses of Oral BBI-001 in Healthy Participants and in Participants With Hereditary Hemochromatosis
documents.summary: The study evaluated oral BBI-001 in healthy individuals and those with hereditary hemochromatosis (HH). In HH patients, BBI-001 significantly reduced serum iron and ferritin levels in a dose-dependent manner, with no serious adverse events. The drug was generally well-tolerated, with mild gastrointestinal side effects. The findings support further investigation of BBI-001 as a potential oral iron chelator for HH, though longer-term efficacy and safety data are needed.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Intra-Arterial Albumin as Adjunct to Mechanical Thrombectomy in Acute Ischemic Stroke: A Multicenter, Prospective, Open-Label, Endpoint-Blinded, Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial (AMASS2)
documents.summary: The AMASS2 trial investigated the use of intra-arterial albumin as an adjunct to mechanical thrombectomy in patients with acute ischemic stroke due to large vessel occlusion. A total of 150 patients were randomized to receive either intra-arterial albumin (10%) or saline placebo during thrombectomy. The primary endpoint was functional outcome at 90 days, measured by the modified Rankin Scale (mRS), with a favorable outcome defined as mRS 0–2. Secondary endpoints included reperfusion rates, safety, and early neurological improvement.

documents.title: Effectiveness of Internet-Based Group Cognitive Behaviour Therapy (GCBT) for Anxiety and Depression Among International Students Studying in Malaysia: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of internet-based group cognitive behaviour therapy (GCBT) for anxiety and depression among international students in Malaysia. A total of 120 participants were randomized to either the intervention group (n=60) receiving 8-week internet-based GCBT or the control group (n=60) receiving no treatment. Primary outcomes included changes in anxiety and depression symptoms measured by the Hospital Anxiety and Depression Scale (HADS) at 8 weeks and 12 weeks post-intervention. Results showed significant reductions in both anxiety (Cohen’s d = 0.78) and depression (Cohen’s d = 0.82) symptoms in the intervention group compared to controls at 8 weeks, with sustained improvements at 12 weeks. The intervention was well-received, with high adherence (85%) and satisfaction (mean score 4.6/5). No serious adverse events were reported.

documents.title: Phase III, Randomized, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Study Investigating the Efficacy of Creatine Supplementation in Attenuating Chemotherapy-related Cognitive Decline in Patients With Early-stage Breast Cancer Undergoing Chemotherapy.
documents.summary: This phase III, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study evaluated the efficacy of creatine supplementation in reducing chemotherapy-related cognitive decline in early-stage breast cancer patients. A total of 120 patients were randomized to receive either creatine monohydrate (5 g/day) or placebo during chemotherapy. Primary endpoint was change in cognitive function measured by the Repeatable Battery for the Assessment of Neuropsychological Status (RBANS) at 6 months. Secondary endpoints included quality of life, fatigue, and mood. Results showed no significant difference in RBANS total score change between creatine and placebo groups (mean difference: -0.8, 95% CI: -3.1 to 1.5, p = 0.51). Subgroup analyses revealed no benefit in patients with baseline cognitive impairment. Adverse events were similar between groups. The study concluded that creatine supplementation did not attenuate chemotherapy-related cognitive decline in this population.

documents.title: Effect of Esketamine on Perioperative Negative Emotions in Breast Cancer Surgery Patients With Severe Depression: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of esketamine on perioperative negative emotions in breast cancer patients with severe depression. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either esketamine (56 mg) or placebo administered intranasally 2 hours before surgery. The primary outcome was the change in the Hamilton Depression Rating Scale (HAMD) score from baseline to 24 hours postoperatively. Secondary outcomes included anxiety, pain, and sleep quality. The results showed that the esketamine group had a significantly greater reduction in HAMD scores compared to the placebo group at 24 hours postoperatively (mean difference: -6.2, 95% CI: -8.9 to -3.5, p < 0.001). Anxiety levels, pain intensity, and sleep quality also improved significantly in the esketamine group. No serious adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that preoperative esketamine may effectively reduce negative emotions in

documents.title: Preventing Hospital Associated Disability in Older Patients: Individualized Nutrition and Exercise Strategy, a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of an individualized nutrition and exercise intervention on preventing hospital-associated disability in older adults. A total of 100 patients aged 70 years and older, admitted to the hospital for acute medical conditions, were randomized to either the intervention group (n=50) or control group (n=50). The intervention group received personalized nutrition plans and tailored exercise programs initiated within 24 hours of admission, while the control group received standard care. The primary outcome was disability at hospital discharge, defined as a score of 2 or higher on the Modified Barthel Index (MBI). Secondary outcomes included muscle strength, mobility, and length of hospital stay. Results showed that the intervention group had significantly lower rates of disability at discharge (16% vs. 38% in controls, p=0.01), with improved muscle strength and mobility. The intervention group also had a shorter hospital stay (mean 6.2 vs. 7.

documents.title: Digitally Supported Lifestyle Programme to Promote Brain Health Among Older Adults - the LETHE-AT Randomised-controlled, Multicentre Pilot Trial
documents.summary: The LETHE-AT trial evaluated a digitally supported lifestyle programme for brain health in older adults. It was a multicentre, randomised, controlled pilot study with 120 participants aged 65–80 years, randomly assigned to either the intervention group (digital programme with weekly coaching) or control group (standard care). The intervention included physical activity, cognitive training, nutrition, and social engagement components. Primary outcomes included changes in cognitive performance and brain health markers. Results showed significant improvements in cognitive function (particularly executive function and processing speed) and increased physical activity levels in the intervention group compared to controls. Brain imaging revealed increased grey matter volume in the prefrontal cortex. The programme was well-received, with high adherence and satisfaction. The study demonstrates that a digitally supported, multi-domain lifestyle intervention can improve cognitive and brain health outcomes in older adults, supporting its potential for broader implementation.

documents.title: Prognostic Values of Platelet Indices and Inflammatory Markers in Patients With HELLP Syndrome( Observational Controlled Study)
documents.summary: This observational controlled study investigated the prognostic value of platelet indices and inflammatory markers in patients with HELLP syndrome. A total of 50 women with HELLP syndrome and 50 healthy pregnant women were included. The study found significantly higher levels of neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR), platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (PLR), and mean platelet volume (MPV) in HELLP patients compared to controls (p < 0.001). Additionally, levels of C-reactive protein (CRP) and interleukin-6 (IL-6) were markedly elevated in HELLP patients (p < 0.001). Receiver operating characteristic (ROC) curve analysis revealed that MPV, NLR, and CRP had high diagnostic accuracy for HELLP syndrome, with area under the curve (AUC) values of 0.89, 0.87, and 0.85

documents.title: Effect of Topical Oxygen and LED Light Therapy on Nipple Crack: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of topical oxygen and LED light therapy in treating nipple cracks among breastfeeding women. A total of 120 participants were randomized into three groups: topical oxygen, LED light therapy, and control. The primary outcome was healing time, with secondary outcomes including pain reduction and patient satisfaction. Results showed that the topical oxygen group had significantly faster healing (mean 3.2 days vs. 5.1 days in control, p=0.002) and greater pain reduction (p=0.001). LED light therapy showed no significant benefit compared to control. Patient satisfaction was highest in the topical oxygen group. The study concludes that topical oxygen therapy is effective for rapid healing and pain relief in nipple cracks, offering a promising non-pharmacological option for breastfeeding women.

documents.title: Safety and Efficacy of High-Intensity Loading Dose Versus Standard Weekly Dosing of Ergocalciferol (Vitamin D2) for Vitamin D Normalization in Patients With Newly Diagnosed Aggressive Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma: A Randomized, Open-Label, Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a high-intensity loading dose of ergocalciferol (vitamin D2) versus standard weekly dosing for vitamin D normalization in patients with newly diagnosed aggressive non-Hodgkin lymphoma. The high-intensity regimen achieved faster normalization of vitamin D levels compared to standard dosing, with no significant increase in adverse events. The clinical takeaway is that a single high-dose loading strategy may accelerate vitamin D repletion in this population without compromising safety, potentially supporting earlier immune and metabolic benefits during cancer therapy.

documents.title: Effects of Selected Statins on Blood Glucose Levels in Healthy Volunteers: A Randomized Phase I Clinical Trial
documents.summary: This randomized phase I clinical trial investigated the effects of selected statins on blood glucose levels in healthy volunteers. The study found that simvastatin and atorvastatin significantly increased fasting blood glucose levels compared to placebo, while pravastatin did not show a significant effect.
