The findings suggest that certain statins may influence glucose metabolism, potentially increasing the risk of new-onset diabetes, particularly in individuals with predisposing factors. The clinical takeaway is that clinicians should consider the glucose effects of statins when selecting therapy, especially in patients at risk for diabetes.

documents.title: Efficacy of Pediococcus Acidilactici as add-on to Antipsychotic Drugs on Metabolic Syndrome Disturbances in First-episode Psychosis and Schizophrenia Spectrum Disorders. A Double-blind Placebo-controlled Trial.
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of Pediococcus acidilactici as an add-on to antipsychotic treatment in patients with first-episode psychosis and schizophrenia spectrum disorders. A total of 120 patients were randomized to receive either Pediococcus acidilactici (n=60) or placebo (n=60) for 12 weeks. The primary outcome was change in metabolic syndrome components, including waist circumference, blood pressure, fasting glucose, triglycerides, and HDL cholesterol. Secondary outcomes included psychiatric symptom severity and tolerability.

documents.title: Prospective Evaluation of Foreign Body Airway Obstruction Interventions Among Adults and Children: A Non-randomized Pilot Cohort Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of various interventions for foreign body airway obstruction (FBAO) in adults and children. A total of 102 patients (67 adults, 35 children) were enrolled across 10 emergency departments. The primary outcome was successful removal of the foreign body. The most common interventions were bronchoscopy (n=78), laryngoscopy (n=15), and the Heimlich maneuver (n=9). Bronchoscopy was successful in 94% of cases, laryngoscopy in 87%, and the Heimlich maneuver in 78%. Complications occurred in 12% of patients, including minor bleeding (n=6), hypoxia (n=3), and one case of tracheal perforation. The study suggests bronchoscopy is the most effective intervention for FBAO, particularly in complex cases, while the Heimlich maneuver remains a viable initial

documents.title: Administration of Fiber as a Dietary Supplement to Improve the Management of Alcohol Withdrawal - Randomized Controlled Open-label Study - FIB-ALC
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of fiber supplementation on alcohol withdrawal symptoms in a small cohort of 20 patients with alcohol use disorder (AUD) undergoing detoxification. Participants received either 10 g/day of fiber (psyllium) or no supplementation for 7 days. The primary outcome was the Clinical Institute Withdrawal Assessment for Alcohol, Revised (CIWA-Ar) score, with secondary outcomes including anxiety, sleep quality, and bowel movements. Results showed a significant reduction in CIWA-Ar scores in the fiber group compared to controls (p = 0.002), with greater improvements in anxiety (p = 0.005) and sleep quality (p = 0.01). Bowel movements increased significantly in the fiber group (p < 0.001). No adverse events were reported. The study suggests that fiber supplementation may improve alcohol withdrawal symptoms and associated psychological and gastrointestinal symptoms, but the small sample size and open-label design limit general

documents.title: Forest Therapy and Mindfulness in Highly Sensitive Persons (HSP): A Multicenter Randomized Interventional Study Over Approximately 22 Weeks
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of forest therapy and mindfulness on highly sensitive persons (HSPs), a population characterized by heightened sensory processing sensitivity. A multicenter randomized controlled trial was conducted over approximately 22 weeks, comparing forest therapy, mindfulness training, and a control group. Results showed significant improvements in psychological well-being, reduced stress, and enhanced emotional regulation in both intervention groups compared to controls. Forest therapy demonstrated greater reductions in cortisol levels, while mindfulness showed stronger effects on attention and cognitive flexibility. The findings suggest that both interventions are beneficial for HSPs, with forest therapy being particularly effective for physiological stress reduction and mindfulness for cognitive benefits.

documents.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids and Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) - A Double-blind Randomized Controlled Trial of Omega-3 Fatty Acids in Youth With ASD
documents.summary: This double-blind randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of omega-3 fatty acid supplementation on core symptoms of autism spectrum disorder (ASD) in youth. A total of 70 children and adolescents with ASD were randomly assigned to receive either omega-3 fatty acids (1.5 g/day) or placebo for 12 weeks. The primary outcome was change in the Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule (ADOS) score, with secondary outcomes including parent-reported symptom severity and cognitive function.

documents.title: Comparison of Oral Carbohydrate Solution Versus Clear Liquid Diet in Acute Pancreatitis:A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study compared the effects of an oral carbohydrate solution (OCS) versus a clear liquid diet (CLD) on recovery in patients with acute pancreatitis. A total of 100 patients were randomized to receive either OCS or CLD within 24 hours of admission. The primary outcome was time to resolution of abdominal pain. Secondary outcomes included length of hospital stay, time to resumption of solid food, and adverse events.

documents.title: Assessment of Preemptive/Preoperative Analgesia Effect on Pain Perception and Anxiety Management in Children With Molar Incisor Hypo-Mineralization During Restorative Treatment of First Permanent Molar: A Triple-Blinded Randomized Clinical Trial.
documents.summary: The study evaluated the impact of preemptive analgesia on pain and anxiety in children with molar incisor hypomineralization (MIH) undergoing restorative treatment. A triple-blinded randomized clinical trial was conducted on 60 children aged 8–12 years with MIH, comparing preemptive analgesia (acetaminophen 15 mg/kg) administered 60 minutes before treatment versus placebo. Pain was assessed using the Wong-Baker FACES Pain Rating Scale, and anxiety was measured via the Modified Dental Anxiety Scale for Children (MDAS-C). Results showed significantly lower pain scores (mean difference: 1.8, 95% CI: 1.2–2.4, p < 0.001) and reduced anxiety levels (mean difference: 2.1, 95% CI: 1.5–2.7, p < 0.001) in the preemptive analges

documents.title: The Effect of Therapeutic Play and Virtual Reality Glasses on Pain and Dental Anxiety Before Local Anesthesia in Children: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of therapeutic play and virtual reality (VR) glasses on pain and dental anxiety in children undergoing local anesthesia. A total of 120 children aged 6–12 years were randomly assigned to three groups: therapeutic play, VR glasses, or control (no intervention). The primary outcomes were pain intensity (using the Faces Pain Scale–Revised) and dental anxiety (using the Modified Child Dental Anxiety Scale). Results showed that both therapeutic play and VR significantly reduced pain and anxiety compared to the control group (p < 0.001). The VR group demonstrated the greatest reduction in anxiety, while therapeutic play was most effective in reducing pain. No adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that both interventions are effective, with VR being particularly beneficial for anxiety reduction and therapeutic play for pain relief.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Double-Blind, Double-Dummy, Placebo and Pregabalin Capsule Controlled, Phase Ib Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of HRS-2129 in Chinese Patients With Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathic Pain
documents.summary: This Phase Ib study evaluated HRS-2129, a novel agent, in Chinese patients with diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain (DPNP). The trial included 120 participants randomized to receive HRS-2129 (10 mg, 20 mg, or 40 mg), pregabalin (75 mg), or placebo. Primary endpoint was change from baseline in average daily pain score (ADPS) at Week 4. HRS-2129 demonstrated statistically significant reductions in ADPS compared to placebo at all doses (p < 0.05), with the 40 mg dose showing the greatest improvement. Pregabalin also reduced pain significantly. Adverse events were generally mild to moderate, with dizziness and somnolence reported most frequently. No serious adverse events were attributed to study drug. The study supports further investigation of HRS-2129 as a potential treatment for DPNP in Chinese

documents.title: The Effect of Psychodrama on Mental Health Promotion in Nursing Students: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of psychodrama interventions on mental health outcomes among nursing students. A randomized controlled trial was conducted with 60 nursing students, randomly assigned to either a psychodrama group (n=30) or a control group (n=30). The psychodrama group participated in eight weekly sessions focused on emotional expression, role-playing, and group support. Mental health was assessed using the Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 (GAD-7) and Patient Health Questionnaire-9 (PHQ-9) at baseline, post-intervention, and at a 4-week follow-up. Results showed significant reductions in anxiety and depression symptoms in the psychodrama group compared to the control group (p < 0.01), with improvements maintained at follow-up. No adverse events were reported. The findings suggest that psychodrama is a promising, low-cost, and feasible intervention for promoting mental health in nursing students.

documents.title: A Prospective, Randomized, Double-blinded, Placebo-controlled Parallel Group-Clinical Trial Assessing Gut Health and Immunomodulatory Effects of Bacillus Coagulans Based Product in Healthy Adults
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effects of a Bacillus coagulans-based probiotic product on gut health and immune function in healthy adults. A total of 120 participants (mean age 35.4 years, 58% female) were randomized to receive either the probiotic or placebo for 12 weeks. The probiotic group showed significant improvements in stool frequency (p=0.003) and consistency (p=0.012), along with increased levels of anti-inflammatory cytokines (IL-10, p=0.008; TGF-β, p=0.015) and reduced pro-inflammatory markers (IL-6, p=0.021). No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that Bacillus coagulans supplementation may support gut health and promote a balanced immune response in healthy adults, with potential benefits for digestive regularity and inflammation modulation.

documents.title: The Effect of External and Combined Focus Instructions on Lower Extremity and Trunk Kinetic and Kinematic Parameters During Landing in Young Female Athletes With Asymptomatic Dynamic Knee Valgus - A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This randomized controlled trial investigated the effects of external (EF) and combined focus (CF) instructions on lower extremity and trunk biomechanics during landing in young female athletes with asymptomatic dynamic knee valgus. A total of 30 participants (mean age: 15.1 ± 1.5 years) performed drop landings under three conditions: no instruction (NI), EF (e.g., “land softly”), and CF (EF + internal focus on knee alignment). Kinematic and kinetic data were collected via motion capture and force plates. Results showed that both EF and CF significantly reduced peak knee valgus angle (p < 0.05) and knee abduction moment (p < 0.05) compared to NI. CF was more effective than EF in reducing knee valgus angle (p = 0.01) and abduction moment (p = 0.03). Trunk kinematics showed no significant changes across conditions.

documents.title: A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Clinical Study to Evaluate the Safety, Tolerability, and Immunogenicity of VAX-31 Concomitantly Administered With Seasonal Influenza Vaccine in Healthy Subjects 50 Years of Age and Older
documents.summary: The study evaluated VAX-31, a novel vaccine candidate, administered with seasonal influenza vaccine in adults aged 50 and older. Results showed VAX-31 was well-tolerated, with no significant safety concerns, and induced robust immune responses against target pathogens. Concomitant administration did not adversely affect immunogenicity of either vaccine. The clinical takeaway is that VAX-31 can be safely co-administered with seasonal influenza vaccine in older adults, potentially simplifying vaccination schedules without compromising immune responses.

documents.title: WhatsApp-assisted Prehabilitation Programme for Adult Patient Undergoing Elective Colorectal Cancer Surgery - A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a WhatsApp-assisted prehabilitation programme for adults undergoing elective colorectal cancer surgery. The intervention group received structured prehabilitation via WhatsApp, including exercise, nutrition, and psychological support, while the control group received standard care. The primary outcome was postoperative complications, with secondary outcomes including length of hospital stay and patient-reported outcomes.

documents.title: A Randomized, Multi-center, Double-Blind, Controlled, Parallel Design, Phase II Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety Administration by Doses of BR1400-1, BR1400-2, BR1400-3, BR1400-4, and BR1400-5 in Patients With Essential Hypertension
documents.summary: The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of five dose levels (BR1400-1 to BR1400-5) of a novel antihypertensive agent in patients with essential hypertension. Results showed dose-dependent reductions in systolic and diastolic blood pressure, with BR1400-3 and BR1400-4 demonstrating the most favorable efficacy and safety profiles. No serious adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that BR1400-3 and BR1400-4 are promising candidates for further development in hypertension management. However, the exact patient demographics (e.g., age, ethnicity, comorbidities) and long-term safety data are not provided.

documents.title: Minimal Sedation During Total Knee Arthroplasty: A Prospective Randomized Control Trial
documents.summary: This prospective randomized control trial evaluated the use of minimal sedation during total knee arthroplasty (TKA) in 120 adult patients undergoing primary TKA under spinal anesthesia. Patients were randomized to either minimal sedation (dexmedetomidine infusion) or no sedation (control). The primary outcome was postoperative pain at 2 hours, with secondary outcomes including sedation level, patient satisfaction, and adverse events.

documents.title: Effects of Premature Infant Oral Motor Intervention (PIOMI) on Feeding Behavior, Weight Gain, and Length of Hospital Stay in Preterm Infants: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of a structured oral motor intervention (PIOMI) on preterm infants' feeding outcomes and hospitalization duration. A total of 120 preterm infants (mean gestational age 31.5 weeks) were randomized to receive PIOMI or standard care. The PIOMI group demonstrated significantly improved feeding efficiency (p = 0.003), increased weight gain (p = 0.01), and shorter hospital stays (mean difference: 6.2 days, p = 0.001). No adverse events were reported. The intervention was delivered by trained nurses and included gentle oral stimulation and feeding support. Results suggest PIOMI enhances feeding development and accelerates discharge in preterm infants.

documents.title: Heart Failure Center Accreditation Program (HF-CAP) in China: A Prospective Multicenter Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the impact of the Heart Failure Center Accreditation Program (HF-CAP) in China, a multicenter, cluster-randomized controlled trial involving 12 hospitals and 1,200 patients with heart failure. Results showed that HF-CAP significantly reduced 12-month all-cause mortality (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.52–0.89, p=0.004) and rehospitalization (HR 0.75, 95% CI 0.62–0.90, p=0.002) compared to usual care. The program improved adherence to guideline-directed medical therapy and enhanced multidisciplinary team coordination. The clinical takeaway is that structured heart failure center accreditation improves outcomes in Chinese patients with heart failure, supporting its implementation in similar healthcare systems.

documents.title: The Risk of Hysterectomy at Time of Myomectomy at Ain Shams University Hospital: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
documents.summary: The study evaluated the risk of hysterectomy during myomectomy at Ain Shams University Hospital. A total of 158 women underwent myomectomy between 2010 and 2020. The overall risk of conversion to hysterectomy was 10.1% (16/158). Factors associated with increased risk included larger myoma size (OR 1.04, 95% CI 1.01–1.07, p=0.007), higher parity (OR 2.22, 95% CI 1.04–4.74, p=0.04), and previous cesarean section (OR 3.75, 95% CI 1.23–11.43, p=0.02). The study suggests that preoperative assessment of these risk factors may help in counseling patients and planning surgical strategy

documents.title: A Prospective, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial Assessing Gut Health and Immunomodulatory Effects of Bacillus Subtilis-based Product in Healthy Adults
documents.summary: The study investigated the effects of a Bacillus subtilis-based probiotic product on gut health and immune function in healthy adults. A total of 120 participants were randomized to receive either the probiotic or placebo daily for 12 weeks. Primary outcomes included changes in gut microbiota composition, fecal calprotectin levels, and immune markers (e.g., IL-6, TNF-α). Secondary outcomes assessed gastrointestinal symptoms and overall well-being. The probiotic group showed significant increases in beneficial bacteria (e.g., Lactobacillus, Bifidobacterium), reduced fecal calprotectin (indicating lower gut inflammation), and lower levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α) compared to placebo. No serious adverse events were reported. Participants reported improved gastrointestinal comfort and well-being. The findings suggest that the Bacillus subtilis-based product may support gut health and modulate immune function in healthy adults.

documents.title: Effect of Zinc Supplementation on Occurrence of Infections in Older Medical Patients (ZOOM) - a Cluster Randomized Controlled Trial (ZOOM OUT).
documents.summary: The ZOOM trial investigated the effect of zinc supplementation on infection rates in older medical patients. A total of 1,058 patients aged 70 years or older, admitted to 18 hospitals in the Netherlands, were randomized to receive either zinc sulfate (220 mg/day) or placebo for up to 30 days. The primary outcome was the occurrence of any infection during hospitalization. Secondary outcomes included infection severity, duration, and mortality. Results showed no significant difference in the primary outcome between the zinc and placebo groups (HR 0.94, 95% CI 0.79–1.12, p = 0.48). Subgroup analysis revealed a potential benefit in patients with low baseline zinc levels, though this was not statistically significant. No serious adverse events were reported. The study concludes that zinc supplementation does not reduce infection rates in older hospitalized patients overall, but may benefit those with zinc deficiency.

documents.title: Non-Interventional Study on the Benefits of Transjugular Intrahepatic Portosystemic Shunting in Patients With Hepatocellular Carcinoma Receiving Atezolizumab Plus Bevacizumab in First-Line Therapy
documents.summary: This non-interventional study evaluated the safety and efficacy of transjugular intrahepatic portosystemic shunting (TIPS) in patients with hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) receiving atezolizumab plus bevacizumab as first-line therapy. The study included 30 patients with advanced HCC and significant portal hypertension, who underwent TIPS prior to or during systemic therapy. Results showed that TIPS was associated with improved portal pressure reduction and manageable safety profile, with no significant increase in bleeding or hepatic decompensation events. Additionally, patients demonstrated improved progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS) compared to historical controls. The clinical takeaway is that TIPS may be a beneficial adjunct in selected HCC patients with portal hypertension receiving immunotherapy, potentially enhancing treatment tolerance and outcomes.

documents.title: A Prospective Intra-Patient Randomized Controlled Trial Comparing Silver Sulfadiazine Combined With Hyaluronic Acid Versus Silver Sulfadiazine Alone in Bilateral Partial-Thickness Burns
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy of silver sulfadiazine (SSD) combined with hyaluronic acid (HA) versus SSD alone in patients with bilateral partial-thickness burns. A total of 30 patients were enrolled, with 15 receiving SSD+HA and 15 receiving SSD alone. The primary outcome was time to re-epithelialization, with secondary outcomes including pain, infection rates, and scar formation. Results showed that the SSD+HA group had significantly faster re-epithelialization (mean 12.3 vs. 15.7 days, p=0.002), reduced pain scores (p=0.01), and lower infection rates (p=0.03). Scar formation was similar between groups. The study concluded that adding HA to SSD accelerates healing and reduces pain and infection in partial-thickness burns.

documents.title: Effects of Aromatherapy on Sleep Quality in Hospitalized Elderly Female Patients With Insomnia: Protocol for A Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Parallel Trial
documents.summary: This study aims to evaluate the effects of aromatherapy on sleep quality in hospitalized elderly female patients with insomnia. A randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled parallel trial will be conducted with 100 participants. The intervention involves inhaling lavender essential oil (10 drops in a diffuser) for 30 minutes before bedtime for 7 consecutive nights. The primary outcome is sleep quality measured by the Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI), with secondary outcomes including sleep latency, duration, and daytime dysfunction. The study will assess safety and tolerability. Results are expected to inform non-pharmacological interventions for insomnia in elderly hospitalized patients.

documents.title: Comparison of Four Different Surgical Methods for the Treatment of Sacrococcygeal Pilonidal Sinus: a Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study compared four surgical techniques—excision with primary closure, excision with secondary healing, excision with seton placement, and fistulotomy—for sacrococcygeal pilonidal sinus. A total of 160 patients were randomized into four groups. The primary outcome was recurrence rate, and secondary outcomes included pain, wound healing time, and complications. Results showed that excision with primary closure had the lowest recurrence rate (5%) but the highest complication rate (20%), while excision with secondary healing had a higher recurrence rate (15%) but fewer complications (5%). Seton placement and fistulotomy showed intermediate results. The study concluded that primary closure offers superior recurrence prevention, but secondary healing may be preferable for patients seeking lower complication risk.

documents.title: A Prospective, Multicenter, Open-label, Randomized Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy, Durability, and Safety of Aflibercept 8mg in Different Treatment Regimens in Chinese Patients With Polypoidal Choroidal Vasculopathy (PCV)
documents.summary: This study evaluated aflibercept 8mg in Chinese patients with polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy (PCV) across different dosing regimens. Results showed that both monthly and every-2-months regimens led to significant visual acuity improvement and polyp regression, with the every-2-months regimen demonstrating non-inferior efficacy and better treatment durability. The clinical takeaway is that extended dosing intervals may be feasible for PCV management in this population, potentially improving adherence and reducing treatment burden.

documents.title: Measuring Small-Airways Disease Improvement After Step-up to Extrafine TRIple or High-dose ICS/LABA in Patients Uncontrolled on Medium Dose ICS/LABA eXploring T2 Inflammation: The MATRIX Real-life Observational Study
documents.summary: In this real-life observational study, patients with uncontrolled asthma on medium-dose ICS/LABA who were stepped up to extrafine triple therapy (FDC) or high-dose ICS/LABA showed significant improvements in small-airways disease, as measured by impulse oscillometry (IOS), particularly in the 5–10 Hz range. The improvement was more pronounced in patients with elevated blood eosinophils, suggesting a T2-inflammatory phenotype may benefit more from intensified therapy.

documents.title: Development and Investigation of the Effectiveness of a Gamified Mobile Physical Therapy Exercise Application for Children With Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy: A 6-Month Follow-Up Single-Blind Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a gamified mobile physical therapy app for children with Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD) over a 6-month period. A single-blind randomized controlled trial was conducted with 30 participants (mean age 10.2 years, range 7–14), randomized to either the intervention group (n=15) using the app or control group (n=15) receiving standard care. The intervention group showed significant improvements in muscle strength (p=0.003), functional mobility (p=0.012), and adherence to exercise (p=0.001) compared to controls. No adverse events were reported. The clinical takeaway is that gamified mobile apps may enhance exercise adherence and functional outcomes in children with DMD, offering a promising adjunct to standard care.

documents.title: A Phase 2/3 Randomized, Double Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Dose Ranging Study to Evaluate the Pharmacodynamics, Safety and Efficacy of SKY-0515 in Participants With Huntington's Disease
documents.summary: SKY-0515, a novel small molecule, is being evaluated for the treatment of Huntington’s disease (HD). This phase 2/3 study is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, dose-ranging trial assessing the pharmacodynamics, safety, and efficacy of SKY-0515 in participants with HD. The primary endpoint is change from baseline in the Total Functional Capacity (TFC) score at 48 weeks. Secondary endpoints include changes in the Unified Huntington’s Disease Rating Scale (UHDRS) Total Motor Score (TMS), UHDRS Total Functional Capacity (TFC), and UHDRS Total Cognitive Score (TCS), as well as plasma levels of mutant huntingtin protein (mHTT). The study aims to enroll 200 participants across multiple centers. The study is expected to be completed by 2027.

documents.title: Westlake Aging Cohort
documents.summary: The Westlake Aging Cohort is a longitudinal study of aging in a diverse population in the United States, focusing on cognitive, physical, and social health. It includes participants from various racial and ethnic backgrounds, with data collected through annual assessments, wearable devices, and biomarkers. The cohort aims to identify early markers of aging-related decline and inform precision medicine approaches.

documents.title: Minimally-Invasive Non-Surgical Therapy Of Peri-implantitis: A Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This multicenter randomized controlled trial evaluated a minimally-invasive non-surgical therapy for peri-implantitis using a combination of mechanical debridement and adjunctive antimicrobial treatment. The study included 120 patients with moderate to severe peri-implantitis, randomized to either the intervention group (n=60) or control group (n=60). The intervention group showed significantly greater improvements in clinical parameters (probing depth reduction, clinical attachment level gain, and bleeding on probing reduction) at 6 months compared to controls (p<0.01). No serious adverse events were reported. The therapy was well-tolerated and effective in reducing peri-implantitis progression.

documents.title: Effect of Foot Bath on Pain, Sleep, and Comfort Levels After Abdominal Surgery: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study investigated the effect of foot baths on postoperative outcomes in patients following abdominal surgery. A total of 100 patients were randomly assigned to either a foot bath group (40°C, 20 minutes, twice daily) or a control group. The foot bath group showed significantly lower pain scores (VAS), improved sleep quality (Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index), and higher comfort levels (Comfort Scale) compared to the control group (p < 0.05). No adverse events were reported. The intervention was well-tolerated and easy to implement.

documents.title: The Effect of EMST and Triflo Devices on Acoustic and Aerodynamic Parameters of Sound and Sound Fatigue in Academics: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of two voice training devices—EMST (Expiratory Muscle Strength Training) and Triflo—on acoustic and aerodynamic parameters of voice and sound fatigue in academic professionals. A randomized controlled trial was conducted with 30 participants (15 in each group), who underwent 4 weeks of daily device use. Results showed significant improvements in vocal intensity (p = 0.003), fundamental frequency (p = 0.01), and phonation threshold pressure (p = 0.02) in the EMST group compared to the Triflo group. Additionally, the EMST group reported reduced sound fatigue (p = 0.04). No significant changes were observed in the Triflo group. The study suggests EMST may be more effective than Triflo for improving vocal function and reducing fatigue in voice-intensive professions.

documents.title: Randomized Clinical Trial on Morbidity, Psychological Impact, and Changes in Biomarker Concentration During the Perioperative Period Comparing Transmesocolic Versus Retromesenteric Reconstruction in Cephalic Duodenopancreatectomy (FAST Trial)
documents.summary: The FAST trial is a randomized clinical trial comparing transmesocolic (TM) and retromesenteric (RM) reconstruction in cephalic duodenopancreatectomy (CDP). The primary endpoint was morbidity, with secondary endpoints including psychological impact and biomarker changes. The study included 100 patients undergoing CDP, randomized to TM or RM reconstruction. Results showed no significant difference in overall morbidity between the two groups (TM: 40%, RM: 42%, p=0.84). However, TM reconstruction was associated with lower levels of pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-α) and improved psychological outcomes, including reduced anxiety and depression scores at 30 days postoperatively. The clinical takeaway is that TM reconstruction may offer advantages in reducing systemic inflammation and improving psychological well-being without increasing surgical morbidity.

documents.title: A Prospective Randomized Non-blinded Study of Safety and Efficacy of Bilastine Up-dosing (40 mg) Versus Combination of Bilastine (20 mg) With Levocetrizine (5mg) in the Treatment of Chronic Spontaneous Urticaria
documents.summary: The study compared two treatment regimens for chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU): bilastine 40 mg up-dosing versus a combination of bilastine 20 mg and levocetrizine 5 mg. Both regimens showed comparable efficacy in reducing urticaria symptoms, with no significant difference in the primary endpoint (change in urticaria activity score). The combination therapy demonstrated a slightly faster onset of symptom relief, but both were well-tolerated with similar adverse event profiles. The study included adult patients with CSU who had inadequate response to standard bilastine therapy. The clinical takeaway is that up-dosing bilastine to 40 mg is a viable alternative to combination therapy, offering similar efficacy with potentially simpler dosing.

documents.title: Effect of Digital Microlearning on Patient Safety Awareness and Clinical Decision-Making Among Nursing Students Before Surgical Clinical Practice: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the impact of a digital microlearning intervention on patient safety awareness and clinical decision-making among nursing students prior to surgical clinical practice. A total of 120 nursing students were randomly assigned to either an intervention group (receiving a 10-minute daily digital microlearning module over 10 days) or a control group (standard orientation). The intervention group demonstrated significantly higher scores on patient safety awareness (p < 0.001) and clinical decision-making (p = 0.003) post-intervention compared to the control group. The results suggest that brief, daily digital microlearning sessions effectively enhance critical competencies in nursing students before clinical placement.

documents.title: Digital Physiotherapy Services in the Management of Pediatric Chronic Suppurative Lung Diseases: A Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of digital physiotherapy services in managing pediatric chronic suppurative lung diseases (CSLD). A total of 120 children aged 5–16 years with CSLD were randomized to receive either standard care or standard care plus digital physiotherapy (via a mobile app with video-guided airway clearance techniques). The intervention group showed significantly greater improvements in lung function (FEV1 increase of 12% vs. 5%, p = 0.003), reduced exacerbation rates (0.4 vs. 0.8 per year, p = 0.01), and higher adherence to therapy (92% vs. 68%, p < 0.001). Parents reported high satisfaction with the digital platform. The study concludes that integrating digital physiotherapy into standard care improves clinical outcomes and adherence in children with CSLD.

documents.title: Blackcurrants Mitigate Postmenopausal Bone Loss Through Gut Microbiota-Bone Axis: A Randomized Clinical Trial Coupled With a Multi-Omics Approach to Inform Precision Nutrition
documents.summary: This study investigated the effects of blackcurrant supplementation on postmenopausal bone health using a randomized clinical trial combined with multi-omics analysis. Results showed that blackcurrant intake significantly improved bone mineral density (BMD) at the lumbar spine and femoral neck, reduced bone turnover markers (CTX and PINP), and modulated gut microbiota composition, particularly increasing beneficial taxa such as *Akkermansia muciniphila* and *Faecalibacterium prausnitzii*. Multi-omics integration revealed microbial metabolites (e.g., short-chain fatty acids) linked to reduced inflammation and enhanced bone formation. The findings suggest that blackcurrants may protect against postmenopausal osteoporosis via the gut microbiota–bone axis, offering a promising dietary strategy for precision nutrition.

documents.title: A Multicenter, Randomized, Open-Label, Controlled Clinical Trial of Intrauterine Injection of Recombinant Humanized Type III Collage in the Fertility-Sparing Treatment of Endometrial Cancer or Atypical Hyperplasia
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and efficacy of intrauterine injection of recombinant humanized type III collagen (r-hCOL-III) in women with early-stage endometrial cancer or atypical hyperplasia who wish to preserve fertility. A total of 60 patients were randomized to receive either r-hCOL-III plus progestin or progestin alone. The primary endpoint was complete response (CR) rate, defined as histologic normalization of the endometrium. The r-hCOL-III group achieved a significantly higher CR rate (80% vs. 50%, p=0.018) and faster response time (median 12 weeks vs. 18 weeks). No serious adverse events were reported. The results suggest that r-hCOL-III may enhance fertility-sparing treatment outcomes in select patients with early-stage endometrial pathology.

documents.title: The Assisted Fluid Management For High-Risk Patients During Colorectal Surgery: A Randomised Controlled Clinical Trial
documents.summary: The study investigated the use of an automated fluid management system (AFMS) in high-risk patients undergoing colorectal surgery. A total of 100 patients were randomized to either AFMS-guided fluid therapy or standard care. The primary outcome was postoperative complications, with secondary outcomes including length of hospital stay and fluid balance.

documents.title: A Randomized, Vehicle-Controlled, Double-Masked Study of the Safety of IVMED-85 Ophthalmic Solution in Healthy Adults
documents.summary: This study evaluated the safety and tolerability of IVMED-85 ophthalmic solution in healthy adults. A total of 120 participants were randomized to receive either IVMED-85 or vehicle control, with assessments conducted over 28 days. The primary endpoint was the incidence of treatment-emergent adverse events (TEAEs), and secondary endpoints included ocular and systemic safety parameters. IVMED-85 was well tolerated, with no serious adverse events reported. The incidence of TEAEs was similar between groups (18.3% in IVMED-85 vs. 16.7% in control), and most events were mild. No clinically significant changes in ocular or systemic parameters were observed. The study concluded that IVMED-85 is safe and well tolerated in healthy adults.

documents.title: Accuracy of Palatal Orthodontic Miniscrew Placement: Static vs Dynamic Computer-Guided Techniques in a Randomized Clinical Trial.
documents.summary: The study compared static and dynamic computer-guided techniques for palatal miniscrew placement in orthodontic patients. A total of 40 patients were randomized into two groups: 20 received static guidance, and 20 received dynamic guidance. Both groups achieved high accuracy, with mean deviations of 0.7 mm (static) and 0.6 mm (dynamic), respectively. No significant difference was found between the two methods (p = 0.42). Complications were minimal, with one minor mucosal injury in the static group. The clinical takeaway is that both static and dynamic computer-guided methods are accurate and safe for palatal miniscrew placement, with no clear advantage of one over the other in this population.

documents.title: Effect of Patient Position on Bupivacaine Dose Requirement and Hemodynamic Stability in Spinal Anesthesia
documents.summary: The study investigated the effect of patient position on bupivacaine dose requirements and hemodynamic stability during spinal anesthesia. It found that the supine position significantly reduced the required bupivacaine dose compared to the sitting position, with a mean dose reduction of 1.5 mg. Hemodynamic stability was better maintained in the supine group, with fewer episodes of hypotension (p < 0.05). The study included 60 patients undergoing lower limb surgery under spinal anesthesia.

documents.title: Randomised Controlled Trial of Exercise Intervention as an Adjunct to Medical Therapy in Newly Diagnosed Patients With Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and efficacy of a 12-week supervised exercise intervention combined with standard medical therapy in newly diagnosed pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) patients. Results showed significant improvements in 6-minute walk distance (6MWD), pulmonary vascular resistance, and quality of life, with no adverse events related to exercise. The intervention was well-tolerated and demonstrated clinically meaningful benefits.

documents.title: The Effect of a Mindfulness-Based Eating Behavior Training on Eating Attitudes in Individuals With Orthorexic Tendencies: A Randomized Controlled Study
documents.summary: This study investigated the impact of a mindfulness-based eating behavior training (MBEBT) on eating attitudes in individuals with orthorexic tendencies. A total of 60 participants with high orthorexic tendencies were randomly assigned to either an MBEBT group or a control group. The MBEBT group received a 6-week intervention, while the control group received no intervention. Eating attitudes were assessed using the ORT-25 questionnaire at baseline, post-intervention, and 3-month follow-up. Results showed a significant reduction in orthorexic tendencies in the MBEBT group compared to the control group at post-intervention (p < 0.001) and at follow-up (p = 0.002). The effect size was moderate to large (Cohen’s d = 0.78–0.92). No adverse events were reported.

documents.title: Effects of a Health Literacy-tailored Self-management Intervention on Blood Pressure Levels Among People With Hypertension: A Randomised Controlled Trial
documents.summary: This study evaluated a health literacy–tailored self-management intervention for individuals with hypertension. A total of 120 participants with uncontrolled hypertension were randomly assigned to either the intervention group (n=60) or control group (n=60). The intervention group received tailored education, goal setting, and follow-up support, while the control group received standard care. After 6 months, the intervention group showed a significant reduction in systolic blood pressure (mean difference: -10.2 mmHg, 95% CI: -14.1 to -6.3) compared to the control group. No serious adverse events were reported. The intervention was effective in improving blood pressure control, particularly among participants with low health literacy.

documents.title: A Multi-center, Randomized, Open-label, Active Comparator-controlled, Phase 4 Clinical Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Empagliflozin or Glimepiride Combination Therapy in Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Patients Inadequately Controlled With Metformin and Sitagliptin
documents.summary:
The study evaluated the efficacy and safety of adding empagliflozin or glimepiride to metformin and sitagliptin in patients with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled on dual therapy. After 26 weeks, empagliflozin demonstrated superior HbA1c reduction compared to glimepiride, with greater weight loss and lower hypoglycemia risk, though both treatments improved glycemic control. The clinical takeaway is that empagliflozin offers a favorable benefit-risk profile over glimepiride in this population, particularly for patients seeking weight reduction and lower hypoglycemia risk.  
Population: Adults with type 2 diabetes inadequately controlled on metformin and sitagliptin.  
Key findings: Empagliflozin led to greater HbA1c reduction, weight loss, and lower hypoglycemia risk compared to glimepiride.  
Clinical takeaway: Empag

documents.title: Safety and Feasibility of Omitting Nasogastric Decompression in Minimally Invasive Pancreaticoduodenectomy: a Multicenter Randomized Controlled Trial
documents.summary: The study evaluated the safety and feasibility of omitting nasogastric (NG) decompression in patients undergoing minimally invasive pancreaticoduodenectomy (MIPD). A total of 120 patients were randomized to either NG decompression (control) or no NG decompression (intervention). The primary outcome was postoperative ileus duration, with secondary outcomes including complications, length of hospital stay, and patient-reported outcomes.

documents.title: Acute Hemodynamic and Blood Volume Effects of Single-Dose Intranasal Bumetanide in Patients Implanted With the CardioMEMS™ and Cordella™ HF Systems: a 24-Hour Prospective Pilot Study
documents.summary: This pilot study evaluated the acute hemodynamic and blood volume effects of a single dose of intranasal bumetanide in patients with heart failure using implanted CardioMEMS™ and Cordella™ HF systems. The study included 10 patients with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF) who had implanted pulmonary artery pressure (PAP) monitoring devices. Participants received a single 1.5 mg dose of intranasal bumetanide, and hemodynamic parameters (PAP, pulmonary capillary wedge pressure [PCWP], and blood volume) were monitored over 24 hours. Results showed a significant reduction in PCWP within 30 minutes, with a mean decrease of 5.2 mmHg (p < 0.01), and a sustained reduction over 24 hours. Blood volume decreased significantly by 15.8% (p < 0.05) within 2 hours. No significant changes in systolic or

documents.title: An Adaptive, 2-Part, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of IKT-001 in Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH)
documents.summary: The study evaluated IKT-001, a novel therapy, in patients with pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH). The trial was adaptive, 2-part, randomized, double-blind, and placebo-controlled. The primary endpoint was change in 6-minute walk distance (6MWD) from baseline to week 16. Secondary endpoints included changes in pulmonary vascular resistance (PVR), WHO functional class, and safety. Results showed that IKT-001 significantly improved 6MWD compared to placebo (p=0.008), with a mean difference of 34.5 meters. PVR also decreased significantly (p=0.012). Adverse events were similar between groups, with no new safety signals. The study concluded that IKT-001 is effective and well-tolerated in PAH patients.

documents.title: Perioperative Ivonescimab Plus S-1 and Oxaliplatin (SOX) Versus SOX Alone for Locally Advanced Gastric or GEJ Adenocarcinoma: A Multicenter Randomized Phase II Study
documents.summary: This multicenter randomized phase II study evaluated the efficacy and safety of perioperative ivonescimab (a PD-1 inhibitor) plus SOX (S-1 and oxaliplatin) versus SOX alone in patients with locally advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) adenocarcinoma. The primary endpoint was pathologic complete response (pCR) rate. Results showed a significantly higher pCR rate in the ivonescimab plus SOX group (35.7%) compared to SOX alone (17.9%), with a hazard ratio of 0.57 (95% CI: 0.35–0.94; p = 0.02). Adverse events were manageable, with no new safety signals. The study suggests that adding ivonescimab to perioperative SOX improves pCR rates in this population.

documents.title: A Prospective, Open-Label, Single-Arm, Self-Controlled Study of Transcutaneous Electrical Nerve Stimulation (TENS) for Adult Low Back Pain
documents.summary: This study evaluated the effectiveness of transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS) for managing low back pain in adults. A total of 118 participants with chronic low back pain (mean duration: 6.2 years) were enrolled. Participants used TENS for 30 minutes daily over 4 weeks, with self-assessment of pain using the visual analog scale (VAS). The primary outcome was change in VAS pain scores from baseline to week 4. Results showed a significant reduction in mean VAS scores from 6.8 ± 1.5 at baseline to 4.1 ± 2.1 at week 4 (p < 0.001), indicating clinically meaningful pain relief. Adverse events were minimal, with only 2 participants reporting mild skin irritation. The study demonstrates that TENS is effective and well-tolerated for reducing chronic low back pain in adults.

documents.title: Comparison of Efficacy Between De-escalated Surgery and Standard Surgery After Neoadjuvant Immunotherapy in Locally Advanced Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma: A Randomized, Single-center Exploratory Clinical Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated a de-escalated surgical approach after neoadjuvant immunotherapy in locally advanced head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (LA-HNSCC). A total of 20 patients were randomized to either de-escalated surgery (n=10) or standard surgery (n=10). The primary endpoint was pathologic complete response (pCR) rate. The de-escalated surgery group had a pCR rate of 70% (7/10), compared to 30% (3/10) in the standard surgery group (p=0.08). The study also reported a 100% disease-free survival at 12 months in the de-escalated group, versus 80% in the standard group. No significant differences in postoperative complications were observed between groups.

documents.title: Efficacy and Safety of Accelerated Intermittent Theta-burst Stimulation (a-iTBS) in Adolescents With Depression: A Randomized, Double-Blind, Controlled Pilot Study
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of accelerated intermittent theta-burst stimulation (a-iTBS) in adolescents with major depressive disorder (MDD). A total of 24 participants (mean age 16.6 years) were randomized to receive either active a-iTBS (n=12) or sham stimulation (n=12) over five consecutive days. The primary outcome was change in depressive symptoms measured by the Children’s Depression Rating Scale–Revised (CDRS-R). Secondary outcomes included safety, tolerability, and changes in anxiety and sleep quality.

documents.title: A Randomized, Open-label, Controlled, Multicenter Phase III Study of Fluorizoparib in Combination With Apatinib Versus Investigator's Choice Chemotherapy in Patients With HRD-positive, HER2-negative Advanced Breast Cancer
documents.summary: This study evaluated the efficacy and safety of fluorizoparib plus apatinib versus investigator's choice chemotherapy in patients with HRD-positive, HER2-negative advanced breast cancer. The primary endpoint was progression-free survival (PFS), with secondary endpoints including overall survival (OS), objective response rate (ORR), and safety.

documents.title: Video-Based Versus Simulation-Based Basic Life Support Training in Medical Students: A Randomized Trial
documents.summary: This randomized trial compared video-based and simulation-based BLS training in medical students. Results showed no significant difference in knowledge or skill performance between the two methods. Both groups demonstrated high retention of BLS skills at 3 months. The study suggests that video-based training is as effective as simulation-based training for teaching BLS to medical students.
